CAUTION: CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe. 

Hi Jason,

 

Thank you for bringing this shipping charge to my attention.  Our transportation manager requires the weights and dimensions of each package so we can rate quote and ship at the best rate.  We have not been able to use UPS for Canada and have had to redirect shippers to use our FedEx account.  If we had done this, our rate would have been $103.42 with FDX Int’l Grd.

 

Please see screenshots in the attached emails and advise if you can bill us for the rate we would have been charged of $103.42.

 

Additionally, in Kim’s screenshot, it does not appear our UPS account was used (see highlighted below).  She also mentioned there were multiple rejected shipments via UPS resulting in chargebacks.  Can you please provide more information such as a PO # or dates of those shipments?

 

Thank you,

 

Jennifer Peng

Automann USA

251 Docks Corner Rd

Monroe Township, NJ 08831

Direct: 732-992-5455,1382

Fax: 201-529-3793

purchasing5@automann.com

www.automann.com

EXTERNAL! 

 

Hey Jenn-

We had a little snafu with a UPS shipment to Brampton. It appears Walther used the wrong UPS number and the payment got rejected. Can you provide the correct Automann Brampton/Canada UPS number? I’m sure we’ll have to speak again about this but let’s start here.

 

Here is the status:

-shipment was received in Canada

-refused to pay freight because account is incorrect (UPS#  E06562)

-PO is attached

 

 

Thanks,
Jason

Hello Jason,

After a fantastic weekend with our clients at the Super Bowl, we are moving on to the next two major events where we still have a few spots available!

 

2026 Masters "Ceremonial Tee Off Opening Rounds" Program

Augusta National, Augusta, GA

*One Deluxe 4 Bedroom Private Rental Home for Three (3) Nights

(Check in Wednesday, April 8th/Check out Saturday, April 11th)

*4 Masters Badges for Thursday, April 9th 

*4 Masters Badges for Friday, April 10th

 *Full Service Staff and Blue Chip Travel Staff on site.

The great thing about going on Thursday and Friday is that your group gets to see the ceremonial tee off early Thursday Morning (Approx: 7:45am) with Jack Nicklaus, Gary Player and Tom Watson which officially begins the tournament!

 

2026 Masters "Green Jacket" Weekend Program

Augusta National, Augusta, GA

*One Deluxe 4 Bedroom Private Rental Home for Three (3) Nights

(Check in Friday, April 10th/Check out Monday, April 13th)

*4 Masters Badges for Saturday, April 11th 

*4 Masters Badges for Sunday, April 12th

 *Full Service Staff and Blue Chip Travel Staff on site.

The great thing about going on Saturday and Sunday is that your group gets

to see the golfers who made the cut and are in contention while also seeing

the Green Jacket Presentation on Sunday which is a once in a lifetime opportunity! 

 

2026 Kentucky Derby in Louisville, KY

"Run for the Roses" Premier Program

*Saturday, May 2nd

*Three (3) Deluxe Accommodations at Crowne Plaza Louisville for Three Nights which is less than 3 Miles from Churchill Downs

*Six (6) Lower 100 Level Clubhouse Tickets

(Clubhouse Tickets offer Premium Food and Beverages as well as Private Betting Windows and access to the Horseshoe Bar)

*Dinner Reservations Friday Evening at Captains Quarters-One of the most sought after places to have dinner Derby Weekend that sits right on the river

*THESE ARE OUR LAST 3 ROOMS!!!

Contact us right away for details and always remember these programs are fully customizable to your exact needs!

Thanks,

Damian

  

 

Additional Blue Chip Event Opportunities:

Los Angeles Lakers Discounted Suites

Chicago Bulls at Los Angeles Lakers

Thursday, Mar 12th

18 Person VIP Private Luxury Suite

Cleveland Cavaliers at Los Angeles Lakers 

(Possibly Last Time LeBron Plays the Cavs!)

Tuesday, Mar 31st

18 Person VIP Private Luxury Suite

Miami F1 Platinum Program!!

Saturday, May 2nd and Sunday, May 3rd

Vista VIP Platinum Club

Two-story structure that features a climate-controlled club with luxurious indoor dining, a balcony for up-close pit lane views and racing action. Includes chef buffet style spread, water, soda, beer, wine.

Access to full bar for purchase.

If you're in need of Private Luxury Suites or a Group of Tickets for any MLB, NBA, NHL, NFL, NCAA Game or Concert, please contact us right away as we work with Private Suite Owners and Season Ticket Owners all across the country.

Damian Scribner

President

Blue Chip Hospitality Group Inc

619-962-2945 (C)

800-807-6954 (O)

dscribner@bchgrp.com

If you feel you have received this email by mistake and wish to unsubscribe from our event opportunities, please reply with "opt out" and we will kindly remove you.  

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Blue Chip Hospitality Group | 6161 El Cajon Blvd Suite B | San Diego, CA 92115 US

AAMkAGFiMThhMWE4LTBiOTYtNDY2OS05NjE3LThk.body:
You don't often get email from klangley@kgi.ca. Learn why this is important 
CAUTION: CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe. 

Hello Jason, 

It was recently brought to engineering attention that the Walther hub assembly 10-00048-101, which was supplied as a direct replacement for KIC hub assembly 00133LT, does not have the same dynamic load rating.  I have attached both load tables for reference.  

For this hub to be considered a suitable replacement part, we would need to match the 25,000 lb GAW (12,500 lb/hub) rating that was previously used.  We would also prefer to have a derated load value for the wheel offsets instead of the prohibited note.  

Can you please confirm that:

The attached table is the most recent version. 
If the load rating can be updated to match what KIC had offered. 

Thanks, 

Kevin Langley
Product Design Engineer 

P. 250.499.2492   
www.kgi.ca
725 Hwy 3, Cawston, BC, V0X 1C3

AAMkAGFiMThhMWE4LTBiOTYtNDY2OS05NjE3LThk.body:
Hello Garette!

 

As Travis indicated, we do not offer lug nut covers or top hats.

 

Your inquiry below mentioned “hub cups”.  If you are asking about hubcaps, we do not offer those.  If you are asking about bearing cups for hubs, then yes, we do have those!

 

Here is a list of bearing cups and cones we can offer bulk-packed (not individually packaged, you would need to box them for distribution if selling in small quantities).  The following bearing cups and cones are produced by SKF’s General Hyatt brand:

3720

3920

25820

33462

HM212011

HM212049

HM218210

HM218248

HM518410

HM518445

JM207010

 

Regarding rotors, here is a link to the rotors we offer with competitive cross references:  https://waltheremc.com/cr-abs-brake-rotors/

 

 

Regarding disc brake calipers, we only offer the Walther Dura-Force Hydraulic disc brake.  It is a four-piston radial mount caliper and Autocar already buys it regularly under the part numbers 30-L0129-S1 and 30-R0129-S1.  These are the only brake calipers we offer since we do not remanufacture brake calipers nor do we offer an “all makes” line of brake calipers.

 

Please let me know if you have any questions or need anything else.

 

Thanks and Best Regards,

Brent

AAMkAGFiMThhMWE4LTBiOTYtNDY2OS05NjE3LThk.body:
Jason,

 

I do not know why as that was rated before my time.

 

My thoughts are it is rated at 20,000 lb because:

The 050 drum that goes with the 048 is only rated at 18,000 lbThe smaller wheel/tire and wheel bolt pattern would not be able to handle as much load as the bigger wheel/tire and bolt pattern.Actual axle load is maxed out at 17,000 lbs Therefore, no need to have the hub rated at 22,500 lbs 

I need to call Larry about something else, I can ask him about this, too.

 

 

Thank you,

_____________________________________________

Jason

AAMkAGFiMThhMWE4LTBiOTYtNDY2OS05NjE3LThk.body:

CAUTION: CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe. 

Hello Jason,

 

Join us as we unveil the next evolution of SOLIDWORKS CAD, Simulation, Electrical and PDM at this special event celebrating 30 years of design innovation. Discover what’s new in the 2026 release, packed with hundreds of user-driven enhancements, performance boosts, and cutting-edge AI-powered features designed to accelerate your product development. We’ll also share an exclusive look at the future roadmap, where SOLIDWORKS is headed and how it will continue to support your innovation for decades to come.

 

Two events are scheduled:

Sent: Tuesday, February 10, 2026 3:38 PM

Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>
Subject: Re: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

You don't often get email from klangley@kgi.ca. Learn why this is important 

Hi Jason,

 

The hubs were purchased as a direct replacement without considering the load rating.  We have some on hand now for a job and 54 more on order.  These are all currently being used in a dual wheel setup, so the offset rating is not critical at the moment.  However, we would need the load rating increased to a minimum of 23k for us to comfortably use them, with 25k rating preferred.  

 

Thanks, 

 

Kevin Langley
Product Design Engineer 

P. 250.499.2492   
www.kgi.ca
725 Hwy 3, Cawston, BC, V0X 1C3

 

 

From: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com>
Sent: Tuesday, February 10, 2026 11:15 AM
To: Kevin Langley klangley@kgi.ca>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub 

 

Apologies Kevin! I did not know KIC had higher ratings for this hub. Let me reach out to our engineering team to see why it is only rated for 20k applications. Our other N spindles are rated for 23k applications (and permit off/insets). We have a severe duty version rated for 25k and allows for offsets. Unfortunately these are 10 bolt hubs, not 8.

 

What is the status, did you end up purchasing these hubs and now you cannot use? Please advise. I will help if this is the case.

 

Regards,

Jason

Hello Jason, 

 

It was recently brought to engineering attention that the Walther hub assembly 10-00048-101, which was supplied as a direct replacement for KIC hub assembly 00133LT, does not have the same dynamic load rating.  I have attached both load tables for reference.  

 

For this hub to be considered a suitable replacement part, we would need to match the 25,000 lb GAW (12,500 lb/hub) rating that was previously used.  We would also prefer to have a derated load value for the wheel offsets instead of the prohibited note.  

 

Can you please confirm that:

 

The attached table is the most recent version. 
If the load rating can be updated to match what KIC had offered. 
 

 

Thanks, 

 

Kevin Langley
Product Design Engineer 

P. 250.499.2492   
www.kgi.ca
725 Hwy 3, Cawston, BC, V0X 1C3

AAMkAGFiMThhMWE4LTBiOTYtNDY2OS05NjE3LThk.subject: In Memory of Jordan Monde
AAMkAGFiMThhMWE4LTBiOTYtNDY2OS05NjE3LThk.body:
Good morning WEMC-

    This past weekend, Jordan received the difficult and unexpected diagnosis of Acute Leukemia and last night he passed away due to complications of his treatment. Jordan leaves behind his wife Brittney and their four children (Braylon, Mason, Carson and Savannah).
    Jordan has been part of the WEMC family for the past 14 years. He began his journey here as an assembler and, through hard work and dedication, grew into his role as a Machining Specialist within the ME Group a position in which he truly excelled. We were extremely proud of his achievements and dedication.   

We hope you'll help us support Brittney, and the children as they get through this difficult time. Below is a link to a GoFundMe account created to help with unexpected expenses. 

https://gofund.me/9c3e182f6

Tony Colon

Production Manager

Walther EMC

(937) 743-8125 ext. 612

tcolon@waltheremc.com

The West Virginia Department of Education shared this informative video during Love the Bus Month to provide a behind the scenes look at the detailed pre-trip inspection routine needed before school buses hit the road. Read More

STN EXPO East Features Exclusive North Carolina Thomas Built Buses Tour

Tour attendees will see in real-time the production of Thomas Type C and Type D school buses as well as automation on the assembly line, welding, painting and body mounting processes. Read More

  Florida School Bus Aide Accused of Child Abuse in Ongoing Beating

Authorities say video footage shows a school bus monitor hitting the boy in the head and on his back, pulling him by the hair, and wresting his backpack away, all while the school bus driver watched and laughed. Read More

  School Bus Driver Charged with Hit-and-Run Death of Brooklyn Girl

As the student starts to run across the street, surveillance vide show the school bus coming into view heading and starting a right-hand turn. The school bus never stops, and the video cuts out right before it hits the sixth grader. Read More

Safety Leadership - New Course Just Created

There is a lot of truth in the saying, there are no bad troops, only bad officers. This philosophy also applies to school bus leadership. The School Bus Safety Company (SBSC) has just completed a new course that trains all levels of management the techniques and practices to become great safety leaders. The course contains seven programs addressing every area of Safety Leadership. Watch this short video that explains the opportunity you have to operate a safely as possible.

Investigation, Documentation Crucial to School Bus Crash Investigations

As long as transportation departments are performing proper bus maintenance and conducting effective training, the Tennessee Highway Patrol advises fleet managers that the best way to prevent accidents is “first and foremost, know your driver.” Read More

Attendees will hear conversation on the proper storage of footage, archiving it for the right amount of time, how to create a documented process for allowing district employees to view the videos, and how these policies are in the best interest of district and transportation employees. Read More

Strongest Case Yet for 3-point Belts?

Since California began requiring lap/shoulder belts on new school buses in 2004, pupil passenger injuries have decreased by three-quarters in absolute numbers and by nearly 50 percent in per million miles traveled. Read More

AAMkAGFiMThhMWE4LTBiOTYtNDY2OS05NjE3LThk.body:
All –

 

With Jordan’s passing comes the inevitable grief and sense of loss.  To help with processing these thoughts and feelings, the manager of our Employee Assistance Program (EAP), Mary Jane Kocian-Figueroa, will be here tomorrow/Thursday to help guide us through.  You are encouraged to participate in a voluntary group session in the conference room scheduled as follows –

 

2/12/2026 (Thursday)

10:00 a.m.

11:00 a.m.

2:00 p.m.

10:00 p.m.

 

Due to conference room capacity, on a first come first serve basis.  If the 10:00 a.m. is full, be sure to come back for the 11:00 a.m.

 

For individual EAP assistance you have the option of calling as well –

 

Crisis Care:  937-224-4646

Appointments:  937-293-4525

 

Please let me know of any questions.

 

Take care,

Scott

 

From: Tony Colon  
Sent: Wednesday, February 11, 2026 9:12 AM
To: Vito Mays ; Anthony Ridenour ; James Dempsey ; Alex Walther ; Bob Jacobs ; Bob Klein ; Bob Montgomery ; Brent Armentrout ; Chris Crabtree ; Chris Walther ; David West ; Del Kay ; Derek Giffin ; Jake Miller ; Jason Heath ; Josh Neer ; Kevin Clawson ; Kim Alfrey ; Lalisa Lawrence ; Larry Russell ; Matt Goetz ; Mat Taylor ; Scott Winhusen ; Shipping ; Tim Brunner ; Tommy Slaton ; Travis Miller ; Jordan Monde ; Customer Service ; Dan Bays ; BG Laurent ; Matt Hatton ; Bill Kindell ; Russ King ; Matt Henderson ; Michell McCormick ; Misty Cantrell ; Nick Burns 
Subject: In Memory of Jordan Monde

 

Good morning WEMC-

 

 

    This past weekend, Jordan received the difficult and unexpected diagnosis of Acute Leukemia and last night he passed away due to complications of his treatment. Jordan leaves behind his wife Brittney and their four children (Braylon, Mason, Carson and Savannah).

    Jordan has been part of the WEMC family for the past 14 years. He began his journey here as an assembler and, through hard work and dedication, grew into his role as a Machining Specialist within the ME Group a position in which he truly excelled. We were extremely proud of his achievements and dedication.   

 

We hope you'll help us support Brittney, and the children as they get through this difficult time. Below is a link to a GoFundMe account created to help with unexpected expenses. 

 

https://gofund.me/9c3e182f6

 

Tony Colon

Production Manager

Walther EMC

(937) 743-8125 ext. 612

tcolon@waltheremc.com

CAUTION: This email was sent from outside the organization. Do not open links or attachments unless you trust the sender.

 

Hello Garette!

 

As Travis indicated, we do not offer lug nut covers or top hats.

 

Your inquiry below mentioned “hub cups”.  If you are asking about hubcaps, we do not offer those.  If you are asking about bearing cups for hubs, then yes, we do have those!

 

Here is a list of bearing cups and cones we can offer bulk-packed (not individually packaged, you would need to box them for distribution if selling in small quantities).  The following bearing cups and cones are produced by SKF’s General Hyatt brand:

3720

3920

25820

33462

HM212011

HM212049

HM218210

HM218248

HM518410

HM518445

JM207010

 

Regarding rotors, here is a link to the rotors we offer with competitive cross references:  https://waltheremc.com/cr-abs-brake-rotors/

 

 

Regarding disc brake calipers, we only offer the Walther Dura-Force Hydraulic disc brake.  It is a four-piston radial mount caliper and Autocar already buys it regularly under the part numbers 30-L0129-S1 and 30-R0129-S1.  These are the only brake calipers we offer since we do not remanufacture brake calipers nor do we offer an “all makes” line of brake calipers.

 

Please let me know if you have any questions or need anything else.

 

Thanks and Best Regards,

Brent

Travis

AAMkAGFiMThhMWE4LTBiOTYtNDY2OS05NjE3LThk.body:
Jason,

 

I talked to Larry this afternoon about the 048 hub rating.  His reasons for the 20k rating were:

The drum is only rated at 18,000 lbThe bridge law only permits 17,000 lbIt was based on a Webb design that was rated at 20,000 lb 

I also asked about the prohibited derated load rating for offset wheels.  He could not remember why the these are prohibited.

 

I also noticed in my Outlook folder the KGI has asked on several occasions about creep ratings and higher load ratings for our hubs.  I assume that they are asking for the 25K and derated load ratings for this reason.

 

At this time I am going to say that we cannot meet those requirements. 

 

 

Thank you,

_____________________________________________

Bob Klein  |  Engineering Manager

3501 Shotwell Drive | Franklin, Ohio 45005 United States

direct 937-743-8132 | main 937.743.8125 ext.615 | fax 937.743.8232

bklein@waltheremc.com | www.waltheremc.com

Sent: Tuesday, February 10, 2026 4:27 PM
To: Jason Heath 
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

Jason,

 

I will talk to Larry about this.  But any decision to up the rating will need to be followed by RFT testing to verify the new rating.  So it will be some time before this happen as we have two other hub tests to run.

 

That said, the current hub goes 300,000 cycles so I think the testing would be a formality.  

 

Thank you,

_____________________________________________

Hi Jason,

 

The hubs were purchased as a direct replacement without considering the load rating.  We have some on hand now for a job and 54 more on order.  These are all currently being used in a dual wheel setup, so the offset rating is not critical at the moment.  However, we would need the load rating increased to a minimum of 23k for us to comfortably use them, with 25k rating preferred.

Kevin Langley

Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub 

 

Apologies Kevin! I did not know KIC had higher ratings for this hub. Let me reach out to our engineering team to see why it is only rated for 20k applications. Our other N spindles are rated for 23k applications (and permit off/insets). We have a severe duty version rated for 25k and allows for offsets. Unfortunately these are 10 bolt hubs, not 8.

 

What is the status, did you end up purchasing these hubs and now you cannot use? Please advise. I will help if this is the case.

 

Regards,

Jason

Hello Jason, 

 

It was recently brought to engineering attention that the Walther hub assembly 10-00048-101, which was supplied as a direct replacement for KIC hub assembly 00133LT, does not have the same dynamic load rating.  I have attached both load tables for reference.  

 

For this hub to be considered a suitable replacement part, we would need to match the 25,000 lb GAW (12,500 lb/hub) rating that was previously used.  We would also prefer to have a derated load value for the wheel offsets instead of the prohibited note.  

 

Can you please confirm that:

 

The attached table is the most recent version. 
If the load rating can be updated to match what KIC had offered.

February 2026

From Executive Director Mike Roeth
We just released the first of five reports on Run on Less – Messy Middle. How NACFE Helped Bring Clarity to the Messy Middle provides a blueprint of the various elements that make up the Run including how and why the NACFE team decided to focus on the long-haul sector of the trucking industry and how the 13 fleets were selected.

The report also provides insight into visits to all 13 fleet sites, the various efforts to promote the Run including fleet videos, Stories from the Run and short Hot Takes videos. It also contains information on the pre-event Bootcamp educational sessions. One section of the report covers what was measured via Geotab devices for all four powertrains as well other specific matrices that were captured for each of the powertrains.

NACFE’s communications director, Denise Rondini, describes the report as a blueprint that includes all the steps involved in taking Run on Less – Messy Middle from an idea to a three-week fuel efficiency event.

This report provides transparency into everything surrounding the Run. We believe transparency about the Run is critical because it fosters confidence among people in the trucking industry about what we are learning from the data collected during the Run. Showing the behind-the-scenes effort should give the industry confidence in our findings and our data.

Next week we are hosting a data workshop in which we will take a deeper dive into the data. At TMC we will be releasing the second report from Run on Less – Messy Middle on operations, Terrain, Technology, and Telematics: The Messy Middle Operations Report. This report will analyze how these trucks performed in active revenue service, focusing on duty cycles, dwell time, and utilization.

An Emissions Footprint report will look at what was learned about emissions from the data collected during the Run. A report on TCO Ramifications will compare the Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) across diesel, CNG, BEV, and hydrogen powertrains. The Final Findings report will revisit the initial findings from the report to see if those findings are still valid in light of the more detailed dive into the dataset by NACFE staff members and from the learnings at the data workshop.

Download the Report
 Today I Learned... 

NACFE does most of its work in the field at industry events and staff members hear a lot of interesting things each month. Here are some things we learned recently. Contact us if you want to ask questions about where and how we learned what we share here.

The EPA is expected to lock in 2026 RFS biofuel quotas by early March and keep them close to the June '25 proposal (24.02B gallons total), while dropping a planned import “penalty” that would’ve reduced credits for renewable fuels/feedstocks — small policy change, potentially big impact on diesel blend costs.  

— Dean Bushey, Director of Programs

Nevoya, a participant in NACFE's recent Run on Less – Messy Middle, is securing 40 EV Class 8 semi-trucks in cooperation with the Center for Green Market Activation, Smart Freight Centre, Amazon, Meta, eBay, and Etsy for Texas high-volume freight operations on the Houston and Dallas corridor.

— Rick Mihelic, Director of Emerging Technologies

Geotab reports that the average battery degradation for an EV is 2.3% per year and largely impacted by speed of charge and the daily charge routine.  

— Ken North, Emerging Technologies Consultant

The buzzword at CES 2026 was 'Agentic AI.' We are moving beyond simple 'software-defined vehicles' to trucks that act as independent agents — capable of negotiating their own charging sessions, diagnosing their own maintenance needs before a fault code appears, and autonomously handling logistics tasks like finding specific parking spots without driver input. 

— Emilia Sibley, Emerging Technologies Consultant

Pilot Travel Centers has entered into an agreement with Tesla to install Semi Chargers to facilitate heavy-duty electric vehicle truck charging at select Pilot locations along I-5, I-10, and several major corridors. The first sites are expected to open in summer 2026.

— Mike McHorse, Director of Industry Engagement

Intermodal has been limited in the past due to longer, and more variable delivery times than trucking. Schneider implemented “Fast Track” and worked with the major rails to develop “truck-like” lanes which allows them to reach competitive truckload delivery times and costs while yielding significant emissions reduction. 

— Jeff Seger, Clean Energy Consultant

CALSTART's Zeroing in on Zero-Emission Trucks report tracks deployment of zero-emission trucks (ZETs) as of June 2025. Key findings: Most ZETs are BEVs (only 197 FCEVs of 59,000 ZETs). Half as many ZETs were deployed in the first half of 2025 vs. the first half of 2024. Normalizing ZET deployments by overall truck deployments identifies CA, WA, WI, NJ, and GA as most efficiently deploying ZETs.

— Rob Graff, Senior Technical Consultant

In December China sold a record 45,300 new electric heavy-duty trucks domestically, representing 54% of total heavy-duty truck sales for the month, and 29% total truck sales for the year 2025. Driving the overall sales trend in China – total cost of EV ownership. 

— Ken North, Emerging Technologies Consultant

A 200-car train carrying double-stack intermodal containers requires 400 trucks delivering the containers to the railhead and 400 trucks taking them away at the other end - one train like this inherently involves 800 truckloads for first- and last-mile freight handling.

— Rick Mihelic, Director of Emerging Technologies

 

Look For Us On The Town
It’s show season and NACFE staff will be out and about in the industry at a variety of conventions, meetings and exposition. These shows are a great way to connect with us, and we look forward to chatting with you to learn more about your efforts to make the trucking industry more efficient and what projects you would like to see NACFE tackle. We have a booth at many of the shows, so stop by and see us. For shows where we do not have a booth, look for us at the educational sessions and networking breaks.

Work Truck Week: North America’s largest work truck event takes place Mar. 10-11 at the Indianapolis Convention Center. This is the meeting place for the people who design, build, use and maintain commercial vocational vehicles and equipment. NACFE staff will be attending this event. Use code NACFE26 at registration for a free admission pass and online promo code for access to all three days of WTW26 exhibits, Ride & Drive and one WTW Breakout Session.

TMC 2026 Annual Meeting & Transportation Technology Exhibition: TMC returns to Nashville Mar. 16-19. NACFE staff will be on hand with the trucking industry’s top technical minds who come together to solve real-world challenges, shape new standards, and drive practical innovation in fleet operations. Visit NACFE in booth 1440.

Mid-America Trucking Show: Billed as the largest trucking show in North America, this show takes place Mar. 26-28 in Louisville, KY. Look for Mike Roeth, NACFE’s executive director, at the show.

Truck World: This premier Canadian trucking show has an enduring focus on the transition to clean transportation. Ken North, NACFE’s emerging technologies consultant, is speaking on a panel and moderating a workshop at this event which takes place Apr. 16-18 in Mississauga, ON.

ACT Expo: Now in its 16th year, ACT Expo takes place May 4–7 at the Las Vegas Convention Center. More than 12,000 industry stakeholders will gather to explore the latest in zero-emission vehicles, AI, autonomy, connected and software-defined fleets, renewable fuels, and advanced infrastructure solutions. In just four days, attendees gain months’ worth of insights — direct from peers, OEMs, technology providers, and policymakers — on what’s working in the real world to cut costs, boost performance, and accelerate decarbonization. On Tuesday, May 5, Mike Roeth, NACFE’s executive director, will lead a panel of fleet leaders and NACFE team members in a discussion during ACT’s breakfast meeting, Regional Haul & Drayage Fleet Sector – Advancing Clean Freight on the Regional Road. Use discount code 26AE-NACFE to save $100 on registration and visit us in booth 3466.

National Private Truck Council: NPTC’s Annual Conference and Exhibition is billed as the marquee national private truck fleet event of the year. There will be more than 1200 attendees and 170 exhibitors at this event which takes place May 9-11 in Orlando. Mike Roeth, NACFE’s executive director, and Rachel Ellenberger, NACFE’s industry engagement manager, will be attending this event.

 EXCEL LOOKUP FUNCTIONS: VLOOKUP, HLOOKUP, AND XLOOKUP MADE EASY 

Overview of the webinar: 

You will begin by understanding the concept of making references absolute through naming cells, which allows you to create formulas much easier. You’ll explore the VLOOKUP and HLOOKUP functions in detail, breaking down their arguments to ensure you can use them with confidence. The course will also cover the latest enhancement in Excel’s lookup capabilities, XLOOKUP, a more versatile and user-friendly alternative. Learn what makes XLOOKUP different, including its ability to search in any direction and handle errors more effectively.

Welcome back to Marketing Qualified! Here’s what we’re talking about this week:

The #1 way to make your content convert: micro-messaging 

Why your SEO isn't working in 2026: plus tactical advice you can use right now 

  🥇 The #1 way to stand out in B2B: pick a fight and prove itBig messages make pretty decks. Small, specific messages make money. Micro-messaging is the art of speaking to one tiny moment in a buyer’s day: a trigger, a task, a short promise. No manifesto, just the exact words that help someone move one step forward. 

Look at Notion’s template gallery. Each page sells a single outcome: “Sprint planning,” “Content calendar,” “Meeting notes.” Short headline, one-line promise, 15-second walkthrough. 

It’s content marketing that feels like a solution, not a story. Cloudflare does it in product updates and docs too: “Stop bot signups with Turnstile.” That’s a micro-message laser-targeted at a developer with a very specific headache. 

How to build micro-messaging that actually converts: 

Map micro-moments: role x trigger x tiny outcome. Example: “Ops lead - missed SLA - auto alerts with routing.” 

Write one promise per asset: strip your headline to the single result someone wants in 10 minutes. 

Show immediate proof: gif, snippet, template, one config step. No fluff. 

Atomize your pillars: turn long guides into 10 micro assets for 10 micro moments. Each gets its own URL and CTA. 

Distribute where moments happen: product UI hints, lifecycle emails, feature tooltips, paid search, docs, and LinkedIn carousels. 

Measure micro-conversions: click to template, doc start, copy-to-clipboard, demo step completed. If it’s not trackable in minutes, it’s not micro. 

Micro-messaging turns content into conversions because it speaks to a moment, not a persona. If your headline can’t finish the sentence “I need to…”, it’s too broad. Win the micro-moment, win the pipeline. 

  🗞️  In the news this week.👀 LinkedIn rolls out Topic Boosts for Sponsored Content: deeper intent targeting for B2B campaigns 

🤳 TikTok adds enterprise lead-gen forms and native CRM hookups: social teams eye lower-funnel attribution 

💜 B2B brands reallocate 2026 spend to product-led content and community over display ads 

📊 Long-form email newsletters outperform nurtures on pipeline velocity in 2026 

🎨 Meta expands AI creative tools in Ads Manager: auto-variant testing promises faster B2B ad iteration 

  🔍 Why your SEO isn't working in 2026If your traffic is flat or your rankings wobble after every core update, it's not bad luck. It's sloppy intent and content sprawl. Google's recent updates reward crystal clear intent, consolidation, and answers that deserve to be cited.

Common mistakes to kill now: 

Treating keywords as the strategy: map queries to intent types first. 

Publishing 20 near-duplicates competing with yourself. 

Thin AI rewrites with zero original insight or proof. 

Weak internal linking that hides your money pages. 

 Ignoring AI Overviews: no direct answers, no FAQs, no citations. 

Before: Monday.com had scattered posts for “project management templates” across blog and help content that overlapped.
After: A single templates hub with filters by use case, internal links to category pages, schema for templates, and comparison modules. Result: cleaner crawl paths, fewer cannibalized queries, clearer intent alignment. 

Before: Zapier tried to rank a generic blog on “connect Slack to Salesforce.”
After: A dedicated integration landing page with step-by-step instructions, screenshots, FAQs, and structured data for how-to content. Bottom-funnel queries land on the exact solution users want. 

How to fix in 7 tactical steps: 

Build an intent map: informational, comparison, integration, template, pricing. Assign each query to one page type. 

Run a cannibalization audit in Search Console: group pages targeting the same query, merge the strongest, 301 the rest. 

Create answer boxes: 2-3 sentences that directly answer the query near the top, plus an FAQ section. 

Add evidence: screenshots, workflows, data points, or short customer quotes. 

Rewire internal links: pillar page links down to clusters with descriptive anchors, clusters link back up. 

Tighten titles and H1s: lead with intent, not fluff. 

Add schema: FAQ, HowTo, Product where relevant to aid AI Overviews and rich results. 

If a page doesn’t have a singular intent and a unique reason to exist, it’s baggage. Make every URL earn its keep. 

Memorable takeaway: Prune ruthlessly, answer directly, and let intent be your North Star.

From: Jason Heath

To: 'Kevin Langley' 
Cc: KGI Engineering ; Bob Klein ; purchasing@kgi.ca
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

Hi Kevin, cc’ing Bob Klein (Walther Engineering Manager) and Brian Farquhar.

 

Today we spoke about your issue in our huddle meeting with the Engineering team. The hub itself appears to be strong as it passes our internal testing with high cycle counts. It is possible/likely they will pass testing at 23k loads but Engineering will need to test to verify before it can increase the rating.

 

I apologize again for the inconvenience. I know you need at least 23k rating for this build and I won’t be able to help you in time. Do you have other options? I might be able to direct you to another source.

 

Do you have 20k applications where you can use up the current inventory or will you need to return?

 

Thank you,

Jason

3501 Shotwell Drive | Franklin, Ohio 45005 United States

cell 616.914.2665 | fax 937.743.8232

Subject: Re: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

You don't often get email from klangley@kgi.ca. Learn why this is important 

Hi Jason,

 

The hubs were purchased as a direct replacement without considering the load rating.  We have some on hand now for a job and 54 more on order.  These are all currently being used in a dual wheel setup, so the offset rating is not critical at the moment.  However, we would need the load rating increased to a minimum of 23k for us to comfortably use them, with 25k rating preferred.  

 

Thanks, 

 

Kevin Langley
Product Design Engineer 

P. 250.499.2492   
www.kgi.ca
725 Hwy 3, Cawston, BC, V0X 1C3

 

 

From: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com>
Sent: Tuesday, February 10, 2026 11:15 AM
To: Kevin Langley klangley@kgi.ca>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub 

 

Apologies Kevin! I did not know KIC had higher ratings for this hub. Let me reach out to our engineering team to see why it is only rated for 20k applications. Our other N spindles are rated for 23k applications (and permit off/insets). We have a severe duty version rated for 25k and allows for offsets. Unfortunately these are 10 bolt hubs, not 8.

 

What is the status, did you end up purchasing these hubs and now you cannot use? Please advise. I will help if this is the case.

 

Regards,

Jason

Hello Jason, 

 

It was recently brought to engineering attention that the Walther hub assembly 10-00048-101, which was supplied as a direct replacement for KIC hub assembly 00133LT, does not have the same dynamic load rating.  I have attached both load tables for reference.  

 

For this hub to be considered a suitable replacement part, we would need to match the 25,000 lb GAW (12,500 lb/hub) rating that was previously used.  We would also prefer to have a derated load value for the wheel offsets instead of the prohibited note.  

 

Can you please confirm that:

 

The attached table is the most recent version. 
If the load rating can be updated to match what KIC had offered. 
 

 

Thanks, 

 

Kevin Langley
Product Design Engineer

Hi Kevin, cc’ing Bob Klein (Walther Engineering Manager) and Brian Farquhar.

 

Today we spoke about your issue in our huddle meeting with the Engineering team. The hub itself appears to be strong as it passes our internal testing with high cycle counts. It is possible/likely they will pass testing at 23k loads but Engineering will need to test to verify before it can increase the rating.

 

I apologize again for the inconvenience. I know you need at least 23k rating for this build and I won’t be able to help you in time. Do you have other options? I might be able to direct you to another source.

 

Do you have 20k applications where you can use up the current inventory or will you need to return?

Jason

Regards,

Jason

Hello Jason, 

 

It was recently brought to engineering attention that the Walther hub assembly 10-00048-101, which was supplied as a direct replacement for KIC hub assembly 00133LT, does not have the same dynamic load rating.  I have attached both load tables for reference.  

 

For this hub to be considered a suitable replacement part, we would need to match the 25,000 lb GAW (12,500 lb/hub) rating that was previously used.  We would also prefer to have a derated load value for the wheel offsets instead of the prohibited note.  

 

Can you please confirm that:

 

The attached table is the most recent version. 
If the load rating can be updated to match what KIC had offered. 
 

 

Thanks, 

 

Kevin Langley
Product Design Engineer

AAMkAGFiMThhMWE4LTBiOTYtNDY2OS05NjE3LThk.subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub
AAMkAGFiMThhMWE4LTBiOTYtNDY2OS05NjE3LThk.body:
The 161 hubs start testing probably early week after next.  Typically, they run for 300,000 cycles which takes about two days to run.  Setup and teardown are each a half day.  So, about 3 days per part.  If they go 300,000 cycles, I need to run 4 parts – 12 days total if no issues.

 

We will not hit KGI’s timeframe.

 

We also have the issue of the derated hub load values for offset wheels.  Larry stated that the offset wheel prohibit was determined by Ken Kelly who designed the hub.  He is not sure of the reasons why – potentially that the payload on the trailer could have a higher center of gravity which would increase the bending load on the hub more than what is calculated for validation or typically on-highway.  I did a model comparison of the 048 and the 042 hubs – the 042 has some additional meet to it compared to the 048.  

 

Thank you,

_____________________________________________

Your All-Access Pass is Waiting!
 

Mid-America Trucking Show | March 26 - 28, 2026

MATS 2026 
More Than a Tradeshow

Beyond the 1,000,000+ square feet of exhibits, MATS 2026 hosts world-class education, led by industry-leading experts and a full schedule of networking and entertainment events —all free to registered attendees.

Learn the latest regulatory updates directly from FMCSA leadership, discover best practices to boost your profits, and explore new equipment and technology that is shaping the future of freight. After hours, recharge with live entertainment and networking events, and connect with the industry peers and friends. 

FREE online registration ends February 27, 2026 

Scroll Down for Show Updates.

Register For Free

SHOW UPDATES: 2/12/2026
Presented by:  FMCSA & TRUCKSTOP  

Education
Learn From The Experts

FEATURED SPEAKERS

Dean Croke 

Principal Analyst, 
DAT

Derek Barrs

Administrator, FMCSA

Lee Klaskow 

Senior Analyst - Transportation and Logistics, Bloomberg Intelligence

Adam Wingfield

Founder & Managing Director, Innovative Logistics Group

Learn from the industry’s best minds about key industry trends and changes to stay informed and keep your business thriving. MATS 2026 will host 40+ free Pro Talks Education Sessions, Opening Breakfast , and much more.

EDUCATION PROGRAMS

MATS Opening Breakfast

Kick off your MATS experience with an opening breakfast designed for owner-operators and small fleets looking to build resilient businesses in a difficult market.

Learn More>

Pro Talks Seminars

Over 40 educational seminars covering the latest industry topics, regulatory updates, emerging technology, and solutions to keep you informed and your business thriving. 

Learn More>

2026 SPONSORS

Networking & Events 
Have Fun & Make Connections

Enjoy a long list of special events and meet in person with industry friends and peers 
so you can expand your network and thrive in 2026.

 

FEATURED EVENTS

RIDE & DRIVES

Experience the performance of the 2027 X15 from Cummins paired with a Peterbilt 579 or Kenworth T680. With up to 605hp and 2,050 lb-ft of torque, the X15 has the power to rise above. Participation is free for all registered attendees. 

Learn More>

Truck Parking Community

Recharge, relax, and have fun with free food, fun games, and more, all brought to you by DAT Freight & Analytics. As a thank you to hardworking drivers and carriers, DAT will provide free morning coffee, plus tasty options from food trucks later in the day. 

Learn More>

MATS Concert 

Come and see this year’s free Friday MATS Concert, featuring Frank Foster, Mark Ware, and Long Haul Paul, sponsored by Bennett Family of Trucks. Witness a high-energy mix of southern rock and country that will have you up and on your feet. 

Learn More>

MAC Championship Pulling

Feel the roar of competition with the MAC Trailer Winter Nationals Championship Truck Pull, featuring classes from hot rod semis to super stock diesels. See a thunderous showdown of high-horsepower engines that will shake Freedom Hall! 

Learn More>

PKY Truck Beauty Championship

The 35th annual PKY event kicks off the show truck season with the most elite custom show trucks in North America. Special features include the light show, parade, and Big Rig Build-Off. 

Learn More>

Opening Reception

Join us at the opening reception for a lively evening of celebration, networking, and fun. The reception includes live music, free food, free drinks, fun games, and the induction of the Wall of Fame nominees. 

Learn More>

Share on Social #MATS2026Let friends and other industry peers know about MATS by sharing updates with the event hashtag, #MATS2026!

Subject: RE: Follow-up on Pricing and direction of our Collaboration
Importance: High

 

Dear Jason,

 

Thank you for your response and for the openness shown regarding this opportunity.

 

After reviewing your proposal, we would like to clarify a few points to ensure strategic alignment with the objective of moving toward a direct partnership.

 

As you know, 17-00116-S1 and 17-00206-S1 represent the highest-volume SKUs. Continuing to source these products through NWF in Canada will inevitably increase our dependency with them, making a future transition to direct purchasing from you more complex over time. Our intent, however, is precisely to move toward a direct relationship.

In addition, excluding 17-00116-S1 and 17-00206-S1 from direct pricing significantly limits our competitiveness in the Canadian market and slows the potential growth of the program. Direct sourcing of these key references is a critical lever to accelerate volumes, improve price positioning, and support sustainable growth — ultimately benefiting both parties.

 

While the opening of Syracuse is an important milestone, we do not intend to pause our sales development until then. To properly structure our commercial strategy and market positioning, we would need pricing for 17-00116-S1 and 17-00206-S1 at this stage.

 

Given that WEMC views this as a strategic partnership and acknowledges that this type of collaboration is not typical, we believe an approach that includes all key references from the outset would be the most coherent path forward.

 

We would welcome the opportunity to discuss this further and to better understand how WEMC envisions a practical transition from NWF to a direct supply model.

 

We look forward to your feedback.

 

Best regards,

Hello Jason 

 

I hope you are doing well.

Following up on our discussion from last week, I wanted to check in regarding the pricing you mentioned you were working on for the rotors. 

This initiative is part of a long-term, volume-driven approach on our side, and visibility on your pricing proposal will help us align our internal analysis and commitments.

 

In parallel, we would like to understand the direction you see for the evolution of our collaboration. As you know, we are currently purchasing your products through NWF; however, our objective is to transition toward a direct partnership, which is the basis for our pricing request.

 

We see strong long-term potential in this relationship and look forward to continuing the discussion.

 

Thank you in advance for your update, and I look forward to hearing from you.

Objet : RE: EXTERNAL - RE: Follow up

 

Hey dude- yes, that is what we needed! Thank you 👍

 

I need to work up your pricing. I’m going to jump on that now.

 

Regarding resurfacing- it is not recommended by Walther but I don’t think many manufacturers ever recommend it. We don’t have much warranty on these rotors because they perform well. But on the trailer rotors where we do have some tone ring issues, we will warrant the tone rings as long as the rotor is not worn out.

 

Resurfacing will reduce the material which will reduce the thickness and increase heat as well. We have seen people use descalers. We generally don’t recommend using them either but some fleets want to when they are looking to sell a bus and they want the rotors to look nicer. We can’t stop people from doing either but these are our recommendations.

The 161 hubs start testing probably early week after next.  Typically, they run for 300,000 cycles which takes about two days to run.  Setup and teardown are each a half day.  So, about 3 days per part.  If they go 300,000 cycles, I need to run 4 parts – 12 days total if no issues.

 

We will not hit KGI’s timeframe.

 

We also have the issue of the derated hub load values for offset wheels.  Larry stated that the offset wheel prohibit was determined by Ken Kelly who designed the hub.  He is not sure of the reasons why – potentially that the payload on the trailer could have a higher center of gravity which would increase the bending load on the hub more than what is calculated for validation or typically on-highway.  I did a model comparison of the 048 and the 042 hubs – the 042 has some additional meet to it compared to the 048.  

 

Thank you,

Wabash closing plants (I’m thinking this was in the news).

Hyundai opening plants in Illinois (going from low-cost Mexico to high-cost Illinois??????!!!!!!!)

 

 

 

From: Jason Heath  
Sent: Friday, February 13, 2026 8:14 AM
To: Brent Armentrout ; Bob Montgomery ; Keith Fally 
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - Market Increase Supplier Letter

 

Wabash forecast is pretty flat (except for truck bodies). Tanks and flats show a modest increase starting in June.

 

From: Brent Armentrout BArmentrout@waltheremc.com> 
Sent: Thursday, February 12, 2026 10:55 AM
To: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com>; Bob Montgomery bmontgomery@waltheremc.com>; Keith Fally kfally@waltheremc.com>
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - Market Increase Supplier Letter

 

Travis and I had our regular monthly forecasting call with Hendrickson on Monday.  Attached is the spreadsheet they update each month for us.

 

Hendrickson is not showing significant growth into Q3/Q4.  Since most of our Hendrickson business is vocational in nature, they’re not as likely to have the spikes which dry vans may see in the 2nd half of the year.

 

Brent

 

From: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com> 
Sent: Thursday, February 12, 2026 10:43 AM
To: Bob Montgomery bmontgomery@waltheremc.com>; Keith Fally kfally@waltheremc.com>; Brent Armentrout BArmentrout@waltheremc.com>
Subject: FW: EXTERNAL - Market Increase Supplier Letter

 

FYI- Meritor forecasting bump in Q3/Q4 citing Truck and Trailer sales teams hearing good things.

 

I’m gonna check in with my key customers, you folks might want to as well. Brent, have you heard anything from Hendrickson?

Sent: Thursday, February 12, 2026 10:22 AM

Cc: Travis Miller tmiller@waltheremc.com>
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - Market Increase Supplier Letter

 

Hi Jason, 

 

General feedback from our sales teams both Truck and Trailer is that sales are expected to pick up starting Q3, however, I may not translate it as a 1:1 for WEMC given the ADB and Duralight products are more fleet driven vs market driven. 

 

WEMC deals direct with OEMs, how does our statement compare vs what you are hearing from them? 

 

Ramses Ortiz Ferretiz

The offer is a discount off the Best Available Rate (BAR) for selected brands:

*15% off the Best Available Rate (BAR)(the “Promotion”) for advance purchase bookings of stays of 2 nights at participating US locations for the following Choice® brands: Ascend Collection, Cambria, Clarion, Clarion Pointe, Comfort, Comfort Suites, Country Inn & Suites by Radisson, Park Inn by Radisson, Quality Inn, Radisson, Radisson Blu, Radisson RED, Sleep Inn, Everhome, Mainstay, Suburban. *20% off the Best Available Rate (BAR)(the “Promotion”) for advance purchase bookings of stays of 3 or more nights at participating US locations for the following Choice® brands: Ascend Collection, Cambria, Clarion, Clarion Pointe, Comfort, Comfort Suites, Country Inn & Suites by Radisson, Park Inn by Radisson, Quality Inn, Radisson, Radisson Blu, Radisson RED, Sleep Inn, Everhome, Mainstay, Suburban. The discount to BAR will be applied immediately upon booking. Unless otherwise stated, quoted rates are per room per night, based on single/double occupancy and do not include taxes, gratuities, or incidental charges.

AAMkAGFiMThhMWE4LTBiOTYtNDY2OS05NjE3LThk.subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub
AAMkAGFiMThhMWE4LTBiOTYtNDY2OS05NjE3LThk.body:
Jason,

 

I understand the situation.  But we need to get the 161s on test as soon as we can.  They should have started testing a few weeks ago, but the parts were lost at PPG for paint.

 

One part Is not enough to validate the hub at a higher axle load.  We typically run 6 parts and do a Weibull analysis.  If four parts make it to 300,000 cycles, then the Weibull analysis is waived.

 

How are we good on the drum?

 

Chris is in the office now, so I can talk to him if needed.

 

Thank you,

_____________________________________________

Bob Klein  |  Engineering Manager

3501 Shotwell Drive | Franklin, Ohio 45005 United States

direct 937-743-8132 | main 937.743.8125 ext.615 | fax 937.743.8232

bklein@waltheremc.com | www.waltheremc.com

 

From: Jason Heath  
Sent: Friday, February 13, 2026 11:25 AM
To: Bob Klein 
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

Bob,

We have a customer who is looking at pushing out 4 builds because they don’t have parts. I’m not sure if you realize but that’s a big issue. They will not be able to source new parts in time.

 

If we are able to run one hub to see if it would pass at 23k, would that be enough confidence to allow them to build these? They will run dual wheels (0 offset).

 

Please be aware. If you refuse that’s fine. I will notify Chris because I think this is important enough for him to weigh in.

 

Don’t worry about the drum. We are good there. If you have questions give me a call.

 

Thanks,
Jason

 

From: Bob Klein BKlein@waltheremc.com> 
Sent: Friday, February 13, 2026 9:56 AM
To: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com>
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

Jason,

 

The setups for the N and P hubs are different on the RFT.  I am not going to set up for the 048 hub and run one hub and maybe a portion of a second hub and then switch over to the 161.

 

Are we also selling the 050 drum with the 048 hubs?  What drum rating does KGI need to go with the higher hub rating?  The 050 drum is only rated at 18,000 lb.  

 

Thank you,

_____________________________________________

Bob Klein  |  Engineering Manager

3501 Shotwell Drive | Franklin, Ohio 45005 United States

direct 937-743-8132 | main 937.743.8125 ext.615 | fax 937.743.8232

bklein@waltheremc.com | www.waltheremc.com

 

From: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com> 
Sent: Thursday, February 12, 2026 5:03 PM
To: Bob Klein BKlein@waltheremc.com>
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

Why not run the 048s now? How many can you get done before you have to start 161s?

 

From: Bob Klein BKlein@waltheremc.com> 
Sent: Thursday, February 12, 2026 4:54 PM
To: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com>
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

Nothing at the moment.  We have been waiting for the 161s.  

 

Thank you,

_____________________________________________

Bob Klein  |  Engineering Manager

3501 Shotwell Drive | Franklin, Ohio 45005 United States

direct 937-743-8132 | main 937.743.8125 ext.615 | fax 937.743.8232

bklein@waltheremc.com | www.waltheremc.com

 

From: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com> 
Sent: Thursday, February 12, 2026 4:41 PM
To: Bob Klein BKlein@waltheremc.com>
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

Ok. What’s running in RFT right now?

 

From: Bob Klein BKlein@waltheremc.com> 
Sent: Thursday, February 12, 2026 4:17 PM
To: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com>
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

The 161 hubs start testing probably early week after next.  Typically, they run for 300,000 cycles which takes about two days to run.  Setup and teardown are each a half day.  So, about 3 days per part.  If they go 300,000 cycles, I need to run 4 parts – 12 days total if no issues.

 

We will not hit KGI’s timeframe.

 

We also have the issue of the derated hub load values for offset wheels.  Larry stated that the offset wheel prohibit was determined by Ken Kelly who designed the hub.  He is not sure of the reasons why – potentially that the payload on the trailer could have a higher center of gravity which would increase the bending load on the hub more than what is calculated for validation or typically on-highway.  I did a model comparison of the 048 and the 042 hubs – the 042 has some additional meet to it compared to the 048.  

 

Thank you,

_____________________________________________

Bob Klein  |  Engineering Manager

3501 Shotwell Drive | Franklin, Ohio 45005 United States

direct 937-743-8132 | main 937.743.8125 ext.615 | fax 937.743.8232

bklein@waltheremc.com | www.waltheremc.com

 

From: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com> 
Sent: Thursday, February 12, 2026 3:54 PM
To: Bob Klein BKlein@waltheremc.com>
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

Ok. I will check with Chris. How long do you expect the 161 testing will take? Is it fair to estimate the RFT could be available mid next week for the next test?

 

From: Bob Klein BKlein@waltheremc.com> 
Sent: Thursday, February 12, 2026 2:56 PM
To: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com>
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

I have 161 hubs that need to go on test here shortly to validate a new pattern. 

 

Then I also have 114s that need to be tested, but these could be bumped.  Chris would need to be in on that decision since the volume is only 200-300 a year. 

 

Thank you,

_____________________________________________

Bob Klein  |  Engineering Manager

3501 Shotwell Drive | Franklin, Ohio 45005 United States

direct 937-743-8132 | main 937.743.8125 ext.615 | fax 937.743.8232

bklein@waltheremc.com | www.waltheremc.com

 

From: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com> 
Sent: Thursday, February 12, 2026 2:32 PM
To: Bob Klein BKlein@waltheremc.com>
Subject: Fwd: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

Any chance you can squeeze this in on the RFT at 23k?

Begin forwarded message:

From: Kevin Langley klangley@kgi.ca>
Date: February 12, 2026 at 12:56:05 PM EST
To: Jason Heath jheath@waltheremc.com>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>, Bob Klein bklein@waltheremc.com>, Brian Farquhar purchasing@kgi.ca>, Collin Shore cshore@kgi.ca>
Subject: Re: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

﻿ 

Hi Jason,

 

We use about 200-300 units per year.  If we cannot get the updated load rating, then there are 4 builds that would potentially go on hold within the next 2-3 weeks.  
What is your estimated timeline for the engineering analysis to be complete?

 

Thanks, 

 

Kevin Langley
Product Design Engineer 

P. 250.499.2492   
www.kgi.ca
725 Hwy 3, Cawston, BC, V0X 1C3

 

 

 

From: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com>
Sent: Thursday, February 12, 2026 5:55 AM
To: Kevin Langley klangley@kgi.ca>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>; Bob Klein BKlein@waltheremc.com>; Brian Farquhar purchasing@kgi.ca>
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub 

 

Kevin, what’s your rough usage annually on this hub?

 

From: Jason Heath 
Sent: Thursday, February 12, 2026 8:29 AM
To: 'Kevin Langley' klangley@kgi.ca>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>; Bob Klein BKlein@waltheremc.com>; purchasing@kgi.ca
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

Hi Kevin, cc’ing Bob Klein (Walther Engineering Manager) and Brian Farquhar.

 

Today we spoke about your issue in our huddle meeting with the Engineering team. The hub itself appears to be strong as it passes our internal testing with high cycle counts. It is possible/likely they will pass testing at 23k loads but Engineering will need to test to verify before it can increase the rating.

 

I apologize again for the inconvenience. I know you need at least 23k rating for this build and I won’t be able to help you in time. Do you have other options? I might be able to direct you to another source.

 

Do you have 20k applications where you can use up the current inventory or will you need to return?

 

Thank you,

Jason

 

____________________________________________

Jason Heath  |  Director, OEM & Fleet Sales

3501 Shotwell Drive | Franklin, Ohio 45005 United States

cell 616.914.2665 | fax 937.743.8232

jheath@waltheremc.com | www.waltheremc.com

 

 

 

From: Kevin Langley klangley@kgi.ca> 
Sent: Tuesday, February 10, 2026 3:38 PM
To: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>
Subject: Re: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

You don't often get email from klangley@kgi.ca. Learn why this is important 

Hi Jason,

 

The hubs were purchased as a direct replacement without considering the load rating.  We have some on hand now for a job and 54 more on order.  These are all currently being used in a dual wheel setup, so the offset rating is not critical at the moment.  However, we would need the load rating increased to a minimum of 23k for us to comfortably use them, with 25k rating preferred.  

 

Thanks, 

 

Kevin Langley
Product Design Engineer 

P. 250.499.2492   
www.kgi.ca
725 Hwy 3, Cawston, BC, V0X 1C3

 

 

From: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com>
Sent: Tuesday, February 10, 2026 11:15 AM
To: Kevin Langley klangley@kgi.ca>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub 

 

Apologies Kevin! I did not know KIC had higher ratings for this hub. Let me reach out to our engineering team to see why it is only rated for 20k applications. Our other N spindles are rated for 23k applications (and permit off/insets). We have a severe duty version rated for 25k and allows for offsets. Unfortunately these are 10 bolt hubs, not 8.

 

What is the status, did you end up purchasing these hubs and now you cannot use? Please advise. I will help if this is the case.

 

Regards,

Jason

 

From: Kevin Langley klangley@kgi.ca> 
Sent: Tuesday, February 10, 2026 1:59 PM
To: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>
Subject: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

You don't often get email from klangley@kgi.ca. Learn why this is important 

 

CAUTION: CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe. 

Hello Jason, 

 

It was recently brought to engineering attention that the Walther hub assembly 10-00048-101, which was supplied as a direct replacement for KIC hub assembly 00133LT, does not have the same dynamic load rating.  I have attached both load tables for reference.  

 

For this hub to be considered a suitable replacement part, we would need to match the 25,000 lb GAW (12,500 lb/hub) rating that was previously used.  We would also prefer to have a derated load value for the wheel offsets instead of the prohibited note.  

 

Can you please confirm that:

 

The attached table is the most recent version. 
If the load rating can be updated to match what KIC had offered. 
 

 

Thanks, 

 

Kevin Langley
Product Design Engineer 

P. 250.499.2492   
www.kgi.ca
725 Hwy 3, Cawston, BC, V0X 1C3

The 161 hubs start testing probably early week after next.  Typically, they run for 300,000 cycles which takes about two days to run.  Setup and teardown are each a half day.  So, about 3 days per part.  If they go 300,000 cycles, I need to run 4 parts – 12 days total if no issues.

 

We will not hit KGI’s timeframe.

 

We also have the issue of the derated hub load values for offset wheels.  Larry stated that the offset wheel prohibit was determined by Ken Kelly who designed the hub.  He is not sure of the reasons why – potentially that the payload on the trailer could have a higher center of gravity which would increase the bending load on the hub more than what is calculated for validation or typically on-highway.  I did a model comparison of the 048 and the 042 hubs – the 042 has some additional meet to it compared to the 048.

Hi guys, thanks for looking at this.

This is a concern, as our brakes are rated for 23K and the axles are rated for 25K structurally.

For reference, we did a comparison with your hub and the older 25K KIC rated one.

Your hub was thicker throughout the cross-section, the flange, and had additional stiffeners. Plus you have the higher grade proprietary material.

I understand you have to do your due diligence, I just wanted to provide a reference to the previous competition.

As an aside, we should look into the options for returning the hub and drum + hub assemblies.

Let us know, thanks again.

Kevin, Bob walked me thru the technical challenges. The hub was originally designed for 20k. Material was removed to reduce weight. In order to be sure we can uprate to 23k, we will have to thorough testing and we have tests in front of this that need to be done first.

 

I had hoped the hub was overdesigned and we could uprate without a lot of testing but that is not the case.

 

Regards,

Jason

The 161 hubs start testing probably early week after next.  Typically, they run for 300,000 cycles which takes about two days to run.  Setup and teardown are each a half day.  So, about 3 days per part.  If they go 300,000 cycles, I need to run 4 parts – 12 days total if no issues.

We also have the issue of the derated hub load values for offset wheels.  Larry stated that the offset wheel prohibit was determined by Ken Kelly who designed the hub.  He is not sure of the reasons why – potentially that the payload on the trailer could have a higher center of gravity which would increase the bending load on the hub more than what is calculated for validation or typically on-highway.  I did a model comparison of the 048 and the 042 hubs – the 042 has some additional meet to it compared to the 048.

AAMkAGFiMThhMWE4LTBiOTYtNDY2OS05NjE3LThk.subject: EXTERNAL - Stemco - Walther QuickSet Hub
AAMkAGFiMThhMWE4LTBiOTYtNDY2OS05NjE3LThk.body:
CAUTION: CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe. 

Hello Maricar, Yolanda and Kevin,

 

I don’t have everyone’s email so please forward this to Chad and Perry when you’re able.

 

I’d like to thank you all again for taking the time to meet with me this week. It was great to have an opportunity to demo the Walther QuickSet and discuss some of the benefits it offers.

 

As mentioned, the demo was using Walther’s Dura-Light Hub which is an iron hub, but lighter than aluminum. All the components inside the hub are Stemco components. They are:

 

Seal: DiscoverXR (premium 1-pc seal)

Bearings: Stemco Platinum bearings – standard SETS

Axle Nut: Auto-Torq – self-locks, preloads bearings, reusable

 

**Hubcap: the QuickSet does not come with a hubcap, but Stemco offers a 7-Year Parts & Labour Warranty for OEMs who use the Sentinel hubcap in conjunction with the Walther QuickSet. Stemco PSI and Hendrickson Tire Max Pro tire inflation systems are also approved.

 

I’ve attached documents on each of the components, as well as the QuickSet and Dura-Light hub.

 

The PPS documents attached contains our warranty packages for both OEM and Aftermarket.

 

I also have a video of the QuickSet being installed but it’s a 6 min video that I will need to share with someone’s iPhone for best resolution. Please let me know whose iPhone I can send it to and I’ll get that underway.

 

I’ve looped in Jason Heath, the Walther Sales Rep as well. Jason can discuss pricing and logistics with you when you’re ready to have that conversation.

 

Please let me know if you have any questions in the meantime.

 

Thanks Very Much,

 

 

   

Brad Major
Regional Sales Manager - MB, SK & Southern BC

E: Brad.Major@stemco.com
M: 204.430.2868
1020 Lorimar Dr., Mississauga,Ontario L5S 1R8
www.stemco.com 

----------Legal Disclaimer---------- 
The information contained in this message may be privileged and confidential, and is intended solely for the use of the named addressee. No other person is authorized to access, copy or re-use this message (or any information contained herein). If you are not the intended recipient, please notify us immediately by replying to this message and delete it from your computer.

Hi Bob,

I assumed that your validation requirements would be more stringent. If it helps, we have not heard of a single KIC failure with a 25K structural, 23K brake rating.

HALDEX: GG4692OEDQ2N
Section thickness. Can you send me a model asap of your Hub and Drum and I will compare?
Correct on-highway ratings. Typically for steerable trailers, lowbeds, and some times for boosters. If it gets used off-highway, then it would be just when moving into a construction site as slow speeds to load/unload.

Thank you!

Brandon Hayter

Director of Products

From: Bob Klein 
Sent: Friday, February 13, 2026 11:54 AM
To: Jason Heath ; Brandon Hayter ; Kevin Langley 
Cc: KGI Engineering ; Brian Farquhar ; Collin Shore 
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub  

Brandon,

 

I believe our validation requirements are more stringent than those of KIC.  We will find out later this year when we test a KIC hub to our requirements.

 

Could you provide a little more detail on the brakes? 

What brake manufacturer and friction material do you use?How do the section thicknesses of our drum compare to the KIC drum? 

One last question, the 23K brake and 25K axle ratings are on-highway ratings, not off-highway ratings, correct?

 

Thank you,

_____________________________________________

Cc: KGI Engineering ; Bob Klein ; Brian Farquhar ; Collin Shore 
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

Thanks for the information, Brandon. Please get me the total count of hub & drum assemblies and loose hubs (by part number). I’ll issue an RMA.

 

From: Brandon Hayter bhayter@kgi.ca> 
Sent: Friday, February 13, 2026 1:53 PM
To: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com>; Kevin Langley klangley@kgi.ca>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>; Bob Klein BKlein@waltheremc.com>; Brian Farquhar purchasing@kgi.ca>; Collin Shore cshore@kgi.ca>
Subject: Re: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

Hi guys, thanks for looking at this.

 

This is a concern, as our brakes are rated for 23K and the axles are rated for 25K structurally.

 

For reference, we did a comparison with your hub and the older 25K KIC rated one.

 

Your hub was thicker throughout the cross-section, the flange, and had additional stiffeners. Plus you have the higher grade proprietary material.

 

I understand you have to do your due diligence, I just wanted to provide a reference to the previous competition.

 

As an aside, we should look into the options for returning the hub and drum + hub assemblies.

 

Let us know, thanks again.

 

 

Brandon Hayter

Director of Products

I had hoped the hub was overdesigned and we could uprate without a lot of testing but that is not the case.

 

Regards,

Jason

Today we spoke about your issue in our huddle meeting with the Engineering team. The hub itself appears to be strong as it passes our internal testing with high cycle counts. Hi Jason,

 

Hope all is well!

 

As you see below, I met with Advance in Regina this week and demo’d the QuickSet. 

 

The demo and meeting went well. 

 

This was jumping off point and there will me more discussions ahead, but for now, they will review the info I sent and I will circle back to further our talks in the near future.

 

They did ask me about pricing and other OEMs using the QuickSet. I said it would be best to bring you into our meetings moving forward so you can talk more specifically about Walther and the other details surrounding the QuickSet.

 

They install ~600 wheel ends per year. Currently standard on ConMet preset plus. 90% of their axles are non-dressed.

 

They did see the advantage of QuickSet, and they really liked the 7-year PPS warranty as well.

 

I’ll keep you posted when I plan to meet with them again (online or in person) and hopefully you’re able to join me.

 

Please let me know if you have any questions in the meantime.

 

Much Thanks,

 

 

   

Brad Major
Regional Sales Manager - MB, SK & Southern BC

E: Brad.Major@stemco.com
M: 204.430.2868
1020 Lorimar Dr., Mississauga,Ontario L5S 1R8
www.stemco.com 

From: Major, Brad 
Sent: Friday, February 13, 2026 4:06 PM
To: Maricar Battuing ; ycordero@aepl.ca; krieger@aepl.ca
Cc: Jason Heath (JHeath@waltheremc.com) 
Subject: Stemco - Walther QuickSet Hub

 

Hello Maricar, Yolanda and Kevin,

 

I don’t have everyone’s email so please forward this to Chad and Perry when you’re able.

 

I’d like to thank you all again for taking the time to meet with me this week. It was great to have an opportunity to demo the Walther QuickSet and discuss some of the benefits it offers.

 

As mentioned, the demo was using Walther’s Dura-Light Hub which is an iron hub, but lighter than aluminum. All the components inside the hub are Stemco components. They are:

 

Seal: DiscoverXR (premium 1-pc seal)

Bearings: Stemco Platinum bearings – standard SETS

Axle Nut: Auto-Torq – self-locks, preloads bearings, reusable

 

**Hubcap: the QuickSet does not come with a hubcap, but Stemco offers a 7-Year Parts & Labour Warranty for OEMs who use the Sentinel hubcap in conjunction with the Walther QuickSet. Stemco PSI and Hendrickson Tire Max Pro tire inflation systems are also approved.

 

I’ve attached documents on each of the components, as well as the QuickSet and Dura-Light hub.

 

The PPS documents attached contains our warranty packages for both OEM and Aftermarket.

 

I also have a video of the QuickSet being installed but it’s a 6 min video that I will need to share with someone’s iPhone for best resolution. Please let me know whose iPhone I can send it to and I’ll get that underway.

 

I’ve looped in Jason Heath, the Walther Sales Rep as well. Jason can discuss pricing and logistics with you when you’re ready to have that conversation.

 

Please let me know if you have any questions in the meantime.

 

Thanks Very Much,

 

 

   

Brad Major
Regional Sales Manager - MB, SK & Southern BC

E: Brad.Major@stemco.com
M: 204.430.2868
1020 Lorimar Dr., Mississauga,Ontario L5S 1R8
www.stemco.com

AAMkAGFiMThhMWE4LTBiOTYtNDY2OS05NjE3LThk.subject: Trip Report - California
AAMkAGFiMThhMWE4LTBiOTYtNDY2OS05NjE3LThk.body:
Last week, Keith and I journeyed from Hyundai Translead to WSD (Tijuana to Sacramento).

 

Keith and I agree that that’s a lot of time to spend in a car with me and Keith.

 

Three principle opportunities arose.

Brake Drums at Hyundai.  Margins would be tight, but not as tight as Hendrickson, and the volume would be from 90K to 170K (bad year to good year).  Hyundai is building out manufacturing capacity in northern Illinois.Wheel ends at Premier Trailer through M&R.  Jason Evett (Premeir Traielr, who was very close to John Logan) is buddies with Jason Reeher (M&R) and buys his axles from M&R now.  Keith and I will discuss with Jason Reeher next week in Texas.WSD – It has been slow, but WSD has put out some big Dura-Light quotes and expressed interest and willingness to making joint sales calls to spread the word among the smaller manufacturers out there. 

Notably, he let us know that Pat Trafican (PT Welding, Sacramento) dropped Dura-Light at some point in the past because they were breaking in the fields.  Henry didn’t have more detail, but committed to help us regain the PT Welding business.

 

Thanks!

Bob

   Top Transportation Teams Feedback was Gold, Participants SayThere are several reasons to enroll in the Top Transportation Teams program, but one that often goes unnoticed is the anonymous survey results you receive. This provides insights into what's important to your team."We found that when leaders create clear avenues for employee voice and feedback, it builds trust, increases engagement, and brings forward real operational insight from the people closest to the work," continues Mapengo.Enroll in the Top Transportation Teams program and show your team, your community, your drivers, and your staff just how excellent you are. This program recognizes the best of the best in school transportation. Whether you're a district or a bus company serving schools, you owe it to yourself and your team to see if you're a Top Transportation Team.By participating in the Top Transportation Teams program you will: Boost team morale by creating an initiative to rally aroundShow your team you believe in themShowcase your achievementsAnd if you win... Earn recognition that lasts all year! And if you don't win the top prize, you still win by gaining insights into your operation.A member of each winning team receives a free ticket to the STN Expo in Reno, Nevada, in July 2026, compliments of School Transportation News. Transfinder covers airfare and lodging.Don't miss out! Click here to get started. And don't miss our Feb. 26th Best Practices webinar featuring districts that used the Top Transportation Teams feedback to improve their departments. Click here to register for the free webinar.
