From: Brandon Hayter  
Sent: Friday, February 13, 2026 5:09 PM
To: Bob Klein ; Jason Heath ; Kevin Langley 
Cc: KGI Engineering ; Brian Farquhar ; Collin Shore 
Subject: Re: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

Hi Bob,

 

I assumed that your validation requirements would be more stringent. If it helps, we have not heard of a single KIC failure with a 25K structural, 23K brake rating.

 

HALDEX: GG4692OEDQ2N
Section thickness. Can you send me a model asap of your Hub and Drum and I will compare?
Correct on-highway ratings. Typically for steerable trailers, lowbeds, and some times for boosters. If it gets used off-highway, then it would be just when moving into a construction site as slow speeds to load/unload.
 

 

Thank you!

 

 

Brandon Hayter

Director of Products

P. 250.499.2492   Ext. 106
www.kgi.ca
725 Hwy 3, Cawston, BC, V0X 1C3

 

 

 

From: Bob Klein BKlein@waltheremc.com>
Sent: Friday, February 13, 2026 11:54 AM
To: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com>; Brandon Hayter bhayter@kgi.ca>; Kevin Langley klangley@kgi.ca>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>; Brian Farquhar purchasing@kgi.ca>; Collin Shore cshore@kgi.ca>
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub 

 

Brandon,

 

I believe our validation requirements are more stringent than those of KIC.  We will find out later this year when we test a KIC hub to our requirements.

 

Could you provide a little more detail on the brakes? 

What brake manufacturer and friction material do you use?How do the section thicknesses of our drum compare to the KIC drum? 

One last question, the 23K brake and 25K axle ratings are on-highway ratings, not off-highway ratings, correct?

 

Thank you,

_____________________________________________

Bob Klein  |  Engineering Manager

3501 Shotwell Drive | Franklin, Ohio 45005 United States

direct 937-743-8132 | main 937.743.8125 ext.615 | fax 937.743.8232

bklein@waltheremc.com | www.waltheremc.com

 

From: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com> 
Sent: Friday, February 13, 2026 2:26 PM
To: Brandon Hayter bhayter@kgi.ca>; Kevin Langley klangley@kgi.ca>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>; Bob Klein BKlein@waltheremc.com>; Brian Farquhar purchasing@kgi.ca>; Collin Shore cshore@kgi.ca>
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

Thanks for the information, Brandon. Please get me the total count of hub & drum assemblies and loose hubs (by part number). I’ll issue an RMA.

 

From: Brandon Hayter bhayter@kgi.ca> 
Sent: Friday, February 13, 2026 1:53 PM
To: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com>; Kevin Langley klangley@kgi.ca>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>; Bob Klein BKlein@waltheremc.com>; Brian Farquhar purchasing@kgi.ca>; Collin Shore cshore@kgi.ca>
Subject: Re: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

Hi guys, thanks for looking at this.

 

This is a concern, as our brakes are rated for 23K and the axles are rated for 25K structurally.

 

For reference, we did a comparison with your hub and the older 25K KIC rated one.

 

Your hub was thicker throughout the cross-section, the flange, and had additional stiffeners. Plus you have the higher grade proprietary material.

 

I understand you have to do your due diligence, I just wanted to provide a reference to the previous competition.

 

As an aside, we should look into the options for returning the hub and drum + hub assemblies.

 

Let us know, thanks again.

 

 

Brandon Hayter

Director of Products

P. 250.499.2492   Ext. 106
www.kgi.ca
725 Hwy 3, Cawston, BC, V0X 1C3

 

 

 

 

From: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com>
Sent: Friday, February 13, 2026 10:41 AM
To: Kevin Langley klangley@kgi.ca>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>; Bob Klein BKlein@waltheremc.com>; Brian Farquhar purchasing@kgi.ca>; Collin Shore cshore@kgi.ca>
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

Kevin, Bob walked me thru the technical challenges. The hub was originally designed for 20k. Material was removed to reduce weight. In order to be sure we can uprate to 23k, we will have to thorough testing and we have tests in front of this that need to be done first.

 

I had hoped the hub was overdesigned and we could uprate without a lot of testing but that is not the case.

 

Regards,

Jason

 

 

From: Jason Heath
Sent: Friday, February 13, 2026 12:11 PM
To: 'Kevin Langley' klangley@kgi.ca>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>; Bob Klein BKlein@waltheremc.com>; Brian Farquhar purchasing@kgi.ca>; Collin Shore cshore@kgi.ca>
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

Kevin, we are working internally to see what can be done about  up-rating. I think you should procure alternate hubs for these builds  (or at least get them on order if you can cancel them). It’s not looking likely that Walther will be able to up-rate these hubs without more time/testing.

 

Question came up about the drum. 03-00050 is rated to 18k. Is that sufficient?

 

Thanks,
Jason

 

From: Kevin Langley klangley@kgi.ca>
Sent: Thursday, February 12, 2026 12:56 PM
To: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>; Bob Klein BKlein@waltheremc.com>; Brian Farquhar purchasing@kgi.ca>; Collin Shore cshore@kgi.ca>
Subject: Re: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

Hi Jason,

 

We use about 200-300 units per year.  If we cannot get the updated load rating, then there are 4 builds that would potentially go on hold within the next 2-3 weeks.  
What is your estimated timeline for the engineering analysis to be complete?

 

Thanks, 

 

Kevin Langley
Product Design Engineer 

P. 250.499.2492   
www.kgi.ca
725 Hwy 3, Cawston, BC, V0X 1C3

Kevin, what’s your rough usage annually on this hub?

 

From: Jason Heath
Sent: Thursday, February 12, 2026 8:29 AM
To: 'Kevin Langley' klangley@kgi.ca>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>; Bob Klein BKlein@waltheremc.com>; purchasing@kgi.ca
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

Hi Kevin, cc’ing Bob Klein (Walther Engineering Manager) and Brian Farquhar.

 

Today we spoke about your issue in our huddle meeting with the Engineering team. The hub itself appears to be strong as it passes our internal testing with high cycle counts. It is possible/likely they will pass testing at 23k loads but Engineering will need to test to verify before it can increase the rating.

 

I apologize again for the inconvenience. I know you need at least 23k rating for this build and I won’t be able to help you in time. Do you have other options? I might be able to direct you to another source.

 

Do you have 20k applications where you can use up the current inventory or will you need to return?

 

Thank you,

Jason

 

____________________________________________

Jason Heath  |  Director, OEM & Fleet Sales

3501 Shotwell Drive | Franklin, Ohio 45005 United States

cell 616.914.2665 | fax 937.743.8232

jheath@waltheremc.com | www.waltheremc.com

 

 

 

From: Kevin Langley klangley@kgi.ca>
Sent: Tuesday, February 10, 2026 3:38 PM
To: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>
Subject: Re: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

You don't often get email from klangley@kgi.ca. Learn why this is important

Hi Jason,

 

The hubs were purchased as a direct replacement without considering the load rating.  We have some on hand now for a job and 54 more on order.  These are all currently being used in a dual wheel setup, so the offset rating is not critical at the moment.  However, we would need the load rating increased to a minimum of 23k for us to comfortably use them, with 25k rating preferred.  

 

Thanks, 

 

Kevin Langley
Product Design Engineer 

P. 250.499.2492   
www.kgi.ca
725 Hwy 3, Cawston, BC, V0X 1C3

 

 

From: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com>
Sent: Tuesday, February 10, 2026 11:15 AM
To: Kevin Langley klangley@kgi.ca>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

Apologies Kevin! I did not know KIC had higher ratings for this hub. Let me reach out to our engineering team to see why it is only rated for 20k applications. Our other N spindles are rated for 23k applications (and permit off/insets). We have a severe duty version rated for 25k and allows for offsets. Unfortunately these are 10 bolt hubs, not 8.

 

What is the status, did you end up purchasing these hubs and now you cannot use? Please advise. I will help if this is the case.

 

Regards,

Jason

 

From: Kevin Langley klangley@kgi.ca>
Sent: Tuesday, February 10, 2026 1:59 PM
To: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>
Subject: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

You don't often get email from klangley@kgi.ca. Learn why this is important

 

CAUTION: CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe.

Hello Jason, 

 

It was recently brought to engineering attention that the Walther hub assembly 10-00048-101, which was supplied as a direct replacement for KIC hub assembly 00133LT, does not have the same dynamic load rating.  I have attached both load tables for reference.  

 

For this hub to be considered a suitable replacement part, we would need to match the 25,000 lb GAW (12,500 lb/hub) rating that was previously used.  We would also prefer to have a derated load value for the wheel offsets instead of the prohibited note.  

 

Can you please confirm that:

 

The attached table is the most recent version. If the load rating can be updated to match what KIC had offered.  

 

Thanks, 

 

Kevin Langley
Product Design Engineer 

P. 250.499.2492   
www.kgi.ca
725 Hwy 3, Cawston, BC, V0X 1C3

Paint or rust on hub and brake drum mating surfaces is strictly prohibited and must be completely removed before assembly.

 

This includes (but is not limited to):

Wheel studsWheel nutsMounting flangeHub pilotsHubcap mounting flangeThreads and screws 

All mating surfaces must be clean, dry, and free of paint, rust, corrosion, debris, or any foreign material to ensure proper fit, torque retention, and safe operation.

 

 

If you have any questions, please give me a call

 

Sincerely

From: Brent Armentrout  
Sent: Tuesday, February 3, 2026 5:34 PM
To: Bob Montgomery ; Jason Heath ; Keith Fally 
Subject: RE: Customer Classes Review - Again

 

Do we expect to sell to STEMCO and GEN in the future?  As you know, Bendix is also a unique type of customer.

 

Some thoughts on these three (STEMCO, GEN, BENDIX)

All three represent an additional mouth to feed on the path to a trailer OEM.All three are not only suppliers but also potential customers.All three are potentially competitors in the hub market:GEN switched from our hubs to WebbSTEMCO had Trifecta with Webb and if they could do it profitablyBENDIX already sells Conmet and Webb hub mated to their rotors.  While they desire to switch those to Walther hubs, their desires could change over time.Regarding the category name “COMP MFG”, appropriately, the COMP could mean component or competitor.  Maybe we just simplify the category name to “COMP”? 

Brent

 

From: Bob Montgomery bmontgomery@waltheremc.com> 
Sent: Tuesday, February 3, 2026 4:46 PM
To: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com>; Brent Armentrout BArmentrout@waltheremc.com>; Keith Fally kfally@waltheremc.com>
Subject: Customer Classes Review - Again

 

Please have a quick look at the attached and let me know if you disagree with any of my changes.

 

We have TRLR and TRLR OEM, which mean the same thing to me, I changed all to TRLR OEMWe have some Trailer OEA accounts that are listed as PDC (same price level, but different)Should we have a category for component suppliers that’s not the same as Axle/Susp Manufacturers?  I’m thinking like STEMCO, GEN, Bendix.  Mayby “Comp MFG”

CAUTION: CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe. 

  
  
TBB Market Watch | View online
  
 
February 16, 2026

 

Editor's Note

Look for Dan and Kevin at NATDA's Trailer Tech Expo in Reno this week. One of them will not be comfortable in the expected snow.

 

Stoughton grows dealer network in West with TEC Equipment
With more than 30 locations in eight states serving the trucking industry, TEC Equipment has become one of the largest full-service truck and trailer dealerships in the United States.
Read More 

Sponsored
 
Maxon MLA – Heavy-Duty Liftgate Design with Tire-Specific Performance
Designed for the demands of tire service, the new Maxon MLA liftgate delivers heavy-duty performance with 1,600- or 2,000-lb capacities and a geometry that lets large tires clear with ease. The MLA comes with a galvanized finish, greaseable pivots for simplified maintenance, plus a variety of ramp and tire-rack options.

Visit www.maxonlift.com 

 
KEITH introduces RX3 for waste, recycling applications
Operators gain a drive designed around real world conditions: heavy loads, contaminants and high duty cycles.
Read More 

Sponsored
 
New 810-TX Sender Bar: High-Resolution, Temperature-Ready Design
The 810-TX Sender bar sets a higher standard for flammable liquid measurement. It offers 1/4" resolution, includes temperature sensors accurate to ±0.1°F, and features a field-replaceable battery module for easy maintenance. Must be paired with compatible displays and floats.

Learn more 

 
Podcast: Ford Pro on work truck TCO, the journey to zero downtime
Ford Pro's Eric Rice describes the latest work truck trends: driver assistance, predictive maintenance, the technician shortage, and more.
Listen Now 

Sponsored

Bob, this is a brand-new RP and the information below for trailers is very misleading. The tractor side shows the savings of Aluminum to cast but the trailer side only shows ADI to Aluminum and very few trailers are built with Aluminum hubs. 

 

We need to confirm the weight savings but also all the weight savings from ADI to Full cast need to be included on this document. I have a feeling our buddies at Conmet help with this chart. 

 

I need to submit this request by tomorrow so it gets counted as a vote.

 

 

If you have any questions, please give me a call

 

Sincerely

 

Keith Fally

Global momentum around sustainable mobility continues to accelerate as industries move from strategy to execution. Investment, infrastructure readiness, and cross-sector collaboration are defining the next phase of information with a clear focus on scalable deployment and measurable impact.

This October 13 - 14 at ADNEC Centre Abu Dhabi, the 5th Edition of 

EVIS Abu Dhabi 2026 co-located with EcoMobility Global, a 2-day exhibition built for structured business engagement, high-level networking and partnership.

In 2026, attendees can expect expanded focused zones that deepen sector conversation, unlock targeted investment opportunities and enable direct engagement with stakeholders driving real market adoption.

INQUIRE NOW

DOWNLOAD PROSPECTUS

APPLY AS A SPEAKER

MEKMÜHDER, in its 10th year, is a strong professional organization bringing together academia, industry, and civil society.

With 1,800 members, 60,000+ subscribers, 8,000+ affiliated companies, and 4,000+ CVs, it serves as the sole representative of the profession in Türkiye, providing an active ecosystem for career development, education, and academic advancement.

It develops competent engineers for the Mechatronics Era.

BE A PARTNER

EVIS America is a 2-day B2B conference and exhibition designed to connect opportunity with execution. It brings the ecosystem into one place for commercial conversations, solution discovery, and partnership building—grounded in real deployment needs and measurable outcomes.

Join us November 03 - 05 at The Glass City Center, Toledo, Ohio to explore what’s next in EV deployment and connect with the partners making it happen.

EVIS America bridges U.S mobility market with global innovation ecosystems—creating new pathways for partnerships, policy, and investment.

CharIN  e.V. is a global association dedicated to advancing electric vehicle charging  standards. Supporting both the Combined Charging System (CCS) and the North  American Charging Standard (NACS), CharIN promotes interoperability,  innovation, and seamless charging experiences across the global e-mobility  ecosystem

INQUIRE NOW

DOWNLOAD PROSPECTUS
EVIS INSIGHTS

First phase of Dubai loop begins construction

Dubai has officially launched construction on one of its most ambitious urban mobility initiatives to date: the Dubai Loop, an underground electric transport network developed in partnership with The Boring Company.

Read more
 

AITO enters The UAE luxury EV market through strategic partnership with Abu Dhabi Motors

The brand confirmed its expansion through a strategic partnership with Performance Plus Motors, a subsidiary of Abu Dhabi Motors (ADM), underscoring the UAE’s growing role as a launchpad for premium electric and intelligent mobility brands.

Read more
 

Xpeng’s land aircraft carrier push signals flying cars are entering a new phase

The company’s flying car business, ARIDGE (formerly AeroHT), is actively ramping up marketing for its Land Aircraft Carrier, a purpose built road vehicle designed to transport, store, and recharge a detachable eVTOL module that ARIDGE positions as its “flying car.”

Read more
 

Why US automakers are turning to grid-scale battery storage

As the electric vehicle market in the United States undergoes a period of recalibration, automakers are increasingly turning their attention to a parallel opportunity: large-scale battery energy storage systems that support renewable energy, utilities, and data center infrastructure.

Read more
 

EV load aggregation & V2G innovation transforming the grid

The Mobility House North America has launched Cascade EV Aggregator, a utility-scale vehicle-to-grid (V2G) integration platform designed to transform electric vehicles into coordinated energy resources across North America.

Read more

Hey Marc-André,

Sorry for the alarm. Nothing has changed with the design at all. KGI noticed on our tech sheets that the 048 hub is rated for 20k but it performs very well on our test and 23k is probably not a problem so we will be testing to make sure. How long have you been using these hubs?

 

I show as far back as 2016 but probably longer. This is a paper issue, not a design issue.

 

Thanks,

Jason

Transportation implications of redistricting rarely show up in early discussions, yet they often determine long-term efficiency.

 Greetings! 

Listen to the new STN Podcast at stnpodcast.com! Our special guest is Heather Perry, Superintendent of Schools for Maine’s Gorham Public Schools, a finalists for the 2026 National Superintendent of the Year Award by AASA. She discusses her leadership journey, winter transportation operations, the value of collaboration and staff support, and a robust student career support program.

Also, we discuss First Student’s purchase of Chicago area contractor Cook Illinois Corp., Blue Bird 2026 Q1 earnings report, and a California study on lap/shoulder seatbelt efficacy.

Special thanks to our sponsors Transfinder, Everdriven and School Radio. 

  ﻿

Regards,

Tony

To: Bob Klein ; Jason Heath ; Kevin Langley

Subject: Re: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

Hi Bob,

 

I assumed that your validation requirements would be more stringent. If it helps, we have not heard of a single KIC failure with a 25K structural, 23K brake rating.

 

HALDEX: GG4692OEDQ2N
Section thickness. Can you send me a model asap of your Hub and Drum and I will compare?
Correct on-highway ratings. Typically for steerable trailers, lowbeds, and some times for boosters. If it gets used off-highway, then it would be just when moving into a construction site as slow speeds to load/unload.
 

 

Thank you!

 

 

Brandon Hayter

Director of Products

From: Bob Klein BKlein@waltheremc.com>
Sent: Friday, February 13, 2026 11:54 AM
To: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com>; Brandon Hayter bhayter@kgi.ca>; Kevin Langley klangley@kgi.ca>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>; Brian Farquhar purchasing@kgi.ca>; Collin Shore cshore@kgi.ca>
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub 

 

Brandon,

 

I believe our validation requirements are more stringent than those of KIC.  We will find out later this year when we test a KIC hub to our requirements.

 

Could you provide a little more detail on the brakes? 

What brake manufacturer and friction material do you use?How do the section thicknesses of our drum compare to the KIC drum? 

One last question, the 23K brake and 25K axle ratings are on-highway ratings, not off-highway ratings, correct?

 

Thank you,

_____________________________________________

Bob Klein  |  Engineering Manager

3501 Shotwell Drive | Franklin, Ohio 45005 United States

direct 937-743-8132 | main 937.743.8125 ext.615 | fax 937.743.8232

bklein@waltheremc.com | www.waltheremc.com

Sent: Friday, February 13, 2026 2:26 PM
To: Brandon Hayter bhayter@kgi.ca>; Kevin Langley klangley@kgi.ca>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>; Bob Klein BKlein@waltheremc.com>; Brian Farquhar purchasing@kgi.ca>; Collin Shore cshore@kgi.ca>
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

Thanks for the information, Brandon. Please get me the total count of hub & drum assemblies and loose hubs (by part number). I’ll issue an RMA.

 

From: Brandon Hayter bhayter@kgi.ca> 
Sent: Friday, February 13, 2026 1:53 PM
To: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com>; Kevin Langley klangley@kgi.ca>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>; Bob Klein BKlein@waltheremc.com>; Brian Farquhar purchasing@kgi.ca>; Collin Shore cshore@kgi.ca>
Subject: Re: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

Hi guys, thanks for looking at this.

 

This is a concern, as our brakes are rated for 23K and the axles are rated for 25K structurally.

 

For reference, we did a comparison with your hub and the older 25K KIC rated one.

 

Your hub was thicker throughout the cross-section, the flange, and had additional stiffeners. Plus you have the higher grade proprietary material.

 

I understand you have to do your due diligence, I just wanted to provide a reference to the previous competition.

 

As an aside, we should look into the options for returning the hub and drum + hub assemblies.

 

Let us know, thanks again.

 

 

Brandon Hayter

Director of Products

P. 250.499.2492   Ext. 106
www.kgi.ca
725 Hwy 3, Cawston, BC, V0X 1C3

 

 

 

 

From: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com>
Sent: Friday, February 13, 2026 10:41 AM
To: Kevin Langley klangley@kgi.ca>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>; Bob Klein BKlein@waltheremc.com>; Brian Farquhar purchasing@kgi.ca>; Collin Shore cshore@kgi.ca>
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

Kevin, Bob walked me thru the technical challenges. The hub was originally designed for 20k. Material was removed to reduce weight. In order to be sure we can uprate to 23k, we will have to thorough testing and we have tests in front of this that need to be done first.

 

I had hoped the hub was overdesigned and we could uprate without a lot of testing but that is not the case.

 

Regards,

Jason

 

 

From: Jason Heath
Sent: Friday, February 13, 2026 12:11 PM
To: 'Kevin Langley' klangley@kgi.ca>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>; Bob Klein BKlein@waltheremc.com>; Brian Farquhar purchasing@kgi.ca>; Collin Shore cshore@kgi.ca>
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

Kevin, we are working internally to see what can be done about  up-rating. I think you should procure alternate hubs for these builds  (or at least get them on order if you can cancel them). It’s not looking likely that Walther will be able to up-rate these hubs without more time/testing.

 

Question came up about the drum. 03-00050 is rated to 18k. Is that sufficient?

 

Thanks,
Jason

 

From: Kevin Langley klangley@kgi.ca>
Sent: Thursday, February 12, 2026 12:56 PM
To: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>; Bob Klein BKlein@waltheremc.com>; Brian Farquhar purchasing@kgi.ca>; Collin Shore cshore@kgi.ca>
Subject: Re: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

Hi Jason,

 

We use about 200-300 units per year.  If we cannot get the updated load rating, then there are 4 builds that would potentially go on hold within the next 2-3 weeks.  
What is your estimated timeline for the engineering analysis to be complete?

 

Thanks, 

 

Kevin Langley
Product Design Engineer 

P. 250.499.2492   
www.kgi.ca
725 Hwy 3, Cawston, BC, V0X 1C3

Sent: Thursday, February 12, 2026 5:55 AM
To: Kevin Langley klangley@kgi.ca>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>; Bob Klein BKlein@waltheremc.com>; Brian Farquhar purchasing@kgi.ca>
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

Kevin, what’s your rough usage annually on this hub?

 

From: Jason Heath
Sent: Thursday, February 12, 2026 8:29 AM
To: 'Kevin Langley' klangley@kgi.ca>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>; Bob Klein BKlein@waltheremc.com>; purchasing@kgi.ca
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

Hi Kevin, cc’ing Bob Klein (Walther Engineering Manager) and Brian Farquhar.

 

Today we spoke about your issue in our huddle meeting with the Engineering team. The hub itself appears to be strong as it passes our internal testing with high cycle counts. It is possible/likely they will pass testing at 23k loads but Engineering will need to test to verify before it can increase the rating.

 

I apologize again for the inconvenience. I know you need at least 23k rating for this build and I won’t be able to help you in time. Do you have other options? I might be able to direct you to another source.

 

Do you have 20k applications where you can use up the current inventory or will you need to return?

 

Thank you,

Jason

 

____________________________________________

Jason Heath  |  Director, OEM & Fleet Sales

3501 Shotwell Drive | Franklin, Ohio 45005 United States

cell 616.914.2665 | fax 937.743.8232

jheath@waltheremc.com | www.waltheremc.com

 

 

 

From: Kevin Langley klangley@kgi.ca>
Sent: Tuesday, February 10, 2026 3:38 PM
To: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>
Subject: Re: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

You don't often get email from klangley@kgi.ca. Learn why this is important

Hi Jason,

 

The hubs were purchased as a direct replacement without considering the load rating.  We have some on hand now for a job and 54 more on order.  These are all currently being used in a dual wheel setup, so the offset rating is not critical at the moment.  However, we would need the load rating increased to a minimum of 23k for us to comfortably use them, with 25k rating preferred.  

 

Thanks, 

 

Kevin Langley
Product Design Engineer 

P. 250.499.2492   
www.kgi.ca
725 Hwy 3, Cawston, BC, V0X 1C3

 

 

From: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com>
Sent: Tuesday, February 10, 2026 11:15 AM
To: Kevin Langley klangley@kgi.ca>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

Apologies Kevin! I did not know KIC had higher ratings for this hub. Let me reach out to our engineering team to see why it is only rated for 20k applications. Our other N spindles are rated for 23k applications (and permit off/insets). We have a severe duty version rated for 25k and allows for offsets. Unfortunately these are 10 bolt hubs, not 8.

 

What is the status, did you end up purchasing these hubs and now you cannot use? Please advise. I will help if this is the case.

 

Regards,

Jason

 

From: Kevin Langley klangley@kgi.ca>
Sent: Tuesday, February 10, 2026 1:59 PM
To: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>
Subject: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

You don't often get email from klangley@kgi.ca. Learn why this is important

 

CAUTION: CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe.

Hello Jason, 

 

It was recently brought to engineering attention that the Walther hub assembly 10-00048-101, which was supplied as a direct replacement for KIC hub assembly 00133LT, does not have the same dynamic load rating.  I have attached both load tables for reference.  

 

For this hub to be considered a suitable replacement part, we would need to match the 25,000 lb GAW (12,500 lb/hub) rating that was previously used.  We would also prefer to have a derated load value for the wheel offsets instead of the prohibited note.  

 

Can you please confirm that:

 

The attached table is the most recent version. If the load rating can be updated to match what KIC had offered.  

 

Thanks, 

 

Kevin Langley
Product Design Engineer 

P. 250.499.2492   
www.kgi.ca
725 Hwy 3, Cawston, BC, V0X 1C3

AAMkAGFiMThhMWE4LTBiOTYtNDY2OS05NjE3LThk.body:
CAUTION: CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe. 

Why Hardware Surface Finish Can Make or Break Polymer Seal Performance
​​​​
Seal failures are often blamed on materials, profiles, or spring selection. In reality, many polymer seal issues originate somewhere else entirely — the surface the seal is running against.

It’s not uncommon to see an application upgraded from an elastomer O-ring to a PTFE spring-energized seal to reduce friction, extend life, or handle higher temperatures. On paper, the upgrade looks flawless. In practice, leakage appears immediately.

​​>> Learn how hardware surface finish affects polymer seal performance

Sustainable Liquid Strategies for Modern Data CentersAs digital transformation drives surging demands for cloud, AI, and high-performance computing, data centres must deliver at new levels of efficiency, scalability, and sustainability - all while keeping uptime uncompromised.

Legacy air-cooling approaches, which historically formed the backbone of thermal management, simply can't keep up with today’s pace. Chip manufacturers continue to develop CPUs and GPUs with unprecedented performance, resulting in significant heat that traditional air systems cannot reliably manage. This has accelerated the adoption of liquid cooling, a smarter, more efficient way to future-proof operations and support the next wave of computing breakthroughs.

>> Explore liquid strategies for data centers

Our mailing address is:

Gallagher Fluid Seals, Inc.
500 Hertzog Boulevard,
King of Prussia, PA 19406
Phone: (800) 822-4063
Fax: (888) 280-4235update subscription preferences

FLEXIBLE EVERY DAYUnlock the Non-CDL Advantage with the Collins MPV

 

When safety is non-negotiable, the Collins non-CDL MPV delivers the one thing you can count on: protection for your passengers. We do not just build buses; we build the foundation for your most important journeys because when safety is your standard, compromise is not an option.

Our Route to Safety Doesn't Allow Detours

 
 
Read Collins MPV vs. Alternative Transportation 

The Unseen ShieldMeeting School Activity Bus FMVSS safety standards, the Collins MPV incorporates a reinforced tubular HSLA steel safety cage, high integrity body panels, and side impact protection to help protect passengers.

 

 

 

Perspective is PowerA full view glass entrance door and expansive side view window give drivers clear visibility of their surroundings and nearby children, helping reduce blind spots and improve safety during loading and unloading of passengers.

A Bus that Molds to Your Mission

Your Custom CanvasThe Collins MPV offers customizable seating and accessibility options that adapt to the needs of your organization, helping you move passengers safely, comfortably, and efficiently every day.

 

Maneuvering Made Easy

AAMkAGFiMThhMWE4LTBiOTYtNDY2OS05NjE3LThk.subject: Undeliverable: FW: Fleetpride
AAMkAGFiMThhMWE4LTBiOTYtNDY2OS05NjE3LThk.body:
Your message to kim.oeigh@fleetpride.com couldn't be delivered.
kim.oeigh wasn't found at fleetpride.com.
JHeath Office 365 kim.oeigh Action Required Recipient Unknown To address 
How to Fix ItThe address might be misspelled or might not exist. Try one or more of the following:Retype the recipient's address, then resend the message - If you're using Outlook, open this non-delivery report message and click Send Again from the menu or ribbon. In Outlook on the web, select this message, and then click the "To send this message again, click here." link located just above the message preview window. In the To or Cc line, delete and then retype the entire recipient's address (ignore any address suggestions). After typing the complete address, click Send to resend the message. If you're using an email program other than Outlook or Outlook on the web, follow its standard way for resending a message. Just be sure to delete and retype the recipient's entire address before resending it.Remove the recipient from the recipient Auto-Complete List, then resend the message - If you're using Outlook or Outlook on the web, follow the steps in the "Remove the recipient from the recipient Auto-Complete List" section of this article. Then resend the message. Be sure to delete and retype the recipient's entire address before clicking Send.Contact the recipient by some other means, (by phone, for example) to confirm you're using the right address. Ask them if they've set up an email forwarding rule that could be forwarding your message to an incorrect address.If the problem continues, ask the recipient to tell their email admin about the problem, and give them the error (and the name of the server that reported it) shown below. It's likely that only the recipient's email admin can fix this problem.
Was this helpful? Send feedback to Microsoft. 
More Info for Email AdminsStatus code: 550 5.4.1 

This error occurred because a message was sent to an email address hosted by Office 365, but the address doesn't exist in the receiving organization's Office 365 directory. Directory Based Edge Blocking (DBEB) is enabled for fleetpride.com, and DBEB rejects messages addressed to recipients who don't exist in the receiving organization's Office 365 directory. This error is reported by the recipient domain's email server, but most often it can be fixed by the person who sent the message. If the steps in the How to Fix It section above don't fix the problem, and you're the email admin for the recipient, try one or more of the following:

Check that the email address exists and is correct - Confirm that the recipient address exists in your Office 365 directory, is correct, and is accepting messages.

Synchronize your directories - Make sure directory synchronization is working correctly, and that the recipient's email address exists in both Office 365 and in your on-premises directory.

Check for errant forwarding rules - Check for forwarding rules for the original recipient that might be trying to forward the message to an invalid address. Forwarding can be set up by an admin via mail flow rules or mailbox forwarding address settings, or by the recipient via the Forwarding or Inbox Rules features.

Make sure the recipient has a valid license - Make sure the recipient has an Office 365 license assigned to them. The recipient's email admin can use the Office 365 admin center to assign a license to them (Users > Active Users > Select the recipient > Assigned License > Edit).

Make sure that mail flow settings and MX records are correct - Misconfigured mail flow or MX record settings can cause this error. Check your Office 365 mail flow settings to make sure your domain and any mail flow connectors are set up correctly. Also, work with your domain registrar to make sure the MX records for your domain are set up correctly.

For more information and additional tips to fix this issue, see this article.

CAUTION: CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe. 

 NTSB to Provide School Bus Investigation Updates at STN EXPO East | Learn More

 STN EXPO East | March 26-31, Charlotte, NC 

Before a school bus incident even happens, it’s important for student transportation professionals to be aware of federal safety recommendations and crash investigation procedures. Meg Sweeney from the National Transportation Safety Board (NTSB) will be at STN EXPO East to give attendees the latest updates.

Her session “NTSB Investigations & Recommendations: Lap/Shoulder Seat Belts to the School Bus Danger Zone” on March 31 wraps up the conference by outlining various facets of school bus crash investigations. She will discuss occupant protection, which will include NTSB recommendations on lap/shoulder seatbelts and their safety impact during crashes, and shed light on “Danger Zone” crashes, including incidents where vehicles struck stopped school buses or hit a pedestrian.

﻿View the full agenda of educational sessions now and secure your spot today. 

Register for the NTSB Crash Safety Session (It takes 2-min to register for the main conference )

 Questions? Contact the STN Events Team at events@stnmedia.com

View as Webpage

STN Media Group | 5334 Torrance Blvd. 3rd Fl. | Torrance, CA 90503 US

Unsubscribe | Update Profile | Constant Contact Data Notice

How to Fix It

The address might be misspelled or might not exist. Try one or more of the following:

·         Retype the recipient's address, then resend the message - If you're using Outlook, open this non-delivery report message and click Send Again from the menu or ribbon. In Outlook on the web, select this message, and then click the "To send this message again, click here." link located just above the message preview window. In the To or Cc line, delete and then retype the entire recipient's address (ignore any address suggestions). After typing the complete address, click Send to resend the message. If you're using an email program other than Outlook or Outlook on the web, follow its standard way for resending a message. Just be sure to delete and retype the recipient's entire address before resending it.

·         Remove the recipient from the recipient Auto-Complete List, then resend the message - If you're using Outlook or Outlook on the web, follow the steps in the "Remove the recipient from the recipient Auto-Complete List" section of this article. Then resend the message. Be sure to delete and retype the recipient's entire address before clicking Send.

·         Contact the recipient by some other means, (by phone, for example) to confirm you're using the right address. Ask them if they've set up an email forwarding rule that could be forwarding your message to an incorrect address.

If the problem continues, ask the recipient to tell their email admin about the problem, and give them the error (and the name of the server that reported it) shown below. It's likely that only the recipient's email admin can fix this problem.

Was this helpful? Send feedback to Microsoft. 

More Info for Email Admins

Status code: 550 5.4.1 

This error occurred because a message was sent to an email address hosted by Office 365, but the address doesn't exist in the receiving organization's Office 365 directory. Directory Based Edge Blocking (DBEB) is enabled for fleetpride.com, and DBEB rejects messages addressed to recipients who don't exist in the receiving organization's Office 365 directory. This error is reported by the recipient domain's email server, but most often it can be fixed by the person who sent the message. If the steps in the How to Fix It section above don't fix the problem, and you're the email admin for the recipient, try one or more of the following:

Check that the email address exists and is correct - Confirm that the recipient address exists in your Office 365 directory, is correct, and is accepting messages.

Synchronize your directories - Make sure directory synchronization is working correctly, and that the recipient's email address exists in both Office 365 and in your on-premises directory.

Check for errant forwarding rules - Check for forwarding rules for the original recipient that might be trying to forward the message to an invalid address. Forwarding can be set up by an admin via mail flow rules or mailbox forwarding address settings, or by the recipient via the Forwarding or Inbox Rules features.

Make sure the recipient has a valid license - Make sure the recipient has an Office 365 license assigned to them. The recipient's email admin can use the Office 365 admin center to assign a license to them (Users > Active Users > Select the recipient > Assigned License > Edit).

Make sure that mail flow settings and MX records are correct - Misconfigured mail flow or MX record settings can cause this error. Check your Office 365 mail flow settings to make sure your domain and any mail flow connectors are set up correctly. Also, work with your domain registrar to make sure the MX records for your domain are set up correctly.

For more information and additional tips to fix this issue, see this article.

Let’s keep widely communicating potential new business, potential lost business, and changes in the market or customer volumes that we foresee.

AAMkAGFiMThhMWE4LTBiOTYtNDY2OS05NjE3LThk.body:
CAUTION: CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe. 

You don’t have a content problem. You have a decision problem. Most B2B content dies because it has no distribution plan, no point of view, and no connection to pipeline.  ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ 
February 18, 2026   |   Read Online

 

In partnership with

Powered by Reel Axis

Welcome back to Marketing Qualified! Here’s what we’re talking about this week:

The 5 mistakes quietly killing your B2B content 

The fastest way to stand out in B2B 

  🤫 The 5 mistakes quietly killing your B2B contentYou don’t have a content problem. You have a decision problem. Most B2B content dies because it has no distribution plan, no point of view, and no connection to pipeline.

Look at what actually works. Notion doesn’t spray blog posts. They productize content with templates and tutorials that map to real jobs to be done, then seed those assets across communities, YouTube, and their own template gallery. Datadog leans into deep, technical walkthroughs that solve specific monitoring headaches, then slices those into docs, webinars, and sales enablement. 

Here are the five mistakes to stop making and how to fix them: 

1) No POV
Mistake: Vanilla listicles that could be written by anyone.
Fix: Pick a stance buyers can disagree with. If your post couldn’t get you fired, it probably won’t get you shared. 

2) No owner for distribution
Mistake: Publish and pray.
Fix: Assign a channel owner before you write. Plan 3 distribution paths per piece: partner newsletter, SME on LinkedIn, and a community post. 

3) Writing for search engines instead of stages
Mistake: Chasing volume keywords that attract tourists.
Fix: Build a content ladder for each stage: problem aware, solution framing, product proof. Map CTAs to the next step, not a demo jump scare. 

4) Ignoring product and customer input
Mistake: Content brainstorms with zero field intelligence.
Fix: Pull 10 real support tickets or sales call snippets and write to them. Your best topics are hiding in your pipeline, not in a keyword tool. 

5) Measuring vanity metrics
Mistake: Celebrating impressions that never convert.
Fix: Track content-assisted revenue, influenced opportunities, and sales usage. If sales won’t share it, rewrite it. 

Memorable takeaway: Make fewer, sharper pieces that ship with distribution baked in or don’t hit publish. 

  ✨ Supercharge Your AI Development with NarrowAI teams, developers, and product innovators… are you ready to build smarter and ship faster? Narrow is an automated prompt engineering and optimization platform that takes the guesswork out of working with large language models. Instead of spending hours writing, testing, and tuning prompts, Narrow does it for you — generating high-quality prompts, testing them across models, and continuously optimizing for performance, cost, and speed. 

What you’ll love:
💬 Automatically generate and refine expert-level prompts
🧪 Test and compare performance across multiple LLMs
📉 Reduce AI costs by up to 95% with smarter model selection
⏰ Deploy AI features up to 10× faster than manual workflows 

Whether you’re building AI-powered products, scaling LLM features, or just want to get more value from your AI stack — Narrow streamlines your workflow so you can focus on innovation, not iteration. Learn more and book a demo today at getnarrow.ai

  🗞️  In the news this week.🔍 Google explores AI Overviews opt-out and new site reputation abuse safeguards in search 

💸 HubSpot: B2B content budgets shift to owned media as long-form research outperforms webinars in 2026 

📧 Email deliverability crackdown continues: Gmail and Yahoo rules push senders to tighten authentication and list hygiene 

🤳 TikTok expands Lead Gen with native forms and CRM integrations for business accounts 

🛒 Amazon Ads introduces B2B buyer segments and Sponsored Display options for SaaS trials 

  ⏰ The fastest way to stand out in B2B: Build an AI POV and ship proofCrowded market? Join the club. The only way out is to say something sharp and back it up fast. 

You do not stand out by listing features. You stand out by owning a POV and proving it with visible, useful artifacts. AI makes this easier if you use it to produce proof, not fluff. 

Before: Generic positioning, feature dumps, blog posts nobody remembers.
After: A named point of view, a simple AI-powered proof, and relentless distribution. 

Try this 5-step playbook:

1) Name your POV: Write a 1-sentence stance that picks a fight. 

Example: "Forms slow revenue. Conversations speed it up." Keep it under 15 words. Make it disagreeable to someone. 

2) Pick the proof: What is the smallest AI thing that proves your stance? Options include: 

A benchmark that ranks your category on the outcome you care about 

A grader that scores prospects on that outcome 

A simulator that shows the delta between old way and your way 

3) Build the “micro-product” in 2 weeks:  

Use off-the-shelf LLMs plus your data. Ship ugly but useful. Add a CSV upload and a shareable result link. 

4) Turn proof into content fuel:

Weekly teardown: spotlight one result and what it means 

Monthly report: aggregate the data into trends 

Customer stories: 3 screenshots, 3 sentences, one metric 

5) Distribute with discipline:

Ads and LPs use the POV name in headline 

Sales deck opens with the benchmark chart, not your logo 

Retargeting promotes the grader or simulator, not another ebook 

Real-world signals:

HubSpot named “inbound” early, then built data reports and product features to reinforce it. 

Drift took a stand on “no forms” and launched a simple chat widget as living proof. 

Cloudflare runs public “Innovation Weeks” that ship small utilities and benchmarks to make their speed story obvious. 

Attention follows evidence. Make your claim, then show the receipts every week.

Memorable takeaway: If your POV has no proof, it is just a vibe.

On Jan 12, 2026, at 1:07 PM, Andrew Halonen  wrote:
Hello Jason, 

It was great to catch up, and thank you for the contribution to my Cast Iron Keynote.  I wear the AI hat too, and companies are catching on to the idea of a Company GPT, a tool to find answers and create content from internal data.  Here’s what Forbes had to say in Why Every Business Needs Its Own GPT. 

Walther EMC has a tremendous amount of information that would be useful to many of you including Sales, Marketing, Engineering, Customer Service, HR, and training new people.   The goal is time-savings and it comes with quick answers, executive-level insight, and creating high quality content that needs minimal iterations to publish.

Customer Testimonials:
“We always knew the answer was here, but we could never find it.  With AmatriumGPT, we now have the answer and the source document.”

“The real power of AI is when it’s secured within a fence and using your own corporate data. AmatriumGPT is this platform, offering an easy-to-use system with unlimited seats and even has a built-in language translator.”

“The drawing and specs were in Korean, and we couldn’t quote the project until a colleague said, ‘put it into Amatrium.’  The AmatriumGPT Translator converted the language to English and Japanese, and then we could quote the job.”

“Amatrium, Inc has transformed how Waupaca Foundry approaches content creation and stakeholder engagement. Whether it's brainstorming blog posts, writing social media copy, or even dislodging writer's block, it’s like having an on-demand creative writer at our disposal. The tool helps us produce high-quality, tailored content in a fraction of the time, allowing us to focus on strategy and execution. Its ability to understand context and offer relevant suggestions has improved both our efficiency and the consistency of our messaging. For any marketer looking to streamline their workflow, Amatrium is a game-changer.”  - Sara Timm, Director of Marketing, Waupaca Foundry

How it works: https://youtu.be/VgCEbEGB1a0 

Let’s arrange a live demonstration on Teams, and follow it with a free 30-day trial.

Thank you,

Andrew Halonen
President
Amatrium, Inc
906.370.4984 mobile
www.amatrium.com

It’s time to build your Company GPT

AAMkAGFiMThhMWE4LTBiOTYtNDY2OS05NjE3LThk.body:
CAUTION: CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe. 

STN Webinar Series

 
  Thursday, February 26, 2026: 10am PT | 1pm ET

Running Transportation Dispatch & Payroll as One System

Student transportation leaders are being asked to do more with less—manage driver shortages, control labor costs, ensure on-time arrivals, and maintain payroll accuracy across increasingly complex operations. Yet many districts still rely on disconnected systems for GPS, routing, dispatch, and payroll, leaving gaps in visibility when it matters most.

In this webinar, hear directly from Little Elm Independent School District (TX) about how they successfully transitioned from a contractor-based model to a fully in-house transportation operation—and why unifying dispatch and payroll into a single command-and-control system was non-negotiable.﻿
﻿

Save Your Seat

 Jonquez Moore

Transportation Director

Little Elm Independent School District (TX)

 Jonathan Agenten

Sales Director

Bytecurve

 Bryan Mitchell

Marketing

Bytecurve

REGISTER NOW Sponsored by Bytecurve 

  STN Multimedia On Demand

Watch recorded webinars, listen to the School Transportation Nation podcast, and watch the STN Video Spotlight anytime and anywhere you have an internet connection—via computer or mobile device. Browse all presentations.

   STN eNews: February 18, 2026

 
February 2026: Keys to Success

Developing a safety culture not only begins and ends with school bus passengers but must encompass everything from driver training to garage layout.

Vote in the Monthly Poll

When was the last time your operation trained with local first responders on emergency scenarios?

Big headlines from Blue Bird, GreenPower & HopSkipDrive.

 School Transportation Nation Podcast

Boots to Buses: Military Formed Georgia Student Transportation Leader

 
Email ruth@stnmedia.com with pictures & info on how you ‘Love the Bus’ and you could be featured on our website or social media 🚌  
 Social Media

School Bus Fun Facts

Highlighting incredible statistics and facts, all made possible by the dedicated work of student transportation professionals throughout the years. Join the Conversation

  100% Buy America Requirement Proposed for EV Chargers

Transportation professionals are encouraged to share their perspectives on the potential impact of the increased domestic content requirement, including any challenges or benefits it may present for school bus electrification projects. Read More

 Event News

UPDATED: National School Bus Inspection Training Returns to STN EXPO East

This year, inspection training participants will also participate in a hands-on competition when identifying defects. They will be split into three groups, with the fastest one that identifies the most defects winning prizes. Read More

  Bitter Winter Weather Halts School Bus Operations in Parts of South, Mid-Atlantic

Transportation officials across the region emphasized that many school bus fleets in southern states are not equipped with snow tires or chains, equipment typically unnecessary given their usual climates. Read More

Sponsored

Safety Leadership - New Course Just Created

There is a lot of truth in the saying, there are no bad troops, only bad officers. This philosophy also applies to school bus leadership. The School Bus Safety Company (SBSC) has just completed a new course that trains all levels of management the techniques and practices to become great safety leaders. The course contains seven programs addressing every area of Safety Leadership. Watch this short video that explains the opportunity you have to operate a safely as possible. 

 Social Media

Blue Bird Acquires Micro Bird

Blue Bird Corporation has signed an agreement to acquire and consolidate Girardin Group’s stake in the 50/50 Micro Bird inc. joint venture. Read the article and share your thoughts

 8 Ways to Simplify and Streamline School Bus Operations

 Boosting K-12 Attendance With Innovative Transportation Solutions

 How to Automate Stop-Arm

Violation Detection

Partner Update sponsored by REI

  Event News

STN EXPO East to Feature Illegal Passing Trends, Safety Recommendations

This session will include a breakdown of the NHTSA toolkit for planning safety school bus stops and routes and their evaluation of the technology offerings that target illegal passing as well their effectiveness. Read More

  Superintendent of the Year Snapshot: Support, Understanding of Transportation

Lamar CISD in Texas relies heavily on technology to streamline operations, enhance communication and improve safety. When it comes to workplace culture, transportation encourages engagement and morale through constant communication. Read More

 Superintendent Snapshot: Communication, Collaboration Key for Maine District Success

“My advice to other superintendents is to intentionally invest in and publicly value their transportation teams,” Gorham School District Superintendent Dr. Heather Perry told STN. Read More

  Wire Report 

Florida School Board Disputes Long-Term Trauma Claims from Student Sexual Assault

The girl was riding a school bus carrying special-needs students of varying ages when she was assaulted by a 15-year-old “emotionally disturbed boy.” The school district attorneys argue the district should not be held financially responsible for years of therapy and specialized education. Read More

  Blog 

Autonomous Vehicle Implications

As we share roads with evolving AVs, collaboration between manufacturers, regulators and districts is crucial. Staying ahead of the curve ensures we don’t lag in safety. Read More

   You Don’t Need a Calendar On

Your Wall Anymore

Bookmark the online STN Calendar to stay updated on both virtual and in-person events for the school bus industry. Read More

2026 Buyer’s Guide

This “Ultimate Resource for the School Transportation Industry” gives you the latest vehicle production data, state student ridership and budget reports, industry trends and contact information for state, national and federal agencies, manufacturers, dealers and suppliers.

It's an invaluable resource that remains on everyone’s desk all year long.

Hello Jason!

ARI-hetra is headed back to Las Vegas for North America’s largest construction show and we’re bringing hydraulic-free, mobile lifting with us!

Visit us March 3-7 at CONEXPO-CON/AGG 2026 in the Festival Lot at booth #F27014 to explore the Battery-Powered Wireless Mobile Column Lift System, engineered with ball-screw technology to deliver smooth, synchronized lifting, continuous mechanical locking, reduced maintenance, and maximum uptime for construction fleet service operations. Configurable to lift vehicles up to 144,000 lbs. with a set of eight columns, this compact, wireless solution helps technicians work more efficiently without cords, hoses, or fixed infrastructure.

You’ll also see a full lineup of heavy-duty shop solutions designed to keep service moving, including transmission jacks, brake service tools, tire equipment, support stands, and self-evacuating oil drains, each built to reduce downtime and support safer, more productive maintenance environments.

Heading to CONEXPO? Visit ARI-hetra at booth #F27014 and experience heavy-duty shop solutions built to keep construction fleets working OR click below to explore solutions now!

EXPLORE HEAVY-DUTY SHOP SOLUTIONS
Have a great day,

Randy Tucker | Regional Sales Manager | 513-267-1843

AAMkAGFiMThhMWE4LTBiOTYtNDY2OS05NjE3LThk.body:
CAUTION: CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe. 

 🚨 STN EXPO East [Session Update]🚨 

March 26-31, Charlotte, NC 

 National School Bus Inspection Training (adds) Skills Competition + OEM Hands-On Experience

Learn More

This comprehensive training program includes 8-hours of classroom instruction with a written exam, and a second day of hands-on identification of defects on real school buses along with OEM training on a propane autogas system, diesel emissions, and high-voltage school bus electrification. Plus, a maintenance software tune-up with tech experts.

Attendees will gain valuable skills in early defect detection—helping fleets avoid expensive repairs, minimize vehicle downtime, and create a smoother, more efficient maintenance schedule. 

(New for 2026) A fun and challenging inspection competition! Participants will divide into teams and race to uncover intentionally hidden defects on mutiple school buses. The fastest team with the most accurate finds will take home exciting prizes courtesy of South Carolina DOE.

Leading OEMs—Blue Bird, IC Bus, RIDE, and Thomas Built Buses—will each deliver dedicated one-hour sessions sharing expert insights on inspecting key components specific to their school bus models.

﻿View the full agenda of STN Expo East educational sessions. 

Reserve My Spot in Charlotte Today (It takes 2-min to register for the main conference )

     Contact the STN events team at events@stnmedia.com

STN Media Group | 5334 Torrance Blvd. 3rd Fl. | Torrance, CA 90503 US

Does your firm need to hire the finest military professionals available?  ﻿Men and women who are self-starters and motivated? 

We’re hosting our next Cohen Partners Military Virtual Hiring Conference, designed to give our clients direct access to the highest-caliber military talent entering the civilian workforce within the next four months.

What:

A curated virtual hiring conference where we match your hiring needs with pre-vetted military candidates.

When: Thursday, March 12th, 2026

How it works:

Interview directly from your computerWe build your interview slate based on your specifications, including:Geographic preferencesCompensation rangeAcademic backgroundMilitary and civilian work experienceCost:

Free to participate. You only pay our standard 20% contingent fee after we help you successfully hire a candidate.

Why this is different from a career fairAll candidates are available within four months — no time wasted on candidates years away from transitioningEvery candidate is thoroughly vetted using military performance documentationWe represent top-performing officers and enlisted leaders only
Roles our candidates commonly fillOur candidates (both degreed and non-degreed) typically transition into roles in the following industries:

Nuclear PowerConsultingEngineeringLogistics / Supply Chain / Materials ManagementMaintenanceProgram & Project ManagementManufacturing SupervisionOperations LeadershipSalesQuality ControlSafetyTechnicians (Electrical, Mechanical, Electro-Mechanical, Electronics, HVAC)Information Technology

To participate, reply to this email or call me at 831-251-0358.

Signup deadline: Tuesday, March 3rd, 2026

Laura Maxwell

Corporate Account Executive

COHEN PARTNERS

Strategic Military Placement

831.251.0358

 
831-251-0358   | lmaxwell@cohen-partners.com | ﻿www.lm.cohen-partners.com

Cohen Partners | 4225 Oceanside Blvd., Ste. H #200 | Oceanside, CA 92056-3473 US

Jason,

 

Mark your calendar for the 2026 FTR Transportation Conference, taking place August 31 – September 3, 2026 in Indianapolis, IN.

 

Each year, the FTR Transportation Conference brings together leaders across trucking, rail, intermodal, equipment, finance, and supply chain sectors for four days of unbiased forecasts, strategic insight, and high-value networking. With 97% of attendees saying they would recommend the conference and 100% meeting their informational objectives, the event continues to set the standard for freight transportation intelligence.

 

Planning is already underway for 2026 — including keynote announcements, agenda releases, networking events, and early registration opportunities.

 

If you would like to be the first to know when:
• Speakers are announced
• The agenda is released
• Registration opens
• Hotel information becomes available

 

Sign-up now to receive the latest conference updates!

Sign up here! 

We look forward to welcoming you to Indianapolis for another year of clarity, connection, and actionable insight.

 

The FTR Conference Team
ftrconference.com

   

www.ftrintel.com, 1720 N Kinser Pike, Suite 210, Bloomington, IN 47404, USA, 888-988-1699

M&R is growing very fast which is good but we need to watch that they pay on-time. We have to hold shipments if they don’t. Also- Jason is cool but you need to watch him. He is your best friend but not loyal at all. We need to make him see the value of being a loyal partner. He was cut off from Stemco as you know. Loyalty gives him access to capacity (he will always be able to get product when he needs and we will expedite for him). There is a lot of value here.

 

I have not been able to sell him on this but I haven’t pushed it yet…thought I had more time. This will be your challenge unfortunately. It’s not a hot priority right now, it’s more of a face-to-face, build the relationship thing. This account will grow significantly this year. Just make sure he pays.

 

This is all free advice…you get what you pay for right. Just trying to help ya here.

February 19, 2026 

Model C2 Software Update 3.1 is Here: Smarter Navigation, Global Language Support & More February 2026 

  Model C2 Software UpdateVersion 3.1 is Now Available!  February 2026 Newsletter

    What's New in Version 3.1Building on the foundation of Version 3.0, this update delivers improvements across navigation, safety, fleet management, and usability — driven directly by customer feedback from real-world deployments.

  Plus, 5 new language options for our onboard interface:

🇪🇸 

🇫🇷 

🇩🇪 

🇰🇷 

🇯🇵 

Spanish

French

German

Korean

Japanese

  Route Management & Recovery:AMRs can now resume route after interruption or navigation failure

“Go to Next Waypoint” screen now shows description of upcoming Waypoint

Routes preserve display order from Cloud Connect and can be manually reordered

  Greater Zone Intelligence:Automatic Door zones detect and wait for doors to open before proceeding

Added Sound zones and Ramp/Slope zones for facility-aware navigation

Ability to navigate out of Keep Out zones for improved edge-case recovery

  Path Planning & Mapping:Upgraded path validation logic reduces unnecessary replanning during navigation

Enhanced ramp and slope handling delivers smoother approaches and safer traversal.

Map Graph display now visible during Area Mapping for easier map-building

  Safety & Diagnostics:Support for four additional ToF sensors at floor level of Model C2 Large & PartPorter

New LiDAR diagnostics screen for live monitoring directly from cart UI

Integrated navigation lockout while charging prevents unintended movement

  For a full list of features & improvements, check out the:

__Press Release__  Update Remotely at Any Time:Access Settings → About → Online Update from the cart interface to initiate the version transition process.

      Still moving materials manually?If your teams are still pushing carts or driving forklifts to keep production moving, you're absorbing unnecessary cost and risk. The Model C2 automates that internal transport with simple deployment and measurable ROI. 

    Redirect labor to higher-value tasks

Free your team from repetitive material runs and let them focus on production and quality.

Reduce handling risks

Fewer manual transport trips means fewer opportunities for strain injuries, collisions, and material damage.

Scale without infrastructure changes
Deploy and reconfigure routes as your needs evolve — no tracks, no floor modifications, no construction downtime.

See results from day one
With setup in under an hour and no training required, your team can start benefiting immediately.

    See how the Model C2 can streamline your daily logistics — book a live virtual demo:

__Book a Demo__    Want to Meet C2 In-Person?  Find us on the show floor to see the C2 in action, ask questions, and get a hands-on feel for our AMRs:

Hi Jason
#JXH266535  |  0 points as of 02/19/2026   | Account     
      Take advantage of this limited time offer to celebrate Choice Privileges' all-new rewards experience - it's a better time than ever to apply for the Choice Privileges® Mastercard®. Our program enhancements now allow members to accelerate their progress to Elite status and rewards with card spend. With this offer, new cardholders will get an even faster track to earn their next getaway.   APPLY TODAY AND EARN40,000 60,000 Bonus Points after you spend $1,000 in purchases
in the first 3 months of account opening.1
That's worth up to 7 reward nights at select
Choice Hotels® properties worldwide! Additional fees and terms may apply. $0 Annual Fee Important credit terms Learn More Offers may differ from time to time and depend on the marketing channel,such as phone, email, online, direct mail, or in branch. You must select
Learn More to take advantage of this offer.       Turn Your Everyday Spending 
into Reward Nights Faster   UP TO 16X TOTAL POINTS
at participating Choice Hotels® properties2   3X POINTS
on purchases at gas stations, grocery stores, home improvement stores and phone plan services 3   1X POINTS
on other purchases3   Benefits and Features
that Go Further  AUTOMATIC GOLD ELITE STATUS
Receive 10 Elite Qualifying Nights each year—guaranteeing you Gold Elite status or helping you reach the next level faster.4 With Gold Elite status, you'll earn 10% more points on stays, plus enjoy perks like welcome gifts, late checkout and more.2   NEW! FAST TRACK YOUR STATUS AND REWARDS
Upgrade to higher status and earn milestone rewards faster with points earned using your card on everyday purchases.5   MASTERCARD® WORLD ELITE BENEFITS
Get access to additional security benefits, exclusive offers, concierge, travel & lifestyle services and more.6   NO FOREIGN TRANSACTION FEE
When you use your card for travel, you won't pay a foreign currency conversion fee for purchases converted to U.S. dollars.   CELLULAR TELEPHONE PROTECTION
Get up to $800 of cell phone protection against damage, theft, or unintended separation. Subject to a $25 deductible.7  $0 Annual Fee Important Credit Terms Learn More 
Other things you need to know:

Offers may differ from time to time and depend on the marketing channel, such as phone, email, online, direct mail, or in branch. You must select Learn More to take advantage of this offer.

Refer to the Choice Privileges® Mastercard® Credit Card Account Agreement and the Choice Privileges® World Elite Mastercard® Credit Card Guide to Benefits to learn more.

This offer is not a guarantee of credit. Credit card is subject to credit qualification.

You may not be eligible for the introductory bonus rewards offer if you opened a Choice Privileges Mastercard Credit Card or a Choice Privileges Select Mastercard Credit Card within the last 15 months from the date of this application, even if that account is closed and has a $0 balance 

You may not be eligible for a Choice Mastercard product if you currently have an open Choice Privileges or Choice Privileges Select Mastercard account.

1To qualify for the 60,000 bonus points, a total of at least $1,000 in net purchases (purchases minus returns/credits) must post to your account within 3 months from the date your account is opened. The bonus rewards points will show as redeemable within 1 – 2 billing periods after they are earned. “Purchases” that do not apply to this offer and do not earn rewards points include: cash advances and equivalents of any kind (such as ATM charges, cash advances (including overdraft protection advance), traveler’s checks, money orders, pre-paid gift cards, peer-to-peer payments, and wire transfers); balance transfers; disputes, illegal actions, and violations (such as disputed or illegal purchases or purchases that violate the terms of your agreements); fees and interest of any kind (such as late fees, returned payment fees, monthly or annual fees); gambling transactions of any kind (such as bets or wagers transmitted over the internet, casino gaming chips, lottery tickets or off-track wagers). Cash advances and balance transfers may affect the credit line available for this offer. Refer to the Choice Privileges® Mastercard® Credit Card Rewards Program Agreement (the “Card Rewards Program”) Terms and Conditions (“Terms”) for more information about the rewards program.

2Additional points received under the Choice Privileges® Rewards Program for stays at hotels participating in the Choice Privileges Rewards Program will be earned in accordance with the rules and regulations of the Choice Privileges Rewards Program. You must have an Elite Gold Membership to earn the 10% point earn bonus on base Choice Privileges points (on the amount of Choice Privileges points earned on a point-eligible stay). WoodSpring Suites® properties do not participate. Eligible stays earn Choice Privileges members 10 points for each whole U.S. dollar when paying qualifying room rates, excluding taxes, restaurant, or other incidental charges. Stays at MainStay Suites®, Suburban Studios®, and Everhome Suites® earn 10 points for each whole U.S dollar spent on eligible stays of 6 nights or fewer, and earn 5 points for each whole U.S. dollar spent on eligible stays of 7+ nights, with a maximum of 30 total nights. Most hotels are independently owned and operated. Refer to the Choice Privileges® Rules and Regulations for additional details at choicehotels.com/choice-privileges/rules-regulations.

3Five points (1 base point plus 4 bonus points) per $1 spent on qualifying stays made on the Card Account at eligible Choice hotel locations. Qualifying Choice Hotel stays are items billed by eligible Choice brand hotels as merchant of record booked through either choicehotels.com or directly with an eligible Choice hotel, or by calling the Choice Hotels Service Center. Three points (1 base point plus 2 bonus points) are earned per $1 spent on net purchases at retailers whose Mastercard merchant code is classified as: Gas: gas stations, automated fuel dispensers, and electric vehicle charging stations. Groceries: grocery stores, supermarkets, delis and bakeries. Telephone plans: landline and cell phone providers considered telecommunication services. Home improvement: home improvement stores, hardware stores, nurseries and garden supply stores, furniture and household appliances stores, home accessories stores, floor, glass, paint and wallpaper stores, lease/rental services for furniture and household appliances, swimming pool sales and supplies, contractors, or specialists providing home improvement services. Purchases not processed using the merchant codes for Mastercard mentioned above will not qualify for bonus points. Wells Fargo does not have the ability to control how a retailer chooses to classify their business and, therefore, reserves the right to determine which purchases qualify for bonus points. You may not earn bonus points for purchases made through third-party payment accounts, at online marketplaces, with retailers who submit purchases using a mobile or wireless card reader, or if you use a mobile or digital wallet. Other purchases: 1 rewards point will be earned per $1 spent on other net purchases. . Cash advances and cash equivalents of any kind do not earn points. For a detailed list of cash advance and cash equivalent exclusions and merchant examples that may not be billed under the Mastercard category codes listed above, refer to the Choice Privileges® Mastercard® Credit Card Rewards Program Agreement (the “Card Rewards Program”) Terms and Conditions (“Terms”) for details.

4To be eligible to receive the 10 Elite Qualifying Nights with your card account, you must have an active Choice Privileges Rewards Program Member account linked to your card account. The 10 Elite Qualifying Nights will be available in your Choice Privileges Rewards Program member account within 14 days upon opening your card account. Within 14 days of January 1 of each subsequent calendar year, the 10 Elite Qualifying Nights will be available in your Choice Privileges Rewards Program account, as long as your card account is open as of December 31 of the prior calendar year. Refer to the Choice Privileges Rewards Program Rules and Regulations at choicehotels.com/choice-privileges/rules-regulations for details.

5Milestone Rewards are earned by reaching the Milestone Qualifying Nights (MQNs) or the Milestone Qualifying Credits (MQCs) thresholds during a calendar year. MQNs are the sum of the Choice Privileges Rewards Program member’s (a “Member”) Choice Privileges Points-Eligible nights. Choice Privileges may also award additional MQNs to a Member as part of certain specific promotions or rewards. Bonus Elite Qualifying Nights(EQNs) awarded by having a Choice Privileges Mastercard are excluded from (do not count toward) MQNs. MQCs are the sum of the Stay Base Points earned from Points-Eligible Stays and the points earned from the spend on a Choice Privileges Mastercard. Choice Privileges may also award additional MQCs as part of certain specific promotions or rewards. Note that not all Choice Privileges points earned count toward MQCs. Elite Stay Bonus Points (such as the bonus percentage/point bonus points an Elite Member earns on a Points-Eligible Stay) do not count toward MQCs. Bonus Choice Privileges points earned through most promotional offers such as national promotions, limited-time cardholder offers, and cardholder anniversary bonuses also do not count towards MQCs. 

Members can earn Elite status (beyond the level already awarded by the Choice Privileges Mastercard) by reaching the EQNs or the Elite Qualifying Credits (EQCs) thresholds for the next Elite status during the calendar year. EQNs are the sum of a Member’s Points-Eligible nights, bonus nights awarded by having a Choice Privileges Mastercard, Choice Privileges Reward Nights, and certain other qualifying nights. The Program may also award additional EQNs to a Member as part of specific promotions or rewards. EQCs are the sum of the Choice Privileges Stay Base Points earned from Points-Eligible Stays and points earned from spend on a Member’s Choice Privileges Mastercard. The Program may also award EQCs to a Member as part of specific promotions or rewards. Not all points earned count toward EQCs. Elite Stay Bonus Points do not count towards EQCs. Choice Privileges points earned through most promotional offers such as national promotions, limited-time cardholder offers, and cardholder anniversary bonuses also do not count toward EQCs. Unless otherwise specified, the only way to earn EQCs is through completing Points-Eligible Stays or completing qualified spend on a Choice Privileges Mastercard. For Milestone Rewards Levels, Elite Status Levels and associated rewards options, and full terms, see the Choice Privileges Rules & Regulations.

6Certain terms, conditions and exclusions apply. Please refer to your Guide to Benefits for details.

7Cellular Telephone Protection can reimburse the Choice Privileges® Mastercard® cardholder for damage to, theft of, or involuntary and accidental parting of a cell phone. Reimbursement is limited to the cost to repair or replace your original cell phone, less a $25 deductible with an allowable maximum of two paid claims per 12-month period. Each approved claim has a benefit limit of $800. This benefit does not cover cell phones that are lost (i.e., mysteriously disappear). Electronic failure or issues related to the software of the device are not covered. This protection is only available when cell phone bills are paid with a Choice Privileges® Mastercard®. Coverage begins the first day of the calendar month following the payment of your first cell phone billing using your Choice Privileges® Mastercard® and remains in effect when you continue to charge your total monthly cell phone bill to Choice Privileges® Mastercard®. Eligible cell phones are the lines listed on your most recent cellular wireless service provider’s monthly billing statement for the billing cycle prior to when the incident occurred. This is supplemental coverage not otherwise covered by another insurance policy, may be applied after all other insurance is exhausted and may not be equivalent to or better than other applicable coverage. For complete coverage benefits and exclusions regarding this protection, refer to the Guide to Benefits.

Note: 
Call your cellular provider (or sign on to their website) and request to set up automatic payments. Setting up automatic payments is not required to qualify for Cellular Telephone Protection; however, you need to pay your monthly cell phone bill with your Choice Privileges® Mastercard® to get up to $800 protection.

Hi Bob,

 

I assumed that your validation requirements would be more stringent. If it helps, we have not heard of a single KIC failure with a 25K structural, 23K brake rating.

 

HALDEX: GG4692OEDQ2N
Section thickness. Can you send me a model asap of your Hub and Drum and I will compare?
Correct on-highway ratings. Typically for steerable trailers, lowbeds, and some times for boosters. If it gets used off-highway, then it would be just when moving into a construction site as slow speeds to load/unload.
 

 

Thank you!

 

 

Brandon Hayter

Director of Products
