Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>; Brian Farquhar purchasing@kgi.ca>; Collin Shore cshore@kgi.ca>
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub 

 

Brandon,

 

I believe our validation requirements are more stringent than those of KIC.  We will find out later this year when we test a KIC hub to our requirements.

 

Could you provide a little more detail on the brakes? 

What brake manufacturer and friction material do you use?How do the section thicknesses of our drum compare to the KIC drum? 

One last question, the 23K brake and 25K axle ratings are on-highway ratings, not off-highway ratings, correct?

 

Thank you,

direct 937-743-8132 | main 937.743.8125 ext.615 | fax 937.743.8232

Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

Thanks for the information, Brandon. Please get me the total count of hub & drum assemblies and loose hubs (by part number). I’ll issue an RMA.

 

From: Brandon Hayter bhayter@kgi.ca> 
Sent: Friday, February 13, 2026 1:53 PM
To: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com>; Kevin Langley klangley@kgi.ca>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>; Bob Klein BKlein@waltheremc.com>; Brian Farquhar purchasing@kgi.ca>; Collin Shore cshore@kgi.ca>
Subject: Re: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

Hi guys, thanks for looking at this.

 

This is a concern, as our brakes are rated for 23K and the axles are rated for 25K structurally.

 

For reference, we did a comparison with your hub and the older 25K KIC rated one.

 

Your hub was thicker throughout the cross-section, the flange, and had additional stiffeners. Plus you have the higher grade proprietary material.

 

I understand you have to do your due diligence, I just wanted to provide a reference to the previous competition.

 

As an aside, we should look into the options for returning the hub and drum + hub assemblies.

 

Let us know, thanks again.

 

 

Brandon Hayter

Director of Products

P. 250.499.2492   Ext. 106
www.kgi.ca
725 Hwy 3, Cawston, BC, V0X 1C3

 

 

 

 

From: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com>
Sent: Friday, February 13, 2026 10:41 AM
To: Kevin Langley klangley@kgi.ca>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>; Bob Klein BKlein@waltheremc.com>; Brian Farquhar purchasing@kgi.ca>; Collin Shore cshore@kgi.ca>
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

Kevin, Bob walked me thru the technical challenges. The hub was originally designed for 20k. Material was removed to reduce weight. In order to be sure we can uprate to 23k, we will have to thorough testing and we have tests in front of this that need to be done first.

 

I had hoped the hub was overdesigned and we could uprate without a lot of testing but that is not the case.

 

Regards,

Jason

 

 

From: Jason Heath
Sent: Friday, February 13, 2026 12:11 PM
To: 'Kevin Langley' klangley@kgi.ca>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>; Bob Klein BKlein@waltheremc.com>; Brian Farquhar purchasing@kgi.ca>; Collin Shore cshore@kgi.ca>
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

Kevin, we are working internally to see what can be done about  up-rating. I think you should procure alternate hubs for these builds  (or at least get them on order if you can cancel them). It’s not looking likely that Walther will be able to up-rate these hubs without more time/testing.

 

Question came up about the drum. 03-00050 is rated to 18k. Is that sufficient?

 

Thanks,
Jason

We use about 200-300 units per year.  If we cannot get the updated load rating, then there are 4 builds that would potentially go on hold within the next 2-3 weeks.  
What is your estimated timeline for the engineering analysis to be complete?

 

Thanks, 

 

Kevin Langley
Product Design Engineer

From: Jason Heath
Sent: Thursday, February 12, 2026 8:29 AM
To: 'Kevin Langley' klangley@kgi.ca>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>; Bob Klein BKlein@waltheremc.com>; purchasing@kgi.ca
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

Hi Kevin, cc’ing Bob Klein (Walther Engineering Manager) and Brian Farquhar.

 

Today we spoke about your issue in our huddle meeting with the Engineering team. The hub itself appears to be strong as it passes our internal testing with high cycle counts. It is possible/likely they will pass testing at 23k loads but Engineering will need to test to verify before it can increase the rating.

 

I apologize again for the inconvenience. I know you need at least 23k rating for this build and I won’t be able to help you in time. Do you have other options? I might be able to direct you to another source.

 

Do you have 20k applications where you can use up the current inventory or will you need to return?

 

Thank you,

Jason

 

____________________________________________

Jason Heath  |  Director, OEM & Fleet Sales

3501 Shotwell Drive | Franklin, Ohio 45005 United States

cell 616.914.2665 | fax 937.743.8232

jheath@waltheremc.com | www.waltheremc.com

Sent: Tuesday, February 10, 2026 3:38 PM
To: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>
Subject: Re: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

You don't often get email from klangley@kgi.ca. Learn why this is important

Hi Jason,

 

The hubs were purchased as a direct replacement without considering the load rating.  We have some on hand now for a job and 54 more on order.  These are all currently being used in a dual wheel setup, so the offset rating is not critical at the moment.  However, we would need the load rating increased to a minimum of 23k for us to comfortably use them, with 25k rating preferred.  

 

Thanks, 

 

Kevin Langley
Product Design Engineer 

P. 250.499.2492   
www.kgi.ca
725 Hwy 3, Cawston, BC, V0X 1C3

 

 

From: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com>
Sent: Tuesday, February 10, 2026 11:15 AM
To: Kevin Langley klangley@kgi.ca>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

Apologies Kevin! I did not know KIC had higher ratings for this hub. Let me reach out to our engineering team to see why it is only rated for 20k applications. Our other N spindles are rated for 23k applications (and permit off/insets). We have a severe duty version rated for 25k and allows for offsets. Unfortunately these are 10 bolt hubs, not 8.

 

What is the status, did you end up purchasing these hubs and now you cannot use? Please advise. I will help if this is the case.

 

Regards,

Jason

Hello Jason, 

 

It was recently brought to engineering attention that the Walther hub assembly 10-00048-101, which was supplied as a direct replacement for KIC hub assembly 00133LT, does not have the same dynamic load rating.  I have attached both load tables for reference.  

 

For this hub to be considered a suitable replacement part, we would need to match the 25,000 lb GAW (12,500 lb/hub) rating that was previously used.  We would also prefer to have a derated load value for the wheel offsets instead of the prohibited note.  

 

Can you please confirm that:

 

The attached table is the most recent version. If the load rating can be updated to match what KIC had offered.  

 

Thanks, 

 

Kevin Langley
Product Design Engineer 

P. 250.499.2492   
www.kgi.ca
725 Hwy 3, Cawston, BC, V0X 1C3

STN Webinar Series

 
  Thursday, February 19, 2026: 11am PT | 2pm ET

3 Student-Centric Transportation Strategies to Reduce Chronic Absenteeism

Chronic absenteeism remains a significant challenge for school districts, even as student transportation needs continue to grow more complex.

In this session, transportation expert Greg Jackson and Director of Pupil Transportation Jim Ellis will discuss three student-centered transportation strategies districts are using to help support consistent attendance in a rapidly shifting landscape.

Plus: Live Q&A – bring your toughest questions!﻿
﻿

Watch Now

 Greg Jackson

General Manager, Busing

EverDriven

 Jim Ellis

Director of Pupil Transportation

Henrico County Public Schools

From: Brandon Hayter bhayter@kgi.ca> 
Sent: Friday, February 13, 2026 5:09 PM
To: Bob Klein BKlein@waltheremc.com>; Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com>; Kevin Langley klangley@kgi.ca>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>; Brian Farquhar purchasing@kgi.ca>; Collin Shore cshore@kgi.ca>
Subject: Re: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

Hi Bob,

 

I assumed that your validation requirements would be more stringent. If it helps, we have not heard of a single KIC failure with a 25K structural, 23K brake rating.

 

HALDEX: GG4692OEDQ2N
Section thickness. Can you send me a model asap of your Hub and Drum and I will compare?
Correct on-highway ratings. Typically for steerable trailers, lowbeds, and some times for boosters. If it gets used off-highway, then it would be just when moving into a construction site as slow speeds to load/unload.
 

 

Thank you!

 

 

Brandon Hayter

Director of Products

P. 250.499.2492   Ext. 106
www.kgi.ca
725 Hwy 3, Cawston, BC, V0X 1C3

 

 

 

From: Bob Klein BKlein@waltheremc.com>
Sent: Friday, February 13, 2026 11:54 AM
To: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com>; Brandon Hayter bhayter@kgi.ca>; Kevin Langley klangley@kgi.ca>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>; Brian Farquhar purchasing@kgi.ca>; Collin Shore cshore@kgi.ca>
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub 

 

Brandon,

 

I believe our validation requirements are more stringent than those of KIC.  We will find out later this year when we test a KIC hub to our requirements.

 

Could you provide a little more detail on the brakes? 

What brake manufacturer and friction material do you use?How do the section thicknesses of our drum compare to the KIC drum? 

One last question, the 23K brake and 25K axle ratings are on-highway ratings, not off-highway ratings, correct?

 

Thank you,

_____________________________________________

Bob Klein  |  Engineering Manager

3501 Shotwell Drive | Franklin, Ohio 45005 United States

direct 937-743-8132 | main 937.743.8125 ext.615 | fax 937.743.8232

bklein@waltheremc.com | www.waltheremc.com

 

From: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com> 
Sent: Friday, February 13, 2026 2:26 PM
To: Brandon Hayter bhayter@kgi.ca>; Kevin Langley klangley@kgi.ca>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>; Bob Klein BKlein@waltheremc.com>; Brian Farquhar purchasing@kgi.ca>; Collin Shore cshore@kgi.ca>
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

Thanks for the information, Brandon. Please get me the total count of hub & drum assemblies and loose hubs (by part number). I’ll issue an RMA.

 

From: Brandon Hayter bhayter@kgi.ca> 
Sent: Friday, February 13, 2026 1:53 PM
To: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com>; Kevin Langley klangley@kgi.ca>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>; Bob Klein BKlein@waltheremc.com>; Brian Farquhar purchasing@kgi.ca>; Collin Shore cshore@kgi.ca>
Subject: Re: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

Hi guys, thanks for looking at this.

 

This is a concern, as our brakes are rated for 23K and the axles are rated for 25K structurally.

 

For reference, we did a comparison with your hub and the older 25K KIC rated one.

 

Your hub was thicker throughout the cross-section, the flange, and had additional stiffeners. Plus you have the higher grade proprietary material.

 

I understand you have to do your due diligence, I just wanted to provide a reference to the previous competition.

 

As an aside, we should look into the options for returning the hub and drum + hub assemblies.

 

Let us know, thanks again.

 

 

Brandon Hayter

Director of Products

P. 250.499.2492   Ext. 106
www.kgi.ca
725 Hwy 3, Cawston, BC, V0X 1C3

 

 

 

 

From: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com>
Sent: Friday, February 13, 2026 10:41 AM
To: Kevin Langley klangley@kgi.ca>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>; Bob Klein BKlein@waltheremc.com>; Brian Farquhar purchasing@kgi.ca>; Collin Shore cshore@kgi.ca>
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

Kevin, Bob walked me thru the technical challenges. The hub was originally designed for 20k. Material was removed to reduce weight. In order to be sure we can uprate to 23k, we will have to thorough testing and we have tests in front of this that need to be done first.

 

I had hoped the hub was overdesigned and we could uprate without a lot of testing but that is not the case.

 

Regards,

Jason

 

 

From: Jason Heath
Sent: Friday, February 13, 2026 12:11 PM
To: 'Kevin Langley' klangley@kgi.ca>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>; Bob Klein BKlein@waltheremc.com>; Brian Farquhar purchasing@kgi.ca>; Collin Shore cshore@kgi.ca>
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

Kevin, we are working internally to see what can be done about  up-rating. I think you should procure alternate hubs for these builds  (or at least get them on order if you can cancel them). It’s not looking likely that Walther will be able to up-rate these hubs without more time/testing.

 

Question came up about the drum. 03-00050 is rated to 18k. Is that sufficient?

 

Thanks,
Jason

 

From: Kevin Langley klangley@kgi.ca>
Sent: Thursday, February 12, 2026 12:56 PM
To: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>; Bob Klein BKlein@waltheremc.com>; Brian Farquhar purchasing@kgi.ca>; Collin Shore cshore@kgi.ca>
Subject: Re: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

Hi Jason,

 

We use about 200-300 units per year.  If we cannot get the updated load rating, then there are 4 builds that would potentially go on hold within the next 2-3 weeks.  
What is your estimated timeline for the engineering analysis to be complete?

 

Thanks, 

 

Kevin Langley
Product Design Engineer 

P. 250.499.2492   
www.kgi.ca
725 Hwy 3, Cawston, BC, V0X 1C3

 

 

 

From: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com>
Sent: Thursday, February 12, 2026 5:55 AM
To: Kevin Langley klangley@kgi.ca>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>; Bob Klein BKlein@waltheremc.com>; Brian Farquhar purchasing@kgi.ca>
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

Kevin, what’s your rough usage annually on this hub?

 

From: Jason Heath
Sent: Thursday, February 12, 2026 8:29 AM
To: 'Kevin Langley' klangley@kgi.ca>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>; Bob Klein BKlein@waltheremc.com>; purchasing@kgi.ca
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

Hi Kevin, cc’ing Bob Klein (Walther Engineering Manager) and Brian Farquhar.

 

Today we spoke about your issue in our huddle meeting with the Engineering team. The hub itself appears to be strong as it passes our internal testing with high cycle counts. It is possible/likely they will pass testing at 23k loads but Engineering will need to test to verify before it can increase the rating.

 

I apologize again for the inconvenience. I know you need at least 23k rating for this build and I won’t be able to help you in time. Do you have other options? I might be able to direct you to another source.

 

Do you have 20k applications where you can use up the current inventory or will you need to return?

 

Thank you,

Jason

 

____________________________________________

Jason Heath  |  Director, OEM & Fleet Sales

3501 Shotwell Drive | Franklin, Ohio 45005 United States

cell 616.914.2665 | fax 937.743.8232

jheath@waltheremc.com | www.waltheremc.com

 

 

 

From: Kevin Langley klangley@kgi.ca>
Sent: Tuesday, February 10, 2026 3:38 PM
To: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>
Subject: Re: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

You don't often get email from klangley@kgi.ca. Learn why this is important

Hi Jason,

 

The hubs were purchased as a direct replacement without considering the load rating.  We have some on hand now for a job and 54 more on order.  These are all currently being used in a dual wheel setup, so the offset rating is not critical at the moment.  However, we would need the load rating increased to a minimum of 23k for us to comfortably use them, with 25k rating preferred.  

 

Thanks, 

 

Kevin Langley
Product Design Engineer 

P. 250.499.2492   
www.kgi.ca
725 Hwy 3, Cawston, BC, V0X 1C3

 

 

From: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com>
Sent: Tuesday, February 10, 2026 11:15 AM
To: Kevin Langley klangley@kgi.ca>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>
Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

Apologies Kevin! I did not know KIC had higher ratings for this hub. Let me reach out to our engineering team to see why it is only rated for 20k applications. Our other N spindles are rated for 23k applications (and permit off/insets). We have a severe duty version rated for 25k and allows for offsets. Unfortunately these are 10 bolt hubs, not 8.

 

What is the status, did you end up purchasing these hubs and now you cannot use? Please advise. I will help if this is the case.

 

Regards,

Jason

 

From: Kevin Langley klangley@kgi.ca>
Sent: Tuesday, February 10, 2026 1:59 PM
To: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com>
Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>
Subject: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

You don't often get email from klangley@kgi.ca. Learn why this is important

 

CAUTION: CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe.

Hello Jason, 

 

It was recently brought to engineering attention that the Walther hub assembly 10-00048-101, which was supplied as a direct replacement for KIC hub assembly 00133LT, does not have the same dynamic load rating.  I have attached both load tables for reference.  

 

For this hub to be considered a suitable replacement part, we would need to match the 25,000 lb GAW (12,500 lb/hub) rating that was previously used.  We would also prefer to have a derated load value for the wheel offsets instead of the prohibited note.  

 

Can you please confirm that:

 

The attached table is the most recent version. If the load rating can be updated to match what KIC had offered.  

 

Thanks, 

 

Kevin Langley
Product Design Engineer 

P. 250.499.2492   
www.kgi.ca
725 Hwy 3, Cawston, BC, V0X 1C3

Cc: KGI Engineering engineering@kgi.ca>
Subject: EXTERNAL - 10-00048-101 Walther vs KIC hub

 

You don't often get email from klangley@kgi.ca. Learn why this is important

 

CAUTION: CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe.

Hello Jason, 

 

It was recently brought to engineering attention that the Walther hub assembly 10-00048-101, which was supplied as a direct replacement for KIC hub assembly 00133LT, does not have the same dynamic load rating.  I have attached both load tables for reference.  

 

For this hub to be considered a suitable replacement part, we would need to match the 25,000 lb GAW (12,500 lb/hub) rating that was previously used.  We would also prefer to have a derated load value for the wheel offsets instead of the prohibited note.  

 

Can you please confirm that:

 

The attached table is the most recent version. If the load rating can be updated to match what KIC had offered.  

 

Thanks, 

 

Kevin Langley
Product Design Engineer 

P. 250.499.2492   
www.kgi.ca
725 Hwy 3, Cawston, BC, V0X 1C3

Overview of the webinar: 

In today’s fast-paced and data-driven world, efficiency, accuracy, and innovation are more critical than ever. Introduction to ChatGPT and its integration with ExcelAutomating repetitive tasks in Excel with AIGenerating complex formulas and functions using ChatGPTReal-world applications and case studiesWho will benefit:

AAMkAGFiMThhMWE4LTBiOTYtNDY2OS05NjE3LThk.body:
CAUTION: CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe. 

Most B2B marketing fails because it copy-pastes whatever was hot last quarter instead of building a unique POV.  ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ 
February 20, 2026   |   Read Online

 

Powered by Reel Axis

Welcome back to Marketing Qualified! Here’s what we’re talking about this week:

Stop copy-pasting B2B trends 

The AI positioning sprint: stand out fast 

  👯 Stop copy-pasting B2B trendsYou don’t need more trends. You need fewer bad habits. Most B2B marketing fails because it copy-pastes whatever was hot last quarter instead of building a unique POV. That’s how you end up with AI-flavored blog sludge, generic webinars, and ad creative that looks like it was generated in a hurry by twelve committees. 

Look at Cloudflare. Their content isn’t “10 SEO tips.” It’s internet-level data from Cloudflare Radar, DDoS reports, and bot insights that only they can publish. It earns links, press, and trust because it’s proprietary and actually useful. Notion does something similar on the product side: opinionated templates and tutorials that make the product the content. They don’t chase formats for the sake of it. They ship stuff people use. 

The fix is simple: build marketing that can’t be easily copied. Here’s how to stop the trend-chasing treadmill: 

Inventory your unique data: customer usage patterns, aggregated benchmarks, anonymized performance insights. Turn that into recurring reports and briefings. 

Pick one distribution moat: email, LinkedIn, or a partner channel. Then design content to win that channel first. 

Create a sharp POV: write a one-page “What we believe about X” and run all copy through it. If your stance can’t be argued, it isn’t a stance. 

Productize content: templates, calculators, diagnostic checklists. If it doesn’t help someone do a job, cut it. 

Measure buying signals, not vanity: replies, demo intent, repeat consumption from accounts. Stop optimizing for views. 

Publish on a cadence you can maintain: monthly flagship report, weekly practical piece. Consistency beats novelty. 

Trends change. Moats don’t. Make the thing only you can make, and the algorithm will eventually catch up. 

Memorable takeaway: Specific beats trendy, every single time. 

  ✨ Supercharge Your AI Development with NarrowAI teams, developers, and product innovators… are you ready to build smarter and ship faster? Narrow is an automated prompt engineering and optimization platform that takes the guesswork out of working with large language models. Instead of spending hours writing, testing, and tuning prompts, Narrow does it for you — generating high-quality prompts, testing them across models, and continuously optimizing for performance, cost, and speed. 

What you’ll love:
💬 Automatically generate and refine expert-level prompts
🧪 Test and compare performance across multiple LLMs
📉 Reduce AI costs by up to 95% with smarter model selection
⏰ Deploy AI features up to 10× faster than manual workflows 

Whether you’re building AI-powered products, scaling LLM features, or just want to get more value from your AI stack — Narrow streamlines your workflow so you can focus on innovation, not iteration. Learn more and book a demo today at getnarrow.ai

  🗞️  In the news this week.🤖 OpenAI starts testing ads inside ChatGPT: agencies line up and brands weigh the risks 

💡 Google releases February 2026 Discover core update: expect volatility and fewer self-promotional listicles 

📹 Meta tests a standalone AI video app while Facebook rolls out AI-generated profile videos 

🤳 YouTube expands auto-dubbing and muted auto captions: creators get new tools to scale global content 

💻 HubSpot drops an AEO audit guide: how answer engine optimization is reshaping SEO in 2026 

  🏃‍♂️‍➡️ The AI positioning sprint: stand out fast in a crowded marketIf your homepage says "AI-powered platform", you're invisible. Everyone sells AI now. You stand out by tying AI to one painful outcome and making it stupidly easy to experience.

Before: "AI for observability." After: "Spot revenue-impacting anomalies before your CFO does." 

Before: "AI-driven ABM." After: "Find in-market accounts doing research today and route them to reps in under 24 hours." 

Before: "Website chatbot." After: "An AI bot that books qualified meetings on your pricing page." 

Here’s the tactical sprint:

Pick one high-value micro-moment. Identify a narrow, expensive failure your buyer hates. Example: 6sense leans into "in-market intent" as the moment that matters. 

Build a contrast message. Use Who + Pain + Outcome + Time. Keep it short and test it against competitor pages. 

Ship a lightweight AI demo. Datadog wins with product-led demos. Give buyers a sandbox, an anomaly simulator, or a live intent heatmap they can click without a login. 

Create a single conversion offer tied to that moment. Drift’s conversational playbooks convert because they route people to the right next step. Offer an "AI audit" or "intent shortlist" delivered in 24 hours. 

Personalize at two layers. Industry layer: language and examples. Persona layer: CFO vs Ops outcomes. Automate with rules, not just models, so it’s consistent. 

Differentiation is the result of specificity plus proof. Show one outcome, let buyers test it fast, and remove every wobble between curiosity and evidence. 

Memorable takeaway: If your AI pitch could be copy-pasted to a competitor, it’s not positioning. It’s wallpaper.

AAMkAGFiMThhMWE4LTBiOTYtNDY2OS05NjE3LThk.subject: Bendix–WEMC  Meeting Minutes — February 18, 2026
AAMkAGFiMThhMWE4LTBiOTYtNDY2OS05NjE3LThk.body:
Below are the meeting minutes from the Bendix–Walther meeting held on Wednesday, February 18, 2026, at WEMC.

Participants:

Nick BurnsBrent ArmentroutBob KleinChris WaltherBob MontgomeryTravis MillerKeith McComseyGuanging ZhaoKeith McComsey and Guanging Zhao visited WEMC to review the status of the Bendix/Walther trailer ADB program and discuss plans moving forward.

Tone Ring Rotational Movement & Safe-Launch Plan Alignment

The tone ring rotational movement acceptance criteria provided by Bendix has been accepted by Walther EMC. Based on prior limit sample cyclo and pull-off testing, we meet the established criteria.The safe-launch plan will be reviewed with Carlisle during one of our regularly scheduled Teams meetings. Carlisle was unable to attend in person due to flight delays.Neither Webb nor ConMet was aware that the tone ring rotated during similar testing. According to Bendix, there have been no field issues with the current designs related to tone ring movement caused by brake forces. All tone ring-related failures to date have been associated with fasteners and corrosion.The ConMet flat rotor snap ring has experienced ongoing corrosion-related field issues at Utility and has proposed a Geomet coating as a potential solution. Keith noted this may delay the issue, but is unlikely to fully resolve it.Marketing & Product Introduction Plan

Bendix is offering the ADB hub/rotor as part of a “Bendix Bundle,” which includes the ADB22X-LT brake, updated air chamber (EnduraSure or EnduraSure-Pro), iSense wear sensing,  ABS system, and wheel end.Stoughton and Hyundai have responded positively to the Bendix Bundle during the Bendix Fleet Council.Bendix is currently benchmarking HDNABI axles and brakes.  They are very concerned about competing long-term with HDNABI.Bendix plans to have fleets place orders for Q3 trailer builds, so this would entail the first Walther shipments in Q2.Some of the fleets which Keith mentioned as specifically interested in the Bendix Walther hub rotors:  Dot Foods, Werner, UPS, others The product will be announced at TMC in Nashville in March at the Bendix booth.  There will be product information on the video kiosk at the display, and it will reference Walther Dura-Light Hubs.  Keith will run this by us for accuracy.Bendix has agreed to stock 1,600 hub/rotors (approximately 400 trailers). We expect to receive a purchase order within the next few weeks.  These are expected to be 193 N hubs with short bolts and Stemco cups.  (This stock plan took shape during this meeting; Keith and Guangning did not have an agreement on this before this meeting.)Bendix and Walther agree that a key part of our strategy should be to offer improved lead times compared to ConMet (currently 14–16 weeks).  We communicated that our lead-time to first shipments is 10-12 weeks, and with properly forecasted demand, would run at 6-8 weeks.EDI integration will eventually be required; however, Bendix will place spot orders in the interim.Guanging will work to increase pallet quantities for the Bendix hub/rotor from 15 to 20 units. Current limitations are due to the rack weight capacity of 2,000 lbs.Warranty

Bendix and Walther will share responsibility for field-related issues.Bendix will serve as the first point of contact for warranty claims and will notify WEMC if the issue is determined to be hub-related or if the issue is uncertain and might be related to the hub and/or the hub/rotor joint.An introductory meeting will be scheduled between the Bendix warranty team and the WEMC warranty team (Lalisa and Product Engineering).Competitive Weights Measured by Bendix and Being Communicated to Potential Customers

Bendix Walther = 120.4 poundsAluminum Hub with Bendix U-shaped = 123 lbsTypical Iron Hub with Bendix U-shaped = 135 lbsLoaded Bendix Walther Hub Rotors (similar to QuickSet)

After we provided Bendix with a budgetary quote for a QuickeSet hub rotor, Bendix began to investigate whether they could load the hub/rotors more cost-efficiently.  This would eliminate the need for double-margining on the QuickSet components and associated labor.Bendix does not plan to promote loaded hub rotors in 2026, possibly in 2027 if a plan is put in place.Bendix agrees that trailer OEMs who want to dress their own ADB axles can do it most cost-effectively by purchasing the AutoTorque nuts directly from Stemco and installing the seal and cones.Bendix Walther Hub Rotors for Tractor Market

Guangining had hoped to have a significant discussion regarding Bendix Walther hub rotors for the tractor market.  He understands that the path from concept through market launch is long.We reviewed some of the past activity for Bendix Walther tractor hub rotors.  Specifically, Walther designed a hub for a splined rotor, and the foundries had significant concerns about porosity due to significant variations in section thickness from thick to thin sections.  Also, obtaining full ADI conversion through the thick sections would be difficult, but all agreed that this may not be a problem.Guangning suggested that we have a separate meeting specifically targeted at starting to make progress towards Bendix Walther tractor hub rotors.Other noteworthy items

Effective at TMC in March, Walther has Bendix permission to talk about the Bendix Walther hub rotors.  Bob M and Brent to share “talking points” and feature/benefit list with Keith M for comment.Prime is moving back to drum brakes.  Confidentially, Prime has a poor approach to maintenance, which has contributed to their poor experience with Bendix and Hendrickson ADBs.Bob M, Brent, Mike Kachin (Bendix), and Keith McComsey to coordinate sales efforts for the Bendix Walther trailer hub rotors. 

 

Nicholas Burns  |  Product Engineer III

3501 Shotwell Drive Franklin, Ohio 45005 United States

main 937.743.8125 Ext. 610 | direct 937.790.1852

nburns@waltheremc.com | waltheremc.com

AAMkAGFiMThhMWE4LTBiOTYtNDY2OS05NjE3LThk.body:
CAUTION: CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe. 

  
  
TBB Market Watch | View online
  
 
February 23, 2026

 

Editor's Note

We're still looking into the Supreme Court's rejection of Trump's 'emergency powers' tariff program on Friday, and the president's subsequent imposition of an across-the-board tariff increase. Regardless, the Sec. 232 duties on steel and aluminum remain in place. Stay tuned.

 

Trailer orders show resilience amid market uncertainty in early 2026
January trailer orders held steady above 23,000 units, reflecting ongoing fleet replacement and freight market strength but manufacturers face challenges from cost inflation, trade uncertainties, and low production levels.
Read More 

 
Tremcar visionary Jacques Tremblay dies at 83
Jacques Tremblay, who acquired A&L Tougas in 1989, renamed it Tremcar, and then partnered with his son to transform the business into one of North America’s leading tank trailer manufacturers, died Feb. 2 in Saint-Jean-sur-Richelieu, Québec, Canada. He was 83 years old.
Read More 

Sponsored
 
Maxon MLA – Heavy-Duty Liftgate Design with Tire-Specific Performance
Designed for the demands of tire service, the new Maxon MLA liftgate delivers heavy-duty performance with 1,600- or 2,000-lb capacities and a geometry that lets large tires clear with ease. The MLA comes with a galvanized finish, greaseable pivots for simplified maintenance, plus a variety of ramp and tire-rack options.

Visit www.maxonlift.com 

 
Novae unveils refresh of 11 trailer brands in its portfolio
The new brand positioning will enable Novae to provide dealers with “differentiated choices” within the portfolio.
Read More 

Sponsored
 
New 810-TX Sender Bar: High-Resolution, Temperature-Ready Design
The 810-TX Sender bar sets a higher standard for flammable liquid measurement. It offers 1/4" resolution, includes temperature sensors accurate to ±0.1°F, and features a field-replaceable battery module for easy maintenance. Must be paired with compatible displays and floats.

Learn more 

 
Wabash expands TrailerHawk.AI cargo security platform
The solution combines a purpose-built cargo door and intelligent locking system with TrailerHawk.AI’s connected platform, delivering real-time visibility.
Read More 

Sponsored
 
EMCO LEAF SPRINGS — MADE IN THE USA
For 35 years, EMCO has been delivering U.S.-manufactured leaf springs with consistent quality, dependable delivery, and zero tariff exposure — all backed by an ISO-certified operation. Connect with us today and see why industry leaders are making the switch.

Click here to learn more 

 
W&B acquires Sanders Trailer Service
The deal expands W&B's geographical footprint and product capabilities with three new locations in Kansas and Western Missouri.
Read More 

Sponsored
 
Instant Tire Status Access with TireView® LIVE Pre-Check™
Scanning P.S.I.’s TireView LIVE Pre-Check QR tag gives drivers and pre-trip inspection personnel real-time tire pressures and temperatures for each tire position, quickly identifying any issues that need attention. Pre-Check can be completed before a vehicle starts a trip or at any point during the scheduled route. Refreshing the device browser updates the information without additional scanning.

Keep Techs AND Drivers in the know! 

 
Hiab adds Custom Truck One Source to grow in West, NE
The partnership is a 'major milestone' for Hiab's getting close to full coverage of the U.S. market for HIAB loader cranes.
Read More 

Sponsored
 
RUGGED JOBS CALL FOR A RUGGED BODY…EAST DUMP BODIES
Engineered to meet the exact hauling demands of your operation, the East steel dump truck body delivers uncompromising durability, performance and long service life. Built with Hardox 500 TUF steel, it combines high strength and exceptional hardness in a single wear plate for the industry’s most punishing applications. When rugged jobs call for rugged, workhorse dump truck bodies, call East.

LEARN MORE 

 
Global Tank extends sales, service capabilities
The family-owned tank trailer specialist is now a MAC Trailer dealer in two states. It also now boasts seven locations after opening a new full-service facility in West Virginia.
Read More 

Sponsored
 
Shurco Designs Flip Arm Tarp Systems to Fit Your Application
Our Flip Arm Tarp Systems are a versatile, go-anywhere solution designed to fit dump bodies, end dumps, and belly dumps. Available in electric or manual configurations, they offer reliable performance to match your operation. Choose durable steel or lightweight aluminum construction, with side- or underbody-mount options to ensure the right fit, strength, and functionality for your application.

LEARN MORE 

 
Manufacturers need faster permitting, more practical trade and immigration policy
Kicking off a national "State of Manufacturing" Tour, National Association of Manufacturers' Jay Timmons and others spoke of the need for expanding grid, investing in infrastructure and more.
Read More 

Sponsored
 
Lightweight SAF AeroBeam™ Delivers Greater Weight Savings
The new CBX AeroBeam series of fixed frame suspensions offers the best in top mount technology with 23k, 25k and 30k capacity models. When equipped with P89 disc brakes, the series provides the lightest weight fixed frame air suspensions available. CBXA meets all vocational fleet applications with the CBXAN23 model being the ideal solution for tank, platform, and bulk transport trailers.

LEARN MORE 

 
Trailer/Body Builders magazine - February 2026
The February 2026 issue of TBB features our annual Trailer Output Report, a look at how trailers played with electronics at CES, and an economic outlook for 2026. And all the news about people, places and more in the TBB world.
Read More 

Sponsored
 
Breathing protection equipment in the booth
When you’re in the booth, reliable respiratory protection is a must. The SATA air vision 5000 delivers complete head and breathing air protection against solvents, sanding dust, and overspray — built for the demanding environment trailer builders face every day. The system includes the hood, belt unit, and choice of the SATA air regulator or air carbon regulator depending on your filtration setup.

Call now 800-533-8016 

Connect With Us

This email is being sent to jheath@waltheremc.com. 

Please add news.endeavorb2b.com and news.trailer-bodybuilders.com to your address book or safe sender list to receive our emails in your inbox.

Unsubscribe | Forward to a Friend | Customer Service Center | Read Privacy Policy

If you have trouble with any of these methods, you can reach us toll-free at 800-547-7377. 

Trailer Body Builders
EndeavorB2B
30 Burton Hills Blvd., Suite 185
Nashville, TN 37215

Does the Walther rotor hub have the same offset as the Walther drum hub? If not, do you sell spider/spindles that would fit?

Saf-holland axles

CBX25-15 on the lift axle

AAMkAGFiMThhMWE4LTBiOTYtNDY2OS05NjE3LThk.subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 8 stud hubs
AAMkAGFiMThhMWE4LTBiOTYtNDY2OS05NjE3LThk.body:
Jason,

 

After working with KGI to get the wheel end parameters, this will not be as big of a jump as I initially thought.

 

Larry had used a tire static loaded radius (SLR) of 17.7in for both the hub and drum testing.  KGI stated they are using a tire with an SLR of 15.6in.  I also googled some other tires to fit that size wheel and I found a range of 15.1in to 15.7 in.  

 

For the rotary fatigue test, the 23k GAWR and 15.6in SLR increase the applied load by only 64 lb and increase the bending moment by only 196 ft-lb when compared to the 20k GAWR and 17.7in values.  I am thinking that the RFT test will not need to be run since the hubs completed 300,000 cycles at the 20k and 17.7in values.

 

Similarly, the torque for the drum durability test increases only 1040 ft-lb going from the 18k (current drum rating) and 17.7in SLR to the 23k and 15.6 SLR.  But this test will need to be run to confirm.

 

I will bring this topic up in our Thursday huddle to discuss.

 

Thank you,

_____________________________________________

Bob Klein  |  Engineering Manager

3501 Shotwell Drive | Franklin, Ohio 45005 United States

direct 937-743-8132 | main 937.743.8125 ext.615 | fax 937.743.8232

bklein@waltheremc.com | www.waltheremc.com

Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - 8 stud hubs

 

Jason,

 

FYI, we will also need to run a drum certification test at 23k.  I am not as confident of passing this test.  It is currently rated/tested at 18k, so 23k is a pretty big jump.

 

Thank you,

Thanks,

Jason

AAMkAGFiMThhMWE4LTBiOTYtNDY2OS05NjE3LThk.subject: EXTERNAL - New Blogs: Thermoplastics and Rubber: Understanding Which Material Fits the Job and Diaphragms Designed to Precisely Fit Different Gas Meter Designs
AAMkAGFiMThhMWE4LTBiOTYtNDY2OS05NjE3LThk.body:
CAUTION: CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe. 

Thermoplastics and Rubber: Understanding Which Material Fits the Job
Material selection plays a defining role in how mechanical systems perform over time. The choice between thermoplastic and rubber components affects not only how a system functions on day one, but also how it holds up under real operating conditions such as temperature swings, chemical exposure, vibration, and wear. While both materials are common in industrial equipment, they are engineered to solve different problems, and understanding those differences is key to long-term reliability.

For engineers, designers, and maintenance teams, the decision is rarely about which material is “better.” It’s about which material is better suited to the demands of the application. When the material aligns with the operating environment, components last longer, perform more consistently, and require less intervention over their service life.

>> Understand which material fits your job

Diaphragms Designed to Precisely Fit Different Gas Meter DesignsIn the safety-driven natural gas industry, system leaks are a dangerous and costly issue. Residential gas meters and regulators, in particular, are at risk when crucial components such as diaphragms are not optimally designed and manufactured for the environment in which they will work.

An advanced polymer technology, trademarked by Freudenberg Sealing Technologies’ Metflex Precision Moulding organization, addresses these challenges with fiber-reinforced materials that improve the reliability, functionality and longevity of critical gas system components. Diaphragms made using Metflex Dispersed Fibre Technology (DFT)™ offer better sealing performance, longer flex life, improved low temperature resilience and enhanced design capabilities than standard diaphragms made from fabric coated polymers. To date, more than 40 million DFT diaphragms have been used in medium and low-pressure application, primarily in North America but also in other regions of the world.

>> See how diaphragms are designed to precisely fit different gas meters

Good investigative work Bob and good news!

 

On Feb 23, 2026, at 4:30 PM, Bob Klein BKlein@waltheremc.com> wrote:

﻿ 

Jason,

 

After working with KGI to get the wheel end parameters, this will not be as big of a jump as I initially thought.

 

Larry had used a tire static loaded radius (SLR) of 17.7in for both the hub and drum testing.  KGI stated they are using a tire with an SLR of 15.6in.  I also googled some other tires to fit that size wheel and I found a range of 15.1in to 15.7 in.  

 

For the rotary fatigue test, the 23k GAWR and 15.6in SLR increase the applied load by only 64 lb and increase the bending moment by only 196 ft-lb when compared to the 20k GAWR and 17.7in values.  I am thinking that the RFT test will not need to be run since the hubs completed 300,000 cycles at the 20k and 17.7in values.

 

Similarly, the torque for the drum durability test increases only 1040 ft-lb going from the 18k (current drum rating) and 17.7in SLR to the 23k and 15.6 SLR.  But this test will need to be run to confirm.

 

I will bring this topic up in our Thursday huddle to discuss.

 

Thank you,

_____________________________________________

Bob Klein  |  Engineering Manager

Jason,

 

FYI, we will also need to run a drum certification test at 23k.  I am not as confident of passing this test.  It is currently rated/tested at 18k, so 23k is a pretty big jump.

Hello Garette!

 

As Travis indicated, we do not offer lug nut covers or top hats.

 

Your inquiry below mentioned “hub cups”.  If you are asking about hubcaps, we do not offer those.  If you are asking about bearing cups for hubs, then yes, we do have those!

 

Here is a list of bearing cups and cones we can offer bulk-packed (not individually packaged, you would need to box them for distribution if selling in small quantities).  The following bearing cups and cones are produced by SKF’s General Hyatt brand:

3720

3920

25820

33462

HM212011

HM212049

HM218210

HM218248

HM518410

HM518445

JM207010

 

Regarding rotors, here is a link to the rotors we offer with competitive cross references:  https://waltheremc.com/cr-abs-brake-rotors/

 

 

Regarding disc brake calipers, we only offer the Walther Dura-Force Hydraulic disc brake.  It is a four-piston radial mount caliper and Autocar already buys it regularly under the part numbers 30-L0129-S1 and 30-R0129-S1.  These are the only brake calipers we offer since we do not remanufacture brake calipers nor do we offer an “all makes” line of brake calipers.

 

Please let me know if you have any questions or need anything else.

 

Thanks and Best Regards,

Brent

Turn scattered AI wins into real outcomes
 March 31, 2026 
 Digital Summit 
Join us for Teamwork in an AI era From power users to empowered teams  For organizations to drive measurable AI impact, they need to nail how human+AI teams coordinate, collaborate, and execute at scale. On March 31, Atlassian is bringing together an expert analyst from Forrester, bestselling author and leading organizational psychologist, Adam Grant, and members of the Atlassian Executive Team for a global* digital summit discussing how AI is reshaping work and driving innovation. Register now and walk away with a clear point of view – and practical examples – on how to: • Turn isolated AI wins into measurable enterprise outcomes • Raise the tide for every team, not just a few super users • Embed AI into the way work is coordinated, not bolted on as another "magic button" If you're ready to move beyond AI pilots and build an organization that executes as one, this event is for you. Register now *This event will run in English, with local language subtitles available.  Read in browser • Privacy policy • Contact us • Read our blog • Join Atlassian Community        Copyright 2026 Atlassian Pty Ltd. All rights reserved. 
We are located at 341 George Street, Sydney, NSW, 2000, Australia  Interested in this event? We'd also love to see you online or in person at Team '26! If you no longer wish to receive these emails, please update your preferences or unsubscribe here.

Brent is working with Bendix on a new project. Bendix needs proof that our tone ring attachment on Dura-Light hubs is robust in corrosive applications. They have requested two hubs from the field. John Grant has parts they have they will send to us. There is no issue with the parts.

 

To accommodate John Grant’s labor costs, Walther has agreed to compensate them by giving them two additional hubs (total of 4). But Brent needs these parts quickly and Walther has a hard time sending parts across the border because it does not have a broker. So I have reached out to a local distributor (Can Truck) who already has parts on the shelf. Can Truck has agreed to give 4 hubs (10-00112-101) to John Grant.

 

The RMA needs to be issued to John Grant since they are returning the parts. The credit needs to be issued to Can Truck because they are giving John Grant the parts. I am not aware that we have ever included two parties on an RMA before. If you can do that, then I’d add both (or simply note that Can Truck is giving them replacement parts and will receive credit from WEMC). If you cannot, I’d only include John Grant. Does that make sense?

 

To recoup our costs, Bendix has agreed to compensate us. Brent is coordinating this.

 

Thanks,

Jason

Indego Winter Weather Update

Once again, Indego is digging out from a major winter storm. Huge shout-out to our field team, who’ve been working hard to keep the system moving. It’s been an all-hands-on-deck effort across the city to dig out stations and bikes, swap batteries, and clear snow from docks and solar panels so stations can be used. We truly appreciate your patience as our team continues to chip away at it. If you’re riding Indego, be sure to check out our Winter Riding Tips to help you stay safe on the road!

Indego’s Winter Riding Tips
 Biking Black History Month

Explore Black History in Brewerytown and Sharswood! This bike route invites you to explore six vibrant works of art that celebrate Black leaders, local voices, and the spirit of community. If you take this ride, be sure to snap a few photos, tag us on Instagram using #IndegoBlackHistoryMuralTour, and share the experience with others who want to learn more about the neighborhood’s rich history. And if you’re riding this month, please be mindful of snow and ice and plan your trip accordingly.

Sharswood & Brewerytown Black History Mural Tour
 Join the Indego Team!

Indego is Hiring! We’re growing our team and are currently seeking a Field Technician, Senior Project Manager, and Chief Financial Officer. If you’re passionate about sustainable transportation and serving Philadelphia communities, we’d love to hear from you. Indego offers competitive pay, affordable health insurance, a 401k with employer match, a 4-day work week, paid parental leave, and free bike share memberships.

See Available Positions
 ICYMI: 2026 Expansion Update

With Philadelphia preparing for a busy and exciting year ahead, Indego is continuing to expand bike share access to support both everyday trips and citywide moments. Click below to learn more about Indego’s 2025 accomplishments, where we’re adding new stations this year, and how you can get involved.

Indego’s 2026 Expansion Update
 Where to Go With Indego

See What’s Happening in Philadelphia
 Indego is an initiative of the City of Philadelphia and sponsored by Independence Blue Cross

Last Chance
For Free Registration

 Don’t miss your chance to attend the world’s largest and longest-running trucking trade show — for free. Your registration unlocks 1,000,000+ square feet of exhibits, expert-led education, regulatory insights from FMCSA, and unmatched networking and entertainment.

Discover new technology, gain strategies to boost profitability, and build relationships that move your business forward. This is the industry’s biggest opportunity of the year: you won't want to miss it.

Networking & Events

so you can expand your network and thrive in 2026.

RIDE & DRIVES

Experience the performance of the 2027 X15 from Cummins paired with a Peterbilt 579 or Kenworth T680. With up to 605hp and 2,050 lb-ft of torque, the X15 has the power to rise above. Participation is free for all registered attendees. 

Learn More>

MATS Opening Breakfast

Kick off your MATS experience with an opening breakfast designed for owner-operators and small fleets looking to build resilient businesses in a difficult market.

Learn More>

Truck Parking Community

Recharge, relax, and have fun with free food, fun games, and more, all brought to you by DAT Freight & Analytics. As a thank you to hardworking drivers and carriers, DAT will provide free morning coffee, plus tasty options from food trucks later in the day. 

Learn More>

MATS Concert 

Come and see this year’s free Friday MATS Concert, featuring Frank Foster, Mark Ware, and Long Haul Paul, sponsored by Bennett Family of Trucks. Witness a high-energy mix of southern rock and country that will have you up and on your feet. 

Learn More>

MAC Championship Pulling

Feel the roar of competition with the MAC Trailer Winter Nationals Championship Truck Pull, featuring classes from hot rod semis to super stock diesels. See a thunderous showdown of high-horsepower engines that will shake Freedom Hall! 

Learn More>

PKY Truck Beauty Championship

The 35th annual PKY event kicks off the show truck season with the most elite custom show trucks in North America. Special features include the light show, parade, and Big Rig Build-Off. 

Learn More>

Opening Reception

Join us at the opening reception for a lively evening of celebration, networking, and fun. The reception includes live music, free food, free drinks, fun games, and the induction of the Wall of Fame nominees. 

Learn More>

Education
Learn From The Experts

Learn from the industry’s best minds about key industry trends and changes to stay informed and keep your business thriving. MATS 2026 will host 40+ free Pro Talks Education Sessions, Opening Breakfast , and much more.

EDUCATION PROGRAMS

MATS Opening Breakfast

Kick off your MATS experience with an opening breakfast designed for owner-operators and small fleets looking to build resilient businesses in a difficult market.

Learn More>

Pro Talks Seminars

Over 40 educational seminars covering the latest industry topics, regulatory updates, emerging technology, and solutions to keep you informed and your business thriving. 

Learn More>

Share on Social #MATS2026Let friends and other industry peers know about MATS by sharing updates with the event hashtag, #MATS2026!

Exhibit Management Associates, Inc.

1404 Browns Lane, Suite E
Louisville, KY 40207

AAMkAGFiMThhMWE4LTBiOTYtNDY2OS05NjE3LThk.subject: EXTERNAL - Scam Alert – NTDA Convention Housing
AAMkAGFiMThhMWE4LTBiOTYtNDY2OS05NjE3LThk.body:
CAUTION: CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe. 

We have been made aware that a scam email regarding hotel accommodations for the 36th Annual NTDA Convention was distributed to at least one NTDA member. We sincerely appreciate our members who promptly bring these issues to our attention so that we can notify you and alert the hotel.

 

Please be advised that registration and housing for the 36th Annual NTDA Convention, taking place October 14–16, 2026, at the Sawgrass Marriott Golf Resort & Spa, will open in May 2026.

 

Important reminders:

 

Official hotel reservation communications will only come from gwen@ntda.org.Only Shelley Reynolds or Gwen Brown will respond to specific questions regarding housing.The Sawgrass Marriott will not contact you directly about reservations unless you specifically reach out to them for assistance.The NTDA does not use third-party housing companies. We will never call you to request credit card information for hotel reservations. 

Other red flags to watch out for:

 

Unsolicited hotel offersUrgent languageRequests for wire transfers or payment by phoneEmail domains that don’t match ntda.orgMisspellings or slightly altered hotel names.Scam artists are becoming increasingly sophisticated. Be alert! 

If you receive any suspicious emails regarding convention registration or hotel reservations or other items, please do not respond or click any links. Instead, forward the message to us so we can investigate.

 

Thank you for your continued vigilance and support.

 

Best Regards,

 

Gwen

 

Gwendolyn Brown

President

 

 

National Trailer Dealers Association

9864 E. Grand River Ave., Ste. 110-290

Brighton, MI  48116

Toll-free: 1-800-800-4552

Direct Dial: (810) 229-5960

Cell: (810) 844-3124

www.ntda.org

 

PLEASE EXCUSE ANY TYPOS.

NTDA 36th Annual Convention 
Oct. 14-16, 2026

TPC Sawgrass Marriott Golf Resort & Spa

Ponte Vedra Beach, FL

 

Alert! If you receive any emails that seem suspicious, please reach out to NTDA President Gwen Brown at (810) 229-5960 or (810) 844-3124. Be cautious when opening or responding to emails to verify their legitimacy.

 

SCAM EMAIL BELOW

 

From: marketing marketing@digitalhotelbooking.com> 
Sent: Tuesday, February 24, 2026 11:41 AM
Subject: NTDA 2026 - 36th Annual National Trailer Dealers Association Convent

 

Hello

 

We would like to let you know that we are down to the last block of rooms at the headquarter hotels for NTDA 2026 - 36th Annual National Trailer Dealers Association Convent, From 14 to 16 Oct  at NTDA 2026 - 36th Annual National Trailer Dealers Association Convent ,You can find the reservation link below;

 

Sawgrass Marriott Golf Resort & Spa    1000            4*           0.0 Miles away

Price per night is $269  instead of $289 .

 

Book Now

 

Note: 10% Discount for group reservation (more than 8 rooms)

The rooms are on first come first serve basis, Let us know how many rooms you might need as soon as possible and it’s a flexible reservation you can always do any changes or cancellations Without any penalties.

 

Best Regards,

Selvia Williams.

Reservation Team.

From: Brent Armentrout  
Sent: Tuesday, February 24, 2026 10:49 AM
To: Bob Montgomery ; Jason Heath ; Keith Fally 
Subject: Sales Metrics for Management Review

 

Bob, Jason, Keith,

 

For the Management Review, we need to not only update the Sales & Marketing section of the notes, but also the KPI/Metrics section.

 

We have two metrics we are supposed to maintain/update/track.

 

Customer Retention Matrix – I traditionally create this each year.  It is attached.Market Share/Strategy – assigned to Bob M according to the KPI chart. 

I looked at last year’s Management Review notes covering 2024 performance.  It appears that the KPI section didn’t get updated last year.  Therefore, we never stated a goal for 2025.

 

Here’s what last year’s Management Review notes for 2024 showed relative to these two KPIs:

 

COP

KPI

Owner

2023

Goal

2023 Actual

Source

Action

2024  Goal

COP 2/3

Customer Retention Matrix

Brent

0 customers lost, >$30,000

1 material customer lost 

4 very small customers did not purchase product in 2023

File:  2023 Sales By Customer Rev C

Try to regain SKF business.

0

COP 2

Market Share/Strategy

Bob M

Maintain MS

Market share generally maintained

See Management Review section 6, Planning 

None

Increase Market Share

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Let’s update the section below to show our targets for last year and for 2026:

 

COP

KPI

Owner

2025

Goal

2025 Actual

Source

Action

2026  Goal

COP 2/3

Customer Retention Matrix

Brent

0 customers lost, >$100,000

No customers lost >$100k.  East moved the H dressed, Autocar lost UPS contracts, other major shifts associated with changing business level at each customer.

File:  2025 Sales By Customer

None

0 customers lost, >$100,000

COP 2

Market Share/Strategy

Bob M

Maintain or grow market share

Market share gained, but difficult to tell if it’s because we actually gained in our target markets or that our target markets (and Hendrickson) outperformed while total trailer sales dropped dramatically

See Management Review section 6, Planning 

Seek more accurate ways to measure share in our markets.  “Protect the carcus.”  Seek new fleet pull (45 accounts identified).  Push OEMs to pull Walther through Axle MFGs.

Maintain or grow market share in target markets.

All taxes, fees (including but not limited to Air Conditioning Excise Recovery Fee, Concession Recovery Fee, Vehicle License Recovery Fee, Energy Recovery Fee, Tire Management Fee, and Frequent Traveler Fee) and surcharges (including but not limited to Customer Facility Charge and Environmental Fee Recovery Charge) are extra. Optional products such as LDW ($29.99/day or less) and refueling are extra. BCD discount is subject to vehicle availability at the time of rental and may not be available on some rates at some times, including some online rates at avis.com. Car rental return restrictions may apply. Offer subject to change without notice. Renter must meet Budget age, driver, and credit requirements. Minimum rental age may vary by location. An additional daily surcharge may apply for renters under 25 years old. Valid on rentals completed between January 15 and April 30, 2026. 

2 Details may vary according to weather and race conditions and other factors. These experiences are only available to members of the Choice Privileges® Rewards Program who have the required number of Choice Privileges Rewards points in their account. Once redeemed the member will receive a confirmation email from Choice Privileges and within 48 hours of redemption, Trackhouse will contact the member with more information and any participation requirements. You (and any guests, if applicable) must be 18 or older to participate and you must provide a government-issued ID upon request. 

Selected members (“Winners”) will receive the experience and redeem for the amount of points bid. The number of points required varies depending upon the experience chosen. The experiences must be taken (used) on the dates stated for that experience, which generally range from January 2026-December 2026. 

Winners may not transfer experience (or any aspect thereof) to other individuals, are not redeemable for cash, and may not be substituted for any other experience or Choice Privileges® Reward, absent Choice’s prior written consent. The actual experience may differ from any promotional images. All federal, state, and other tax liabilities arising from winning an experience are the sole responsibility of the Winner. 

Other terms may apply. You (and any guest) will need to sign a release about your participating and about having your name, picture, image, appearance, likeness, voice, quotes, or statements in a live event and/or video/audio production or photography by Choice and/or other video or photography production companies on behalf of Choice. You are required to abide by any rules or instructions provided by Trackhouse, Choice, and by the race staff, at all times. 

3 Vacation package includes accommodations and named presentation incentives only. Transportation, personal expenses, taxes, parking and other expenses are not included. Accommodations are subject to availability at a participating hotel or resort within the Choice Hotels portfolio (retail value of $175—$900/night), except in locations where accommodations at another brand hotel or resort may be offered. Certain restrictions may apply. Accommodation upgrades, stays over weekends, high season, and holidays are limited and may include additional fees. Guests must attend a two-hour timeshare sales presentation and tour on the benefits of ownership with Bluegreen Vacation Club. Vacation packages are nontransferable and cannot be combined with other promotional offers. One offer per household. For full Details of Participation, click here.

Guests will receive a gift of 30,000 Choice Privileges® Points (retail value of up to $309) after the timeshare sales presentation. The Choice Privileges® points referenced in the offer are only available for purchasers who are qualified by meeting the terms and conditions set forth for this offer. All reservations subject to availability. Terms and conditions for specific merchants are available when redeeming points. For program details and point redemption rules, visit www.choiceprivileges.com.

Guests will receive a gift of a $100 MasterCard® Reward Card (retail value of $100) after the timeshare sales presentation. The MasterCard® Reward Card referenced in this offer is only available for purchasers who are qualified by meeting the terms and conditions set forth for this offer. MasterCard is issued by Four Corners Bank, member FDIC, pursuant to a license by MasterCard International Incorporated. MasterCard and the MasterCard brand mark are registered trademarks of MasterCard International Incorporated. Use of this card is subject to the Terms and Conditions of the Card Holder Agreement found at www.transcardgift.com. By accepting, signing or using the card, you agree to the terms of the Card Holder Agreement. As the property of Four Corners Bank the surrender of this card may be required at any time. For account access or customer service, go online to www.transcardgift.com. or call (800) 554-2707.

THIS MATERIAL IS FOR THE PURPOSE OF SOLICITING TIMESHARE OWNERSHIP INTERESTS. THE COMPLETE OFFERING TERMS ARE IN AN OFFERING PLAN AVAILABLE FROM THE DEVELOPER. Prices range from $9,900 to $853,990 USD, subject to availability. Pricing is dependent on product purchased, without promotions and discounts and subject to change. Eligibility and financing requirements apply. Additional restrictions may apply. 

This is neither an offer or solicitation to sell to residents in jurisdictions in which registration requirements have not been fulfilled, and your eligibility and the timeshare plan available for purchase will depend upon the state of the purchaser.

Mid-America Trucking Show | March 26 - 28, 2026

MATS 2026
Book Your Hotel Now!

Jason,

Hotel rooms for MATS 2026 are quickly filling up - hurry and book yours now before it's too late!  

We've negotiated the lowest hotel rates for our exhibitors and attendees at the most popular and most convenient hotels in the Louisville area. To book your hotel for the 2026 show, click below.

Book Your Hotel Room Now

What do MATS Hotel Rooms Offer?

Access to the best hotels, the best rates, and a dedicated team to ensure you are well taken care of. Click the link above to view a list of hotels, sortable by distance from the Expo Center, price, or rating, along with a convenient map and list of included amenities.

Best Rates
By reserving 1000s of room nights in the Louisville area, MATS is able to negotiate the best rates available during the show and pass the savings on to you.

Best Hotels
With conveniently located hotels featuring large room blocks, MATS attendees can meet and network with other industry professionals outside of show hours.

Dedicated Team
MATS works closely with the City of Louisville, Louisville Tourism, and the local hotels to ensure the best experience and to address any last-minute needs.

 If you need to reserve more than 10 room nights, please complete the  Room Block Request Form and a team member will get back to you with tailored options and pricing to meet your needs.

Follow #MATS2026 on Social

AAMkAGFiMThhMWE4LTBiOTYtNDY2OS05NjE3LThk.body:
CAUTION: CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe. 

Hi Jason, you may remember we were discussing your Extended warranty package for Hub and Rotor Package.

We are currently working on a 105 axle deal. They require disc brake, and the extended wheel end warranty.

We have asked about rotor replacement, and were told it is generally accepted in the industry that the warranty is void if you have to remove the hub to do a rotor replacement.

My question to you:
When would you have a package ready to offer for P-style Disc Brake R22.5
Is the Autotorq nut a requirement, or can it be packaged with the Protorq. This is generally more accepted in the market.
If you cannot offer a full package, is it possible us to buy the full set from you, and get the warranty through Stemco? If so, how would the hub and rotor warranty work?

Thanks! Let me know if you have any questions.

Brandon Hayter

Director of Products

P. 250.499.2492   Ext. 106
www.kgi.ca
725 Hwy 3, Cawston, BC, V0X 1C3

Monthly Update 
If you are having trouble viewing this email, view online.
  What to Expect at Cleo Connect: Orchestrate - Spring 2026!

We will kick off on Tuesday afternoon with our CEO Keynote, followed by can't-miss sessions featuring: 
Agentic AI and how it’s embedded within Cleo’s productsAn inside look at the latest CIC Orchestration Edition release, featuring Chargeback Prevention and On-Time Shipment in FullA forward-looking Cloud product roadmap session highlighting what’s ahead.Wednesday will dig in to features such as TM&M, Cockpit, and Containerization. You'll also get a look at our CIC Private Cloud (On-Premise) roadmap, hear from your peers during Customer Panels, and more!

Check out our Cleo Connect Event page to learn more about the venue and the awesome Welcome Reception we have planned - it includes a VIP Tour of AT&T Stadium, home of the Dallas Cowboys! Also, make sure you bookmark it and check back regularly for updates, including the full Agenda and Speaker announcements.

Register Now 

 

 Live Demo with Q&A: Direct Procurement Orchestration

Join our upcoming live demo and Q&A on Thursday, March 12, to see how direct procurement orchestration streamlines supplier collaboration, automates critical workflows, and improves visibility across your supply chain. Discover how to reduce disruptions, eliminate manual processes, and drive smarter, faster procurement decisions. Click below to reserve your spot today.

Reserve Your Spot 

 Cleo launches chargeback prevention and supply chain orchestration tools for retail suppliers

Cleo is making waves in retail supply chains. Inside Logistics covers the launch of Cleo’s new chargeback prevention and supply chain orchestration capabilities designed to help retail suppliers reduce costly deductions and improve partner performance. Want to see it in action? Sign up below for our upcoming live demo on March 26.

Register Now! 

 Supreme Court Strikes Down Trump’s Global Tariffs in Landmark Separation of Powers Ruling

The Supreme Court struck down President Trump’s global tariffs in a landmark ruling on separation of powers — a decision poised to reshape U.S. trade policy and global supply chains. The article includes perspective from our CEO Mahesh Rajasekharan on what this means for businesses navigating ongoing trade volatility.

Read the Techstrong.IT Article 

 Podcast: Supply Chain Orchestration: Unpacking Data Clarity and Alignment with Cleo

In this episode of Unraveling IT, Peter Rodenhauser sits down with our Frank Kenney to discuss how relationship management is transforming the digital supply chain. Learn how CIC goes beyond EDI to automate processes, reduce chargebacks, improve compliance, and harness AI—while ensuring strong governance in the age of agentic AI.

Listen to the Podcast

AAMkAGFiMThhMWE4LTBiOTYtNDY2OS05NjE3LThk.body:
You don't often get email from tiara.simons@monasolutions.com. Learn why this is important 
CAUTION: CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe. 

Here are four simple examples of automation already empowering the trucking industry. Here are four simple examples of automation already empowering the trucking industry... MONA PAYMENT SOLUTIONS | MERCHANT PROCESSING SERVICES | (805) 418-7595 
 United States & Canada    Your Trucking Industry Specialist!    4 Practical Ways
Automation Is Improving
the Trucking Industry    According to the American Trucking Association (ATA), trucking is the most prolific method of transportation in the country, accounting for 11.46 billion tons of freight per year. Despite that, it still faces efficiency issues in a variety of processes, from logistics to warehousing. One way for the trucking industry to become more productive is to rely more on automation.

Automation in transportation can eliminate many roadblocks slowing down the growth of the industry, such as repetitive manual tasks and poor working conditions. Here are four simple examples of automation already empowering the trucking industry.

1. Better Warehouse Capabilities

In the trucking industry, warehousing involves the packaging, storing, loading, and unloading of goods. Unsurprisingly, these processes can be very repetitive and time-consuming. Automation can do a lot to speed things up. For example, automated Robotic Truck Loaders (RTLs) will usually process heavy-duty shipments faster and more efficiently than manual laborers.

You also have Goods-to-Persons (GTP) robots, which can expedite almost any warehousing process. In doing so, they contribute to reducing wait times for truck drivers, allowing them to make better delivery times and improving trucking companies’ retention rates.

2. Truck Safety and Maintenance

Automated truck monitoring systems come with a variety of sensors that ensure that the truck is always in optimal condition. Any irregularities in internal machinery are easily detected, which is essential for the routine maintenance of trucking fleets. Since trucking maintenance is often overlooked, these monitoring systems can have a major impact on your bottom line.

Fleet management software can also generate telematics alerts or work orders from inspection reports, automatically assigning them to technicians whenever necessary. 

  Automation: Key Factor in Payment Processing! 
  3. Improved Dispatch and Delivery

Truck drivers often need to carry tons of perishables or products that are prone to damage. Automation can predict the best routes for drivers carrying these goods, helping keep the goods in safe and usable condition. When calculating these routes, automation can consider factors such as local traffic conditions, prevailing weather, and a lot more.

Your company may also use smart trucks, which are fitted with state-of-the-art data transmitters. This makes it easier for the dispatchers to assist drivers in real time, providing guidance on how to handle sensitive cargo and informing them of any changes to their route.

4. Faster Payments

Automation can be a key factor in payment processing as well. It allows you to optimize your merchant services, significantly reducing the labor involved in payment settlement operations. Some payment processors also allow you to share data-backed insights instantly, paving the way for greater operational efficiency at no additional effort.

In the long run, automating your trucking company’s payment settlement process will ensure better fundflow management, quicker reconciliation, and reduced operational risks.

As automation continues transforming logistics, warehousing, fleet management, and dispatch, your payment systems should evolve alongside the rest of your operation. Efficient, optimized payment processing plays a critical role in maintaining strong cash flow, reducing operational friction, and supporting long-term growth. 

At MONA Payment Solutions, we work with trucking companies to strengthen and streamline their payment strategies. Call today to schedule a consultation and discover how optimizing your payment systems can help move your business forward.

AAMkAGFiMThhMWE4LTBiOTYtNDY2OS05NjE3LThk.subject: Waupaca Surcharges
AAMkAGFiMThhMWE4LTBiOTYtNDY2OS05NjE3LThk.body:
Our pricing model is based on December What-if costing, but we have the capability to add known changes since then to make sure we capture appropriate margins.

 

Since December, 043 drum surcharges are up $4.05.  106/043 is up $4.75, and 161/043 is up $4.82.  This is similar to what happened last spring when they went up $4.00, $5.40, and $5.52, respectively, February to April.

 

When quoting, be sure to check the surcharges since December and add them to your cost base.

 

I’ll start calculating revised surcharges, but will need 2026 costing models to make sure we capture any internal increases.

 

Thanks!

Bob

AAMkAGFiMThhMWE4LTBiOTYtNDY2OS05NjE3LThk.subject: EXTERNAL - Ashland University Advisory Board Opening for Strategic AI for Sales Certificate Program
AAMkAGFiMThhMWE4LTBiOTYtNDY2OS05NjE3LThk.body:
You don't often get email from jen_luedtke@ashland.education. Learn why this is important 
CAUTION: CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe. 

Hi Jason,

I would like to set up a brief call to explore having you or someone from your organization join the Advisory Board for our Strategic AI for Sales Certificate Program at Ashland University’s Workforce Development. Membership on the Advisory Board is by invitation only, based upon your personal and professional accomplishments. 

The Board meets three times per year to discuss the curriculum of our Strategic AI for Sales Certificate Program. The meetings include a short thought leadership presentation by one of our faculty members, followed by a networking event. You can learn more about the Advisory Board and the program by clicking on the following links:

Advisory Brochure

Program Brochure

Program Website

Other Board members include senior executives at companies such as Amazon, Cleveland Clinic, Goodyear, Kroger, LiftMaster, Progressive Insurance, Sherwin-Williams, Summa Health, UPS, Walmart and Wipro. Once you’ve had a chance to review the materials, please reply, indicating if you would like to be part of this exciting initiative. 

Thank you for taking the time to learn about our program and potentially serving on our Advisory Board. I look forward to hearing from you soon so we can discuss this opportunity in more detail.

If you are interested, please book on Jennifer's calendar to set up a time to discuss this exciting opportunity. This program reports to Joanne Healy, VP of University Relations (Jhealy@Zschool.com) and Stephanie D. Meeks, Director of the Office for Workforce Development in the division of eAshland (smeeks2@ashland.edu). Please direct questions to Jennifer Luedtke, the program manager, who is best suited to share information and answer questions regarding the program.

Best regards,

Jennifer Luedtke

Program Manager, Zschool

Strategic AI for Sales Certificate Program

Workforce Development at Ashland University

(419) 324-8002 

My Calendar

ashland.edu/strategic-ai-sales-program

AAMkAGFiMThhMWE4LTBiOTYtNDY2OS05NjE3LThk.body:
CAUTION: CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe. 

  
  
TBB Market Watch | View online
  
 
March 2, 2026

 

 

Dealer education, new tech define 2026 Trailer Tech Expo
The small-format expo encouraged more substantive conversations between dealers, manufacturers and vendors, many offering hands-on demos as well as sneak peeks at new products and services. 
Read More 

Sponsored
 
Maxon MLA – Heavy-Duty Liftgate Design with Tire-Specific Performance
Designed for the demands of tire service, the new Maxon MLA liftgate delivers heavy-duty performance with 1,600- or 2,000-lb capacities and a geometry that lets large tires clear with ease. The MLA comes with a galvanized finish, greaseable pivots for simplified maintenance, plus a variety of ramp and tire-rack options.

Visit www.maxonlift.com 

 
Altec to expand North Carolina facility, add 100 jobs
Dubbed Project Poplar, the expansion represents the next phase of Altec’s growth in Mount Airy, where it is celebrating the 10-year anniversary of its facility there
Read More 

Sponsored
 
Introducing SeeLeveL Octane™ 812 — Multi-Compartment Tank Monitoring
The SeeLeveL Octane™ model 812 is the ultimate multi-compartment monitoring system, delivering precise liquid level, volume, and temperature readings across up to six compartments. With Bluetooth? connectivity, mobile app control, and comprehensive alarm management, it ensures safety, efficiency, and accurate inventory tracking.

Learn more 

 
Ford recalls 4.4 million trucks, vans, SUVs over trailer software glitch
A Trailer Module software error can cause trailers towed by certain 2021-2026 models to lose trailer lights and possibly brakes.
Read More
