AAMkAGFiMThhMWE4LTBiOTYtNDY2OS05NjE3LThk.subject: Capacity Loadings
AAMkAGFiMThhMWE4LTBiOTYtNDY2OS05NjE3LThk.body:
I updated the orders vs planning capacity this morning.

I’ll start with the easy one.

 

AS5 (Dura-Light)

This is based on a planning capacity of 760 pcs per day across all Dura-Light assemblies.

We are only 62% loaded looking out over the next 6 weeks.

We have plenty of room to take on additional orders within our standard leadtimes (6 to 8 weeks).

 

Onto the hard ones.

AS6  (EasyRoll Hub Assembly)

 

This is based on a planning capacity of 400 pcs per day.

We are very full looking out 7 weeks.

We do get up to 4 days behind by 4/6 week.   

We are moving some Stoughton orders ahead into March (based on their request) which will help to level load this.

 

Basic message:  We are fully loaded over the next 7 weeks, or until 4/27 week.

 

AS1  (Easy Roll Hub/Drum Assembly)

This is lighter at an average of 270 per day over the next 6 weeks.

However, we need an Easyroll hub to go along with a drum, so the overall message here is AS1 just as full as AS6.

 

Travis

Team

Michael Barlow

From: Barlow, Michael 
Sent: Tuesday, November 18, 2025 9:52 AM
To: customerservice@waltheremc.com; Lightfoot, Chris 
Cc: Donnell, Eric R. ; Ross, Lilly ; Lunceford, Phillip H. 
Subject: RE: 2026 USMCA Solicitation - Walther Engineering

 

 

Dear SAF Holland Supplier,

 

SAF Holland / Haldex is requesting your company provide a 2026 United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement (USMCA) Certificate or Certificate of Origin and Conflict Mineral Reporting for all parts you supply to SAF Holland. 

 

USMCA

You are only required to Certify Country of Origin, The below link id helpful guide can be found on the U.S. Customs website at https://www.cbp.gov/trade/priority-issues/trade-agreements/free-trade-agreements/USMCA

The following items have been included to assist you in responding to this request:

1.                      2026 USMCA Certification Request Letter

2.                      General Guidelines for completing a USMCA Certificate of Origin

3.                      USMCA Certificate of Origin, Instructions for completion of the USMCA Certificate of Origin, USMCA Certificate continuation pages

We have supplied a “Part Data” tab in the attached file that contains part detail for the parts that we show you currently provide.These part details are based on PO data and the USMCA responses that we reviewed for 2025.Please review this list and remove any parts you no longer supply and if there are parts you are now supplying that are not on the list, please add them.Please review HS Tariff Classification, Origin Criteria, and Country of Origin to be sure you still agree with this data.Please highlight any changes you make in Yellow.If you agree with the data provided or have made necessary adjustments, please complete the USMCA Certification Page. We ask that you reply with the reviewed Excel file but sign the certificate page and provide the signed certificate page as a PDF. 

SAF Holland requests that your certificate be returned electronically, in excel format, to the email address below.

 

***If you are supplier located in North America, we ask that you complete the USMCA Certificate of Origin if your parts are eligible. If your parts are not eligible for USMCA Preferential Treatment or you are not located in North America, we ask that you provide a Country-of-Origin Declaration per number 4 above for these items.  This includes obsolete or inactive parts. ***

 

Please complete the attached documents by December 1, 2025 and return by email attachment to the following e-mail addresses michael.barlow@safholland.com; phillip.lanceford@safholland.com; eric.donnell@safholland.com and lilly.ross@safholland.com

 

Thank you for your cooperation and prompt response. If you have any questions regarding this request, please feel free to reach out to us for assistance.

 

 

Phillip Lunceford

Import/Export Compliance Specialist

 

Haldex Brake Products Corporation

10930 N. Pomona Avenue

Kansa City, MO 64153

Phone: 816.801.2500

 

phillip.lunceford@safholland.com

Follow SAF-HOLLAND                   Follow Haldex

 

   

 

 

 

The content of this e-mail is meant exclusively for the person to whom this address is designated. If you are not the person to whom this e-mail is addressed or his/her representative, please take notice, that any form of knowledge, publication, duplication or distribution of the content of this e-mail is inadmissible. We ask you, therefore, in such a case to please get in contact with the sender of this e-mail destroy this email. This e-mail will be archived. In our privacy policy you will find further information about the processing of your personal data at SAF-HOLLAND.

On Mon, Mar 2, 2026 at 1:47 PM Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com> wrote:

Here is an example of a cold email I received today. I can see how flattery could bring interest.

 

From: Jennifer Luedtke  
Sent: Monday, March 2, 2026 10:32 AM
To: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com>
Subject: EXTERNAL - Ashland University Advisory Board Opening for Strategic AI for Sales Certificate Program

 

You don't often get email from jen_luedtke@ashland.education. Learn why this is important 

 

CAUTION: CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe. 

Hi Jason,

 

I would like to set up a brief call to explore having you or someone from your organization join the Advisory Board for our Strategic AI for Sales Certificate Program at Ashland University’s Workforce Development. Membership on the Advisory Board is by invitation only, based upon your personal and professional accomplishments. 

The Board meets three times per year to discuss the curriculum of our Strategic AI for Sales Certificate Program. The meetings include a short thought leadership presentation by one of our faculty members, followed by a networking event. You can learn more about the Advisory Board and the program by clicking on the following links:

 

Advisory Brochure

Program Brochure

Program Website

Other Board members include senior executives at companies such as Amazon, Cleveland Clinic, Goodyear, Kroger, LiftMaster, Progressive Insurance, Sherwin-Williams, Summa Health, UPS, Walmart and Wipro. Once you’ve had a chance to review the materials, please reply, indicating if you would like to be part of this exciting initiative. 

Thank you for taking the time to learn about our program and potentially serving on our Advisory Board. I look forward to hearing from you soon so we can discuss this opportunity in more detail.

 

If you are interested, please book on Jennifer's calendar to set up a time to discuss this exciting opportunity. This program reports to Joanne Healy, VP of University Relations (Jhealy@Zschool.com) and Stephanie D. Meeks, Director of the Office for Workforce Development in the division of eAshland (smeeks2@ashland.edu). Please direct questions to Jennifer Luedtke, the program manager, who is best suited to share information and answer questions regarding the program.

Best regards,

 

Jennifer Luedtke

Program Manager, Zschool

Strategic AI for Sales Certificate Program

Workforce Development at Ashland University

(419) 324-8002 

My Calendar

ashland.edu/strategic-ai-sales-program

Subject: RE: 2026 USMCA Solicitation - Walther Engineering

 

 

Dear SAF Holland Supplier,

 

SAF Holland / Haldex is requesting your company provide a 2026 United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement (USMCA) Certificate or Certificate of Origin and Conflict Mineral Reporting for all parts you supply to SAF Holland. 

 

USMCA

You are only required to Certify Country of Origin, The below link id helpful guide can be found on the U.S. Customs website at https://www.cbp.gov/trade/priority-issues/trade-agreements/free-trade-agreements/USMCA

The following items have been included to assist you in responding to this request:

1.                      2026 USMCA Certification Request Letter

2.                      General Guidelines for completing a USMCA Certificate of Origin

3.                      USMCA Certificate of Origin, Instructions for completion of the USMCA Certificate of Origin, USMCA Certificate continuation pages

We have supplied a “Part Data” tab in the attached file that contains part detail for the parts that we show you currently provide.These part details are based on PO data and the USMCA responses that we reviewed for 2025.Please review this list and remove any parts you no longer supply and if there are parts you are now supplying that are not on the list, please add them.Please review HS Tariff Classification, Origin Criteria, and Country of Origin to be sure you still agree with this data.Please highlight any changes you make in Yellow.If you agree with the data provided or have made necessary adjustments, please complete the USMCA Certification Page. We ask that you reply with the reviewed Excel file but sign the certificate page and provide the signed certificate page as a PDF. 

SAF Holland requests that your certificate be returned electronically, in excel format, to the email address below.

 

***If you are supplier located in North America, we ask that you complete the USMCA Certificate of Origin if your parts are eligible. If your parts are not eligible for USMCA Preferential Treatment or you are not located in North America, we ask that you provide a Country-of-Origin Declaration per number 4 above for these items.  This includes obsolete or inactive parts. ***

 

Please complete the attached documents by December 1, 2025 and return by email attachment to the following e-mail addresses michael.barlow@safholland.com; phillip.lanceford@safholland.com; eric.donnell@safholland.com and lilly.ross@safholland.com

 

Thank you for your cooperation and prompt response. If you have any questions regarding this request, please feel free to reach out to us for assistance.

 

 

Phillip Lunceford

Import/Export Compliance Specialist

 

Haldex Brake Products Corporation

10930 N. Pomona Avenue

Kansa City, MO 64153

Phone: 816.801.2500

 

phillip.lunceford@safholland.com

Follow SAF-HOLLAND                   Follow Haldex

Just for clarity regarding our manpower capacity versus our equipment capacity…

 

How many shifts are assumed for each of these?

 

AS5 (Dura-Light)

760 pcs per day based on 3 shifts per day (Machining and assembly). 

 

AS6  (EasyRoll Hub Assembly)

400 pcs per day based on 2 shifts per day (Machining).

                      Currently 1 shift/day (assembly).
                      Started training an AS6 operator today 3-2-26.
 

AS1  (Easy Roll Hub/Drum Assembly)

270 pcs per day based on 2 shifts per day 

 

Many Thanks,

Brent

 

From: Travis Miller tmiller@waltheremc.com>
Sent: Monday, March 2, 2026 11:13 AM
To: Alex Walther AWalther@waltheremc.com>; Tony Colon TColon@waltheremc.com>; Customer Service customerservice@waltheremc.com>; Bob Montgomery bmontgomery@waltheremc.com>; Brent Armentrout BArmentrout@waltheremc.com>; Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com>; Keith Fally kfally@waltheremc.com>; Michell McCormick MMcCormick@waltheremc.com>
Cc: Chris Walther CWalther@waltheremc.com>; Jake Miller jmiller@waltheremc.com>; Kevin Clawson kclawson@waltheremc.com>
Subject: Capacity Loadings

 

I updated the orders vs planning capacity this morning.

I’ll start with the easy one.

 

AS5 (Dura-Light)

This is based on a planning capacity of 760 pcs per day across all Dura-Light assemblies.

We are only 62% loaded looking out over the next 6 weeks.

We have plenty of room to take on additional orders within our standard leadtimes (6 to 8 weeks).

 

Onto the hard ones.

AS6  (EasyRoll Hub Assembly)

 

This is based on a planning capacity of 400 pcs per day.

We are very full looking out 7 weeks.

We do get up to 4 days behind by 4/6 week.  

We are moving some Stoughton orders ahead into March (based on their request) which will help to level load this.

 

Basic message:  We are fully loaded over the next 7 weeks, or until 4/27 week.

 

AS1  (Easy Roll Hub/Drum Assembly)

This is lighter at an average of 270 per day over the next 6 weeks.

However, we need an Easyroll hub to go along with a drum, so the overall message here is AS1 just as full as AS6.

 

Travis

If your parts are not eligible for USMCA Preferential Treatment or you are not located in North America, we ask that you provide a Country-of-Origin Declaration per number 4 above for these items.  This includes obsolete or inactive parts. ***

 

Please complete the attached documents by December 1, 2025 and return by email attachment to the following e-mail addresses michael.barlow@safholland.com; phillip.lanceford@safholland.com; eric.donnell@safholland.com and lilly.ross@safholland.com

Just for clarity regarding our manpower capacity versus our equipment capacity…

 

How many shifts are assumed for each of these?

 

AS5 (Dura-Light)

760 pcs per day based on 3 shifts per day (Machining and assembly). 

 

AS6  (EasyRoll Hub Assembly)

400 pcs per day based on 2 shifts per day (Machining).

                      Currently 1 shift/day (assembly).

                      Started training an AS6 operator today 3-2-26.

 

AS1  (Easy Roll Hub/Drum Assembly)

270 pcs per day based on 2 shifts per day 

 

 

Many Thanks,

Brent

 

From: Travis Miller tmiller@waltheremc.com>
Sent: Monday, March 2, 2026 11:13 AM
To: Alex Walther AWalther@waltheremc.com>; Tony Colon TColon@waltheremc.com>; Customer Service customerservice@waltheremc.com>; Bob Montgomery bmontgomery@waltheremc.com>; Brent Armentrout BArmentrout@waltheremc.com>; Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com>; Keith Fally kfally@waltheremc.com>; Michell McCormick MMcCormick@waltheremc.com>
Cc: Chris Walther CWalther@waltheremc.com>; Jake Miller jmiller@waltheremc.com>; Kevin Clawson kclawson@waltheremc.com>
Subject: Capacity Loadings

 

I updated the orders vs planning capacity this morning.

I’ll start with the easy one.

 

AS5 (Dura-Light)

This is based on a planning capacity of 760 pcs per day across all Dura-Light assemblies.

We are only 62% loaded looking out over the next 6 weeks.

We have plenty of room to take on additional orders within our standard leadtimes (6 to 8 weeks).

 

Onto the hard ones.

AS6  (EasyRoll Hub Assembly)

 

This is based on a planning capacity of 400 pcs per day.

We are very full looking out 7 weeks.

We do get up to 4 days behind by 4/6 week.  

We are moving some Stoughton orders ahead into March (based on their request) which will help to level load this.

 

Basic message:  We are fully loaded over the next 7 weeks, or until 4/27 week.

 

AS1  (Easy Roll Hub/Drum Assembly)

This is lighter at an average of 270 per day over the next 6 weeks.

However, we need an Easyroll hub to go along with a drum, so the overall message here is AS1 just as full as AS6.

 

Travis

Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - RE: Favor?

 

Good questions Lalisa,

Brent is working with Bendix on a new project. Bendix needs proof that our tone ring attachment on Dura-Light hubs is robust in corrosive applications. They have requested two hubs from the field. John Grant has parts they have they will send to us. There is no issue with the parts.

 

To accommodate John Grant’s labor costs, Walther has agreed to compensate them by giving them two additional hubs (total of 4). But Brent needs these parts quickly and Walther has a hard time sending parts across the border because it does not have a broker. So I have reached out to a local distributor (Can Truck) who already has parts on the shelf. Can Truck has agreed to give 4 hubs (10-00112-101) to John Grant.

 

The RMA needs to be issued to John Grant since they are returning the parts. The credit needs to be issued to Can Truck because they are giving John Grant the parts. I am not aware that we have ever included two parties on an RMA before. If you can do that, then I’d add both (or simply note that Can Truck is giving them replacement parts and will receive credit from WEMC). If you cannot, I’d only include John Grant. Does that make sense?

 

To recoup our costs, Bendix has agreed to compensate us. Brent is coordinating this.

 

Thanks,

Jason

Sent: Thursday, February 5, 2026 10:40 AM
To: Jason Heath JHeath@waltheremc.com>

Subject: RE: EXTERNAL - RE: Favor?

Book Preferred Hotels & Resorts With Choice Privileges® Points

 
Exquisite Stays in Iconic Destinations

Get more from your Choice Privileges® points when you redeem them for luxury stays. Whether you dream of a remote getaway or a five-star sanctuary, Preferred Hotels & Resorts opens the door to the world’s most exquisite destinations.

Explore Destinations 
 
Explore More

Preferred Hotels & Resorts

 

Boston Harbor Hotel

Boston, Massachusetts, United States

Set along Rowes Wharf in the heart of the city, Boston Harbor Hotel blends sweeping harbor views with timeless New England elegance. Inviting accommodations, exceptional waterfront dining and an ideal location near historic sites and the Harborwalk define stays that are both indulgent and undeniably Bostonian.

Serras Barcelona

Barcelona, Spain

Overlooking Marina Port Vell, Serras Barcelona is a contemporary urban oasis in the Gothic Quarter. The building where Picasso once worked now features sleek rooms, a rooftop pool and Michelin‑Starred dining at Informal—an experience that unites art, cuisine and the city’s lively energy.

The Leela Palace Bengaluru

Bengaluru, India

Set amid seven acres of lush gardens in India’s Garden City, The Leela Palace Bengaluru invites guests into a world of regal grandeur. Grand architecture, a magnificent art collection and hospitality rooted in heritage create an immersive five-star retreat for experiencing India’s vibrant culture.

Choice Privileges points will only guarantee the Reward Night room fee. The hotel will collect a credit card at check-in, and any resort fees, incidental fees, service charges, food and beverage costs or room deposits are the sole responsibility of the guest travelling and must be paid at check out. Taxes are included in the points redemption, and you will not be charged additional room taxes.

 

Reward Night reservations with Preferred Hotels & Resorts cannot be modified. If you desire to change or extend your stay using your Choice Privileges points, you must cancel your reservation by using the cancellation link provided on your reservation confirmation email and then rebook your stay through the booking link provided on Choicehotels.com. You are not able to extend your redemption stay using points by calling the hotel direct or altering the reservation during your stay at the hotel. All reservations utilizing points for a Reward Night redemption must be made through the booking link provided on Choicehotels.com. 

 

The cancellation policy of the selected hotel applies to the Choice Privileges Reward Night and supersedes Choice Privileges program rules. If you cancel your reservation in accordance with the hotel’s cancellation policy stated on your reservation confirmation, all points used for the reward night will be re-deposited into your Choice Privileges account after 72 hours. If you are unable to use your room and fail to cancel by the time noted on your confirmation, your Choice Privileges account will not be credited with the points that you redeemed. To cancel your reservation, you must use the cancellation link provided on your confirmation email from Preferred Hotels & Resorts. 

 

This redemption option is valid for all Choice Privileges members globally.

 

Reward Nights are based on single/double occupancy. Additional person charges may apply. Point requirements for some hotels may vary based on time of year. Choice may change the point of requirements.

Brent is working with Bendix on a new project. Bendix needs proof that our tone ring attachment on Dura-Light hubs is robust in corrosive applications. They have requested two hubs from the field. John Grant has parts they have they will send to us. There is no issue with the parts.

 

To accommodate John Grant’s labor costs, Walther has agreed to compensate them by giving them two additional hubs (total of 4). But Brent needs these parts quickly and Walther has a hard time sending parts across the border because it does not have a broker. So I have reached out to a local distributor (Can Truck) who already has parts on the shelf. Can Truck has agreed to give 4 hubs (10-00112-101) to John Grant.

 

The RMA needs to be issued to John Grant since they are returning the parts. The credit needs to be issued to Can Truck because they are giving John Grant the parts. I am not aware that we have ever included two parties on an RMA before. If you can do that, then I’d add both (or simply note that Can Truck is giving them replacement parts and will receive credit from WEMC). If you cannot, I’d only include John Grant. Does that make sense?

 

To recoup our costs, Bendix has agreed to compensate us. Brent is coordinating this.

 

Thanks,

Jason

In recent weeks, I’ve had a fair amount of issues with VPN connections to the G drive.

 

Frequent disconnectsVPN says its connected but it cannot access the G drive.Rebooting my computer often fixes the issue, but later that day I’ll have troubles again.I am not sure whether Michelle, Jason, or Keith have similar issues.Because of the spotty functionality, I generally only connect to G drive when I specifically have a need to access a file or directory or SYSPRO.  Otherwise, I disconnect it.  For me that means I’m disconnected 95% of the time but when I do need to connect, I often have to reboot to get it to work.I’m wondering if our VPN router is out of date?  Mayber there are other models which are more reliable? 

Thanks,

Brent

From: Jason Heath  
Sent: Tuesday, March 3, 2026 12:13 PM
To: Brent Armentrout 
Subject: Momentum USA - New Friction - Max Power

 

Brent,

Skip at Momentum wants to discuss the new friction Momentum has.  Over the years, I’ve discussed this with you but things have changed now and I’d like to pull you in for a call with them.

 

Momentum acquired Max Power. It is a higher quality friction made in Canada. They do not have friction to fit the current UPS pad but they offer friction for the Bosch/Quad rotors. They currently supply Girardin and Waste Management. When Girardin was in Franklin I asked them about the friction. Girardin likes it. I understand that the pad lasts longer than Momentum’s other friction line. Additionally, it performs well, not aggressive on rotors and obviously more affordable than Frasle.

 

When talking, I mentioned Dynamite friction… he suspects you mean Dynamic Friction. This is offshore friction but it has a decent reputation. 

 

I’m looking at a teams call on March 12 or 13th. Will you be out of the office then (sorry I missed when you’re surgery is)?

 

Max Power Friction | Brake Pads

 

Thanks,
Jason

I have been using Avis the last couple of years.  I’ve found that their rates are more reasonable and they have a free “skip the counter” program like most rental companies now offer.  I also like their app because after I land, I can select the specific vehicle on the app and know the parking spot.  If I don’t like the specific vehicle and color they are offering I can select something else.

 

There is a section in the individual account settings for “Company Information” where I have added the company name as shown below.  Scott, would this approach (or a similar approach at other rental agencies) be sufficient?

 

Thanks,

Brent

AAMkAGFiMThhMWE4LTBiOTYtNDY2OS05NjE3LThk.body:
CAUTION: CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe. 

Sealing Solution Extends Service Life of Swivel Joints

Mobile cranes perform a wide variety of tasks, typically of the heavy-duty kind. The work they do and the locations at which they operate are frequently exposed to harsh climatic conditions in places with insufficient infrastructure. This means that the sites at which the cranes are positioned and the environment in which they move is often not entirely suitable for this kind of heavy construction equipment.

Accordingly, there are high loads acting on the components, which often wear out prematurely as a result. A new sealing solution for swivel joints in cranes subjected to high loads, which combines a polyurethane O-ring with a nobrox® backup ring, has effectively remedied this issue.

>> Understand how sealing solution extends the service life of swivel joints

3 Types of Backup Rings and Their UsesThe Backup ring likely found its roots in the use of leather packings, where leather was used as the sealing device in glands prior to the use of O-Rings. The O-ring provided a much better seal compared to leather. However, the leather often filled the extrusion gap allowing for larger gaps.

The persistent problem that engineers faced was how to take an O-ring that operated optimally in close extrusion gaps, and extend its service with widening extrusion gaps.

>> Explore how the backup ring came to be

Our mailing address is:

Gallagher Fluid Seals, Inc.
500 Hertzog Boulevard,
King of Prussia, PA 19406
Phone: (800) 822-4063
Fax: (888) 280-4235update subscription preferences

To recoup our costs, Bendix has agreed to compensate us. Brent is coordinating this.

Jason

Supply chain pressure is widespread: 72% report capacity swings, route instability, and port/warehouse bottlenecks. See how leaders are responding. Cash flow is under strain: 65% say customer payment delays are impacting liquidity. Learn practical ways peers are reducing exposure.   As a new admin to the Microsoft 365 admin center, you’ll get weekly emails from the message center about upcoming changes to your services until April 2, 2026. Update your preferences to continue getting emails like this one. Go to preferences

Message center announcementsMarch 2, 2026 - March 8, 2026BOB'S DOCK

 (Updated) Teams admin center: Troubleshoot meetings and calls with automatic issue identification and recommendations

MC1193405 | March 2 - Teams admin center will enhance meeting troubleshooting with automatic issue identification, detailed participant telemetry, smarter search/filter options, and integration with M365 Copilot. Rollout begins late January 2026, completing by mid-March. No action needed; existing features remain, and admins can analyze and resolve meeting issues more efficiently.

 (Updated) Microsoft Teams admin center: New Teams External Collaboration Administrator role

MC1215071 | March 2 - Microsoft Teams is introducing a new RBAC role, Teams External Collaboration Administrator, for managing external collaboration settings via PowerShell. Rolling out worldwide from mid to late March 2026, it allows delegation without full Teams admin rights. Global admins can assign this role through Entra or Microsoft 365 admin centers.
View more

 (Updated) Unread News Notifications in Viva Connections

MC1138793 | March 2 - Starting mid-March 2026, Viva Connections will send weekly Microsoft Teams notifications to users about unread SharePoint news from the past week. Notifications are enabled by default but can be managed in Teams settings. No admin action is needed; rollout completes by end of March 2026.
View more

 (Updated) Noise suppression for dial-in participants in Teams audio conferences

MC1135397 | March 2 - Microsoft Teams will introduce automatic noise suppression for PSTN dial-in participants starting October 2025, improving audio clarity by reducing background noise. The feature will be enabled by default after rollout, requires no admin action but can be manually enabled earlier via policy or PowerShell.
View more

 (Updated) Microsoft 365 apps for Mac suite installer now includes the Microsoft 365 Copilot app

MC1230456 | March 2 - Starting mid-February 2026, the Microsoft 365 apps for Mac suite installer will include a shim for the Microsoft 365 Copilot app, enabling users to download the full Copilot app. Deployment completes by mid-May 2026. Organizations must ensure the shim app is included and meet macOS 14.0+ requirements.
View more
